title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Fasting adipose insulin sensitivity/resistance indices one call: Revised_QUICKI, McAuley_index, Adipo_inv, Belfiore_inv_FFA, TyG_inv, TG_HDL_C_inv, sex-specific VAI/LAP (inverted negative = worse). Inputs fasting; common glucose/insulin/TG/HDL unit conversions handled internally.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: need multiple adipose IR markers metabolic phenotyping clustering. fasting glucose, insulin, lipids, adiposity (waist/BMI); sex optional improves VAI/LAP labeling. want built-plausibility screening NA policy controls.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Required columns: G0 (mmol/L), I0 (pmol/L), TG (mmol/L), HDL_c (mmol/L), FFA (mmol/L), waist (cm), bmi (kg/m^2). Fasting values ; post-prandial inputs misstate IR. Units conversion: glucose mmol/L, insulin pmol/L, TG mmol/L, HDL mmol/L, FFA mmol/L, waist cm, BMI kg/m^2. different, convert first. Optional helpful: sex (1/2 labels) understand VAI/LAP variant applies.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Use small subset simulated data. Swap sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Map required inputs; left-hand keys fixed.
code: col_map <- list(   G0 = "G0",   I0 = "I0",   TG = "TG",   HDL_c = "HDL_c",   FFA = "FFA",   waist = "waist",   bmi = "BMI" )

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: adipo_is() returns tibble computed index columns — original data. Bind back source rows dplyr::bind_cols() needed. Values ending _inv inverted negative = worse adipose IR. sex variants (VAI_Men/Women, LAP_Men/Women) always returned; pick appropriate column per subject based biological sex:
code: ais <- adipo_is(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   normalize = "none" )  # Group 1 — non-sex-specific indices ais %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(Revised_QUICKI, McAuley_index, Adipo_inv, Belfiore_inv_FFA, TyG_inv) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   Revised_QUICKI McAuley_index Adipo_inv Belfiore_inv_FFA TyG_inv #>            <dbl>         <dbl>     <dbl>            <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1          0.342          6.50     -8.96           -0.201   -8.39 #> 2          0.354          4.97     -7.18           -0.245   -9.13 #> 3          0.352          6.12     -7.81           -0.227   -8.42 #> 4          0.360          6.62     -5.90           -0.290   -9.07 #> 5          0.340          5.52     -9.22           -0.196   -8.95  # Group 2 — sex-specific and ratio indices ais %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(TG_HDL_C_inv, VAI_Men_inv, VAI_Women_inv, LAP_Men_inv, LAP_Women_inv) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   TG_HDL_C_inv VAI_Men_inv VAI_Women_inv LAP_Men_inv LAP_Women_inv #>          <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1        -1.59      -0.689         -1.05       -11.9         -19.3 #> 2        -3.42      -2.08          -3.17       -71.3         -87.0 #> 3        -2.18      -1.28          -1.95       -40.1         -48.2 #> 4        -4.67      -2.67          -4.04       -92.4        -106.  #> 5        -3.25      -2.08          -3.16       -79.2         -92.1 # Bind sex back from source, then derive a single VAI and LAP per subject. # Adjust the if_else condition to match your sex coding (e.g. 1/2, "M"/"F"). ais_with_sex <- dplyr::bind_cols(dplyr::select(sim_small, sex), ais)  ais_with_sex %>%   dplyr::mutate(     VAI_inv = dplyr::if_else(sex == 1, VAI_Men_inv, VAI_Women_inv),     LAP_inv = dplyr::if_else(sex == 1, LAP_Men_inv, LAP_Women_inv)   ) %>%   dplyr::select(sex, Revised_QUICKI, TyG_inv, VAI_inv, LAP_inv) %>%   slice_head(n = 5)

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Screen small slice, cap extremes, compare row handling.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("G0", "I0", "TG", "HDL_c", "FFA", "waist", "BMI")] demo$G0[2] <- NA        # missing glucose demo$TG[3] <- 30        # extreme TG (mmol/L)  ais_keep <- adipo_is(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   normalize = "none",   verbose = FALSE )  ais_omit <- adipo_is(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   normalize = "none",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(ais_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(ais_omit),   capped_preview = ais_keep %>% select(ends_with("_inv")) %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 5 #>  #> $capped_preview #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   VAI_Men_inv VAI_Women_inv TG_HDL_C_inv TyG_inv LAP_Men_inv LAP_Women_inv #>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1      -0.689         -1.05        -1.59   -8.39       -11.9         -19.3 #> 2      -2.08          -3.17        -3.42   NA          -71.3         -87.0 #> 3     -22.0          -33.5        -37.4   -11.3       -688.         -828.  #> # ℹ 1 more variable: Adipo_inv <dbl>

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Override built-plausibility bounds extreme_rules (values original units conversion):
code: custom_rules <- list(   G0    = c(2, 25),    # mmol/L — tighter glucose cap   TG    = c(0.1, 15),  # mmol/L — tighter TG cap   I0    = c(5, 1500),  # pmol/L   FFA   = c(0.05, 2),  # mmol/L   waist = c(50, 200)   # cm )  ais_custom <- adipo_is(   data           = demo,   col_map        = col_map,   na_action      = "keep",   check_extreme  = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   extreme_rules  = custom_rules,   normalize      = "none" )  ais_custom %>%   select(Revised_QUICKI, TyG_inv, TG_HDL_C_inv) %>%   slice_head(n = 6) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   Revised_QUICKI TyG_inv TG_HDL_C_inv #>            <dbl>   <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1          0.342   -8.39        -1.59 #> 2         NA       NA           -3.42 #> 3          0.352  -11.0        -28.1  #> 4          0.360   -9.07        -4.67 #> 5          0.340   -8.95        -3.25 #> 6          0.321   -8.37        -1.55

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Use normalize put indices common scale modelling dimension reduction: Available options: "none" (default — raw scale), "z" (mean 0 / SD 1), "range" (0–1 min–max), "robust" (median / IQR), "inverse" (1/x).
code: ais_z <- adipo_is(   data      = sim_small,   col_map   = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   normalize = "z"      # z-score: mean 0, SD 1 )  ais_z %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(Revised_QUICKI, McAuley_index, TyG_inv, Adipo_inv) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   Revised_QUICKI McAuley_index TyG_inv Adipo_inv #>            <dbl>         <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1       -0.342           0.711  1.53     -0.285  #> 2        0.00367        -1.01  -0.583     0.191  #> 3       -0.0407          0.284  1.44      0.0219 #> 4        0.186           0.843 -0.412     0.530  #> 5       -0.389          -0.392 -0.0537   -0.354

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key resolved . Example: adipo_is(): column map: G0 -> 'G0', I0 -> 'I0', TG -> 'TG', ... Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: adipo_is(): results: Revised_QUICKI 28/30, VAI_Men_inv 28/30, McAuley_index 27/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") ("debug") active session messages print: Enable globally entire session: options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform"). Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) options(healthmarkers.verbose = "none").
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  invisible(adipo_is(   data          = sim_small[1:5, ],   col_map       = col_map,   na_action     = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   normalize     = "none",   verbose       = TRUE )) #> adipo_is(): preparing inputs #> adipo_is(): column map: G0 -> 'G0', I0 -> 'I0', TG -> 'TG', HDL_c -> 'HDL_c', FFA -> 'FFA', waist -> 'waist', bmi -> 'BMI' #> adipo_is(): results: Revised_QUICKI 5/5, VAI_Men_inv 5/5, VAI_Women_inv 5/5, TG_HDL_C_inv 5/5, TyG_inv 5/5, LAP_Men_inv 5/5, LAP_Women_inv 5/5, McAuley_index 5/5, Adipo_inv 5/5, Belfiore_inv_FFA 5/5  options(old_opt)  # restore

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: seven required mappings must provided; missing mappings columns abort. na_action controls row handling: keep propagates NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts. check_extreme screens raw inputs conversion; extreme_action can warn, cap, NA, error. Built-conversions: glucose 18 (mg/dL), insulin /6 (muU/mL), TG 88.57 (mg/dL), HDL *38.67 (mg/dL); FFA/waist/BMI unchanged.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Non-fasting samples inflate TG/glucose distort TyG/VAI/LAP. Mis-specified units (e.g., mg/dL glucose without converting) skew every index; convert upstream lab units differ. Missing sex block computation one VAI/LAP applies per subject; interpret accordingly. modelling, use normalize = "z" (z-score), "range" (0–1), "robust" (median/IQR); default "none" keeps raw index scales. See Normalise outputs section .

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Revised_QUICKI: typical adults ~0.3–0.6; lower worse IR. Computed log10(I0 [mU/L]) + log10(G0 [mg/dL]) + log10(FFA [mmol/L]). McAuley_index: typical adults ~3–10; higher better insulin sensitivity. Uses ln(TG [mmol/L]) ln(I0 [mU/L]) — TG kept mmol/L internal mg/dL conversion. TyG_inv: typically −8 −12 adults; negative = worse IR. Computed TG G0 mg/dL. LAP_Men_inv / LAP_Women_inv: computed −(WC−threshold) × TG [mmol/L] (Kahn 2005). Typical values −10 −80; negative = worse. VAI_Men_inv / VAI_Women_inv: uses TG HDL mmol/L Amato 2010 reference constants (1.03, 1.31, 0.81, 1.52). Typical range −1 −5; negative = worse. Spot-check TG_HDL_C_inv: equal −(TG_mg/dL / HDL_mg/dL). Use check_extreme = TRUE lab-appropriate extreme_rules capping erroring.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: glycemic_markers() glucose-centric indices. insulin_secretion() insulin_sensitivity() vignettes complementary beta-cell metrics.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Non-stratified SDS (z-scores) anthropometric measures using single reference (mean/sd), NA policies, optional raw extreme screening, SDS capping.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: want simple z-scores one reference (sex stratification). need NA handling optional capping implausible raw values extreme SDS magnitudes. plan standardize BMI/waist/height (similar) modeling QC.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Required: reference list (ref) mean sd > 0 per variable, matching columns data (via col_map$vars identity names). Units: data units must match reference (e.g., BMI kg/m^2, waist cm, height cm). Policies: choose na_action (keep/omit/error), check_extreme + extreme_action raw screening, sds_cap + extreme_action large SDS values. Optional: return_summary = TRUE get diagnostics, diagnostics = TRUE warnings, verbose = TRUE progress.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Use small slice packaged simulated data. Replace sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Map variable column names.
code: col_map <- list(   vars = list(     BMI = "BMI",     waist = "waist",     height = "height"   ) )

title: Adiposity SDS
text: adiposity_sds() returns tibble <var>_SDS columns — original data. Bind back dplyr::bind_cols() needed. Interpretation: <var>_SDS (x - mean)/sd; values near 0 average, positive , negative reference. extreme_action = "cap" cap SDS beyond sds_cap present.
code: ref <- list(   BMI = c(mean = mean(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE)),   waist = c(mean = mean(sim_small$waist, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(sim_small$waist, na.rm = TRUE)),   height = c(mean = mean(sim_small$height, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(sim_small$height, na.rm = TRUE)) )  asds <- adiposity_sds(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap = 6,   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(asds), names(sim_small)) asds %>% slice_head(n = 5) %>% select(all_of(new_cols)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS height_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1  0.294     -1.46      -0.523 #> 2 -0.597      0.105     -0.187 #> 3 -0.0777     0.297      1.64  #> 4  1.48       1.30       0.120 #> 5 -0.0968     0.930      0.180

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Compare NA policies raw screening.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("BMI", "waist", "height")] demo$BMI[3] <- NA        # missing demo$waist[5] <- 300     # extreme high waist demo$height[2] <- 20     # extreme low height  demo_keep <- adiposity_sds(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap = 5,   verbose = FALSE )  demo_omit <- adiposity_sds(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap = 5,   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(demo_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(demo_omit),   capped_preview = demo_keep %>% select(ends_with("_SDS")) %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $capped_preview #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS height_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1   0.294    -1.46      -0.523 #> 2  -0.597     0.105     -5     #> 3  NA         0.297      1.64

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Set return_summary = TRUE get list SDS tibble plus counts omitted rows, raw extreme adjustments, SDS extremes:
code: res <- adiposity_sds(   data           = demo,   col_map        = col_map,   ref            = ref,   na_action      = "omit",   check_extreme  = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap        = 5,   return_summary = TRUE )  res$summary #> $rows_in #> [1] 8 #>  #> $rows_out #> [1] 7 #>  #> $omitted_rows #> [1] 1 #>  #> $total_extreme #> [1] 2 #>  #> $raw_adjusted #> [1] 2 #>  #> $per_var #>   variable n_missing n_extreme #> 1      BMI         0         0 #> 2    waist         0         1 #> 3   height         0         1 head(res$data)     # SDS tibble #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS height_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1  0.294     -1.46      -0.523 #> 2 -0.597      0.105     -5     #> 3  1.48       1.30       0.120 #> 4 -0.0968     5          0.180 #> 5  1.26       0.339      1.80  #> 6  1.34      -1.30       0.518 res$warnings       # any diagnostic messages collected #> [1] "adiposity_sds(): 'BMI' missingness 12.5%"                             #> [2] "adiposity_sds(): adjusted 2 raw extreme values (cap)."                #> [3] "adiposity_sds(): capped 1 SDS beyond +/-5 for 'waist'."               #> [4] "adiposity_sds(): capped 1 SDS beyond +/-5 for 'height'."              #> [5] "adiposity_sds(): high extreme SDS proportion 9.52% (threshold 1.00%)"

Example: adiposity_sds(): results: BMI_SDS 28/30, waist_SDS 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".

title: Adiposity SDS
text: mapped variable must exist; ref must provide finite mean sd > 0 . na_action controls row handling: keep propagates NA, omit drops, error aborts. check_extreme + extreme_action operate raw values SDS; sds_cap + extreme_action govern large SDS magnitudes. Numeric coercion warns NAs introduced; align data units reference units. return_summary = TRUE returns data plus summaries omissions extremes.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Unit mismatches (e.g., inches vs cm) distort SDS; ensure units match reference. Using biased tiny sample build ref yields unstable SDS; use appropriate reference population. Forgetting map variables col_map$vars causes missing-column errors. Leaving extreme_action = "ignore" high sds_cap can hide implausible values; review extremes QC matters.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: SDS roughly center 0 SD near 1 sample matches reference. Spot-check: (value - ref_mean)/ref_sd one row equal <var>_SDS. Enable check_extreme = TRUE tailored extreme_rules flag implausible anthropometrics prior standardizing.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: adiposity_sds_strat() sex-stratified SDS. obesity_indices() obesity_metrics() related adiposity measures.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Sex-stratified SDS (z-scores) anthropometric measures using separate male/female references, NA policies, optional extreme screening, flexible variable mapping.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: need sex-specific standardization modeling flagging outliers. study men women want SDS relative sex-appropriate reference means/SDs. want built-options missing data handling plausibility screening.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Required: sex (coded M/F 1/2 mapping) least one anthropometric variable present ref$M ref$F. Reference list: ref$M ref$F must cover identical variable names, mean sd > 0. Units: use units data reference (e.g., BMI kg/m^2, waist cm, height cm). Optional: allow_partial = TRUE skip missing variables instead erroring.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Use small slice packaged simulated data. Replace sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path)  # Normalize sex labels to M/F and ensure both sexes appear in the demo slice sim_small <- sim %>%   mutate(sex = case_when(     sex %in% c("M", "F") ~ sex,     sex %in% c("male", "Male", "m") ~ "M",     sex %in% c("female", "Female", "f") ~ "F",     sex %in% c(1, "1") ~ "M",     sex %in% c(2, "2") ~ "F",     TRUE ~ NA_character_   )) %>%   filter(!is.na(sex)) %>%   slice_head(n = 200)

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: adiposity_sds_strat() returns tibble <var>_SDS columns — original data. Use dplyr::bind_cols() rejoin. Spot-check row 1 (male): BMI_SDS = (25.2 − 24.5) / 3.8 = 0.1842. Row 2 (female): BMI_SDS = (21.8 − 22.1) / 4.2 = -0.0714.
code: # Hard-coded reference values (typical population means/SDs) ref <- list(   M = list(BMI = c(mean = 24.5, sd = 3.8), waist = c(mean = 88, sd = 12)),   F = list(BMI = c(mean = 22.1, sd = 4.2), waist = c(mean = 76, sd = 11)) )  df_demo <- data.frame(   sex   = c("M", "F", "M", "F"),   BMI   = c(25.2, 21.8, 27.1, 19.5),   waist = c(85, 72, 95, 68) )  col_map <- list(sex = "sex", vars = list(BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist"))  sds_out <- adiposity_sds_strat(df_demo, col_map = col_map, ref = ref) sds_out #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  0.184     -0.25  #> 2 -0.0714    -0.364 #> 3  0.684      0.583 #> 4 -0.619     -0.727

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Map sex variable column names.
code: col_map <- list(   sex = "sex",   vars = list(     BMI = "BMI",     waist = "waist",     height = "height"   ) )

what: Walkthrough with simulated data (build sex-specific refs)
title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Interpretation: <var>_SDS standardized sex-specific mean/SD; values near 0 average sex, positive , negative .
code: # Build sex-specific references from the demo slice ref_sex <- sim_small |>   group_by(sex) |>   summarise(     BMI = list(c(mean = mean(BMI, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(BMI, na.rm = TRUE))),     waist = list(c(mean = mean(waist, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(waist, na.rm = TRUE))),     height = list(c(mean = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(height, na.rm = TRUE)))   )  if (!all(c("M", "F") %in% ref_sex$sex)) {   stop("Demo data slice must contain both M and F; normalize or increase sample.") }  ref <- list(   M = list(     BMI = ref_sex$BMI[[ref_sex$sex == "M"]],     waist = ref_sex$waist[[ref_sex$sex == "M"]],     height = ref_sex$height[[ref_sex$sex == "M"]]   ),   F = list(     BMI = ref_sex$BMI[[ref_sex$sex == "F"]],     waist = ref_sex$waist[[ref_sex$sex == "F"]],     height = ref_sex$height[[ref_sex$sex == "F"]]   ) )  asds_strat <- adiposity_sds_strat(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = "cap",   allow_partial = FALSE,   prefix = "",   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(asds_strat), names(sim_small)) asds_strat %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(all_of(new_cols))

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Compare row handling capping.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("sex", "BMI", "waist", "height")] demo$BMI[3] <- NA         # missing demo$waist[5] <- 300      # extreme high waist demo$height[2] <- 20      # extreme low height  demo_keep <- adiposity_sds_strat(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   allow_partial = FALSE,   prefix = "" )  demo_omit <- adiposity_sds_strat(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   allow_partial = FALSE,   prefix = "" )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(demo_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(demo_omit),   capped_preview = demo_keep %>% select(ends_with("_SDS")) %>% slice_head(n = 3) )

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Use prefix namespace output columns combining multiple calls data frame:
code: # Same data as quick-start but prefix with "z_" sds_prefixed <- adiposity_sds_strat(   df_demo, col_map = col_map, ref = ref, prefix = "z_" ) names(sds_prefixed)  # "z_BMI_SDS" "z_waist_SDS" #> [1] "z_BMI_SDS"   "z_waist_SDS"

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)

Numeric coercion happens warnings NAs introduced; align data units reference units.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)

Unit mismatches (e.g., inches vs cm, lb vs kg) distort SDS; keep units consistent reference. Forgetting allow_partial = TRUE variables absent error; enable compute ’s available.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Compute composite Allostatic Load (AL) score: biomarker exceeding threshold contributes 1 count. multiple biomarkers rule >; single biomarker >=. Returns tibble single integer column AllostaticLoad. Optional extreme screening applies columns whose names contain “sds”.

title: Allostatic Load
text: want simple count biomarkers exceeding risk cut-points. small set biomarkers clear thresholds control. may include pre-computed SDS columns optionally cap implausible SDS values.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Thresholds: named list scalar numeric cutoffs; names must match columns (mapped via col_map). Data: matching biomarker columns; units align thresholds supply. Policies: na_action (keep treats missing unflagged; omit drops rows; error aborts). check_extreme/sds_limit/extreme_action affect columns whose names contain “sds”. Optional: return_summary = TRUE counts flags/omissions; verbose progress.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Use small subset simulated data. Replace sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Allostatic Load
text: Provide thresholds , needed, map column names.
code: thresholds <- list(sbp = 130, dbp = 85, CRP = 3) col_map <- list(sbp = "sbp", dbp = "dbp", CRP = "CRP")

title: Allostatic Load
text: Interpretation: AllostaticLoad count biomarkers thresholds. >1 biomarker rule >; single biomarker >=.
code: al <- allostatic_load(   data = sim_small,   thresholds = thresholds,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  al %>% slice_head(n = 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              2 #> 2              3 #> 3              3 #> 4              1 #> 5              1

title: Allostatic Load
text: Demonstrate row handling SDS screening (affects columns containing “sds”).
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("sbp", "dbp", "CRP")] demo$sbp[3] <- NA  demo$CRP_sds <- c(0, 7, -8, 1, NA, 0, 0, 0)  # example SDS column to show screening  al_keep <- allostatic_load(   data = demo,   thresholds = thresholds,   col_map = c(col_map, CRP_sds = "CRP_sds"),   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_limit = 6,   verbose = FALSE )  al_omit <- allostatic_load(   data = demo,   thresholds = thresholds,   col_map = c(col_map, CRP_sds = "CRP_sds"),   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_limit = 6,   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(al_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(al_omit),   head_keep = al_keep %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $head_keep #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              2 #> 2              3 #> 3              2

title: Allostatic Load
text: return_summary = TRUE returns list(data, summary, warnings) instead bare tibble. summary element contains rows, biomarkers, total_flags, mean_flags.
code: df_rs <- data.frame(   SBP = c(118, 142, 130, 155, 120),   DBP = c(76,   92,  85,  98,  78),   CRP = c(1.2,  4.8, 2.1, 5.0, 0.9) ) thr_rs <- list(SBP = 130, DBP = 85, CRP = 3)  res <- allostatic_load(   data      = df_rs,   thresholds = thr_rs,   na_action  = "keep",   return_summary = TRUE,   verbose    = FALSE ) res$summary #> $rows #> [1] 5 #>  #> $biomarkers #> [1] 3 #>  #> $total_flags #> [1] 6 #>  #> $mean_flags #> [1] 1.2 res$data #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              0 #> 2              3 #> 3              0 #> 4              3 #> 5              0

title: Allostatic Load
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column threshold key resolved . Example: allostatic_load(): column map: SBP -> 'SBP', DBP -> 'DBP', ... Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: allostatic_load(): results: AllostaticLoad 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- data.frame(SBP = c(118, 142), DBP = c(76, 92)) allostatic_load(df_v, thresholds = list(SBP = 130, DBP = 85), verbose = TRUE) #> allostatic_load(): preparing inputs #> allostatic_load(): column map: SBP -> 'SBP', DBP -> 'DBP' #> allostatic_load(): results: AllostaticLoad 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              0 #> 2              2  options(old_opt)

Multi-biomarker rule >; single-biomarker rule >=.

title: ALM/BMI Index

Policies: na_action (keep/warn/ignore behave like keep; omit drops; error aborts). check_extreme + extreme_action can cap/NA/error implausible ALM/BMI. FNIH cut-points built ; nothing configure beyond column mapping.

title: ALM/BMI Index

code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  # Example proxy for ALM (replace with measured ALM when available) sim_small <- sim_small %>%   mutate(ALM_kg = pmax(10, pmin(35, weight * 0.35)))

code: alm_out <- alm_bmi_index(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  alm_out %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(alm_bmi_ratio, low_muscle_mass) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   alm_bmi_ratio low_muscle_mass #>           <dbl> <lgl>           #> 1         0.929 FALSE           #> 2         0.964 FALSE           #> 3         1.16  FALSE           #> 4         0.941 FALSE           #> 5         1.00  FALSE

title: ALM/BMI Index

code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("ALM_kg", "BMI", "sex")] demo$ALM_kg[3] <- NA demo$BMI[4] <- 2    # implausible, will be capped  alm_keep <- alm_bmi_index(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   extreme_rules = list(alm = c(5, 40), bmi = c(10, 60)),   verbose = FALSE )  alm_omit <- alm_bmi_index(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   extreme_rules = list(alm = c(5, 40), bmi = c(10, 60)),   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(alm_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(alm_omit),   preview = alm_keep %>% select(alm_bmi_ratio, low_muscle_mass) %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   alm_bmi_ratio low_muscle_mass #>           <dbl> <lgl>           #> 1         0.929 FALSE           #> 2         0.964 FALSE           #> 3        NA     NA

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Compute atherogenic risk ratios lipid panel: - AIP = log10(TG / HDL_c) - Castelli Risk Index (CRI_I) = TC / HDL_c - Castelli Risk Index II (CRI_II) = LDL_c / HDL_c ratios unitless; use consistent lipid units (mg/dL mmol/L).

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: triglycerides HDL cholesterol want AIP. also total /LDL cholesterol want Castelli indices. need optional extreme-value screening strict missing-data handling.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Data frame lipid columns; required keys: TG HDL_c. Optional: TC LDL_c. Numeric lipids; non-numeric coerced warnings; non-finite become NA. Decide row policy: na_action = keep (default), omit, error. Optional extreme screening: enable check_extreme choose extreme_action.

title: Atherogenic Indices

code: aip_out <- atherogenic_indices(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = "warn",   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(aip_out), names(sim_small)) head(select(aip_out, all_of(new_cols))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>       AIP CRI_I CRI_II #>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 -0.159   2.83   1.94 #> 2  0.175   3.95   1.74 #> 3 -0.0214  3.70   1.92 #> 4  0.310   4.92   4.12 #> 5  0.152   4.09   2.48 #> 6 -0.169   3.97   2.46

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: col_map: named list TG, HDL_c (required), optional TC, LDL_c. Missing required keys error. na_action: keep (default, rows retained; ratios become NA), omit (drop rows NA used lipids), error (abort NA). check_extreme: FALSE default; TRUE, screen lipids using extreme_rules. extreme_action: warn (default, change), cap, error, ignore, NA. Defaults use wide mg/dL ranges: TG 0-10000, HDL_c 0-1000, LDL_c 0-10000, TC 0-10000. verbose: emit step messages.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Non-numeric lipids coerced numeric; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. Zero HDL_c denominators yield NA ratios warning; Castelli indices use HDL_c divisor. Row policy: keep vs omit vs error via na_action.

title: Atherogenic Indices
code: demo <- sim_small demo$HDL_c[c(2, 5)] <- NA  aip_keep <- atherogenic_indices(demo, col_map, na_action = "keep") aip_omit <- atherogenic_indices(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit")  list(   keep_rows = nrow(aip_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(aip_omit),   sample_ratios = head(select(aip_keep, AIP, CRI_I, CRI_II)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 30 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 28 #>  #> $sample_ratios #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>       AIP CRI_I CRI_II #>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 -0.159   2.83   1.94 #> 2 NA      NA     NA    #> 3 -0.0214  3.70   1.92 #> 4  0.310   4.92   4.12 #> 5 NA      NA     NA    #> 6 -0.169   3.97   2.46

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: AIP: log10(TG / HDL_c) CRI_I: TC / HDL_c (NA TC mapped/present) CRI_II: LDL_c / HDL_c (NA LDL_c mapped/present) Rows drop na_action = “omit” na_action = “error” encounters missing.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Keep lipid units consistent; ratios unitless inputs must share units. Mapping omissions TG HDL_c throw errors; missing TC/LDL_c just yield NA Castelli indices. Zero HDL_c produces NA ratios warning; address true zeros computing. Tighten extreme_rules lab ranges narrower; use extreme_action = “error” strict QA. Use na_action = “error” pipelines must fail fast missing lipids.

title: BODE Index
text: Compute 0-10 BODE score (BMI, Obstruction, Dyspnea, Exercise capacity) FEV1 % predicted, 6-minute walk distance, mMRC dyspnea, BMI. Accepts percent-predicted column derives raw/predicted FEV1. Higher scores indicate worse prognosis. Component scoring: FEV1% (>=65 -> 0, 50-64 -> 1, 36-49 -> 2, <=35 -> 3); 6MWD meters (>=350 -> 0, 250-349 -> 1, 150-249 -> 2, <=149 -> 3); mMRC (0-1 -> 0, 2 -> 1, 3 -> 2, 4 -> 3); BMI (>21 -> 0, <=21 -> 1).

title: BODE Index
text: spirometry plus 6MWD, mMRC, BMI COPD risk stratification. need optional extreme-value screening explicit row-retention rules. want derive FEV1% raw/predicted FEV1 use existing percent-predicted column.

title: BODE Index

Numeric inputs; non-numeric coerced warnings; non-finite become NA. Row policy via na_action: keep (default), omit, error; warn/ignore behave like keep emit messages. Optional extreme screening: check_extreme + extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore/NA).

text: bode_index (0-10) Component scores: fev1_score, walk_score, mmrc_score, bmi_score fev1_pct (derived mapped) Rows drop na_action = “omit” na_action = “error” aborts.

title: BODE Index
text: Map exactly one FEV1 source; providing multiple percent-predicted sources allowed. Keep units consistent: FEV1 liters deriving percent, 6MWD meters, BMI kg/m^2. Use extreme_action = “error” strict QA; cap winsorization. mMRC must 0-4; true zeros valid. warn/ignore behave like keep; choose omit error strict pipelines.

title: BODE Index
text: Spot-check: FEV1% = 40, 6MWD = 200, mMRC = 3, BMI = 20 yields scores 2 + 2 + 2 + 1 = bode_index 7. Verify rows NA mmrc retained vs dropped according na_action. Confirm percent-predicted derivation: fev1_pp match 100 * fev1 / fev1_pred test row.

title: BODE Index
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key resolved . Example: bode_index(): column map: fev1_pct -> 'FEV1pct', sixmwd -> 'Walk_m', mmrc -> 'mMRC', bmi -> 'BMI' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: bode_index(): results: bode_index 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- data.frame(   FEV1pct = c(68, 45, 30),   Walk_m  = c(400, 280, 140),   mMRC    = c(1, 2, 3),   BMI     = c(24, 19, 18) ) bode_index(   df_v,   col_map = list(fev1_pct = "FEV1pct", sixmwd = "Walk_m", mmrc = "mMRC", bmi = "BMI"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> bode_index(): preparing inputs #> bode_index(): column map: fev1_pct -> 'FEV1pct', sixmwd -> 'Walk_m', mmrc -> 'mMRC', bmi -> 'BMI' #> bode_index(): results: bode_index 3/3, fev1_pct 3/3, fev1_score 3/3, walk_score 3/3, mmrc_score 3/3, bmi_score 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   bode_index fev1_pct fev1_score walk_score mmrc_score bmi_score #>        <int>    <dbl>      <int>      <int>      <int>     <int> #> 1          0       68          0          0          0         0 #> 2          5       45          2          1          1         1 #> 3          9       30          3          3          2         1  options(old_opt)

title: BODE Index
text: spirometry_markers() broader spirometry-derived metrics. pulmo_markers() additional pulmonary markers.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Compute OSTA, ALMI, FMI, BMD_Tscore DXA anthropometry, optionally pass bone-turnover markers (TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin). Inputs coerced numeric; non-finite become NA. BMD_Tscore uses supplied reference mean SD; SD must positive.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: DXA outputs (ALM, FM, BMD) plus age, weight, height, reference BMD stats. want OSTA mass indexes (ALMI/FMI) alongside BMD T-scores. may additional bone markers pass unchanged.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Required columns: age, weight, height (meters), ALM, FM (kg), BMD (g/cm^2), BMD_ref_mean, BMD_ref_sd (positive). Optional pass-: TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin (coerced numeric mapped present). Row policy via na_action: keep (default), omit, error missing/non-finite required inputs. Optional SDS screening: check_extreme mapped key containing “sds”; control sds_limit extreme_action.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: simulated data lack DXA values; create simple placeholders illustration ; replace measurements.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  # Illustrative stand-ins; replace with real DXA outputs sim_small$ALM <- pmax(12, pmin(30, sim_small$weight * 0.32)) sim_small$FM  <- pmax(8,  pmin(40, sim_small$weight * 0.28)) sim_small$BMD <- 0.9 + (sim_small$age - mean(sim_small$age, na.rm = TRUE)) * 0.001 sim_small$BMD_ref_mean <- 1.0 sim_small$BMD_ref_sd   <- 0.12

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Required keys plus optional markers (commented ).
code: col_map <- list(   age = "age",   weight = "weight",   height = "height",   ALM = "ALM",   FM = "FM",   BMD = "BMD",   BMD_ref_mean = "BMD_ref_mean",   BMD_ref_sd   = "BMD_ref_sd"   # Optional: TBS = "TBS", HSA = "HSA", PINP = "PINP", CTX = "CTX", BSAP = "BSAP", Osteocalcin = "Osteocalcin" )

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Defaults keep rows missing inputs return NA derived scores.
code: bm_out <- bone_markers(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   sds_limit = 6,   extreme_action = "cap" )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(bm_out), names(sim_small)) head(select(bm_out, all_of(new_cols))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>    OSTA     ALMI      FMI BMD_Tscore   HSA #>   <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  1.73 0.000986 0.000862     -0.696    NA #> 2  2.83 0.000832 0.000728     -0.827    NA #> 3  8.59 0.000905 0.000806     -0.868    NA #> 4 11.9  0.00105  0.00104      -0.921    NA #> 5  8.42 0.000918 0.000803     -0.972    NA #> 6 13.7  0.000892 0.00101      -0.867    NA

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: col_map: required mappings age, weight, height, ALM, FM, BMD, BMD_ref_mean, BMD_ref_sd; optional TBS/HSA/PINP/CTX/BSAP/Osteocalcin pass mapped. na_action: keep (default, retain rows; derived scores become NA inputs missing), omit (drop rows missing/non-finite required inputs), error (abort missing/non-finite required inputs). check_extreme: FALSE default; TRUE scans mapped keys containing “sds” (case-insensitive) |value| > sds_limit. extreme_action (check_extreme = TRUE): cap, NA, error. sds_limit: threshold SDS-like screening (default 6).

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Non-numeric inputs coerced; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. BMD_ref_sd height must positive function aborts. Row policy: keep vs omit vs error governed na_action.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("age","weight","height","ALM","FM","BMD","BMD_ref_mean","BMD_ref_sd")] demo$ALM[c(2, 5)] <- NA  a_keep <- bone_markers(demo, col_map, na_action = "keep") a_omit <- bone_markers(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit")  list(   keep_rows = nrow(a_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(a_omit),   preview = head(select(a_keep, OSTA, ALMI, FMI, BMD_Tscore)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>    OSTA      ALMI      FMI BMD_Tscore #>   <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1  1.73  0.000986 0.000862     -0.696 #> 2  2.83 NA        0.000728     -0.827 #> 3  8.59  0.000905 0.000806     -0.868 #> 4 11.9   0.00105  0.00104      -0.921 #> 5  8.42 NA        0.000803     -0.972 #> 6 13.7   0.000892 0.00101      -0.867

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: supply mapped key containing “sds”, can cap/NA/error extremes.
code: demo2 <- demo # Example SDS-like column for illustration set.seed(1) demo2$BMD_z_sds <- rnorm(nrow(demo2), mean = 0, sd = 7) col_map_sds <- c(col_map, list(BMD_z_sds = "BMD_z_sds"))  bm_screen <- bone_markers(   data = demo2,   col_map = col_map_sds,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   sds_limit = 6,   extreme_action = "cap" )  summary(abs(demo2$BMD_z_sds) > 6) #>    Mode   FALSE    TRUE  #> logical       7       1

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Ensure BMD_ref_sd > 0 height > 0; otherwise function aborts row filtering. Replace placeholder ALM/FM/BMD values actual DXA measurements; keep units consistent (height meters; masses kg; BMD g/cm^2). Optional markers numeric-coerced; missing mapped, return NA columns. SDS screening applies mapped keys containing “sds”; defaults nothing unless provide keys.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Manual check: OSTA age 70 weight 60 (60 - 70) * 0.2 = -2. ALMI equal ALM / height^2; FMI equals FM / height^2. BMD_Tscore drop ~1 BMD one SD reference.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key resolved . Example: bone_markers(): column map: age -> 'age', weight -> 'weight', height -> 'height', ... Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: bone_markers(): results: T_score 28/30, Z_score 28/30, PINP 29/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- tibble::tibble(   age = c(60, 72), weight = c(65, 50), height = c(1.65, 1.58),   ALM = c(18.2, 14.7), FM = c(22.0, 20.5),   BMD = c(0.95, 0.80), BMD_ref_mean = c(1.00, 1.00), BMD_ref_sd = c(0.12, 0.12) ) cm_v <- list(   age = "age", weight = "weight", height = "height",   ALM = "ALM", FM = "FM", BMD = "BMD",   BMD_ref_mean = "BMD_ref_mean", BMD_ref_sd = "BMD_ref_sd" ) bone_markers(df_v, col_map = cm_v, verbose = TRUE) #> bone_markers(): column map: age -> 'age', weight -> 'weight', height -> 'height', ALM -> 'ALM', FM -> 'FM', BMD -> 'BMD', BMD_ref_mean -> 'BMD_ref_mean', BMD_ref_sd -> 'BMD_ref_sd' #> bone_markers(): completed #> bone_markers(): results: OSTA 2/2, ALMI 2/2, FMI 2/2, BMD_Tscore 2/2, TBS 0/2, HSA 0/2, PINP 0/2, CTX 0/2, BSAP 0/2, Osteocalcin 0/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>    OSTA  ALMI   FMI BMD_Tscore   TBS   HSA  PINP   CTX  BSAP Osteocalcin #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1   1    6.69  8.08     -0.417    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA          NA #> 2  -4.4  5.89  8.21     -1.67     NA    NA    NA    NA    NA          NA  options(old_opt)

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: sarc_f_score() alm_bmi_index() sarcopenia-related assessments. frax_score() fracture risk estimation.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Standardize selected variables SDS (z-scores) using supplied reference means SDs. Supports row-retention policies, extreme SDS handling, optional diagnostic list output.

title: Calculate SDS
text: need z-scores one variables using chosen reference. want explicit control missing-row handling extreme SDS treatment. may need diagnostic list summarizing missing extreme counts.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Data columns: variables listed vars must exist numeric/coercible; id_col optional. Reference table ref: columns variable, mean, sd; one finite mean positive sd per var. Row policy: na_strategy = keep (default), omit, error. Extreme policy: check_extreme + extreme_strategy (cap/warn/error/NA) sds_cap threshold.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Build small example reference BMI systolic BP; replace variables reference stats.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30) %>%   dplyr::select(id, BMI, sbp)  vars <- c("BMI", "sbp") ref <- tibble::tibble(   variable = vars,   mean = c(mean(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE), mean(sim_small$sbp, na.rm = TRUE)),   sd   = c(sd(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE),   sd(sim_small$sbp, na.rm = TRUE)) )

title: Calculate SDS
text: Defaults keep rows missing inputs return NA SDS cells.
code: sds_tbl <- calc_sds(   data = sim_small,   vars = vars,   ref = ref,   id_col = "id",   na_strategy = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_strategy = "cap",   sds_cap = 6,   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(sds_tbl), names(sim_small)) head(select(sds_tbl, id, all_of(new_cols))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>      id BMI_sds sbp_sds #>   <int>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1     1  0.294   -0.565 #> 2     2 -0.597    1.49  #> 3     3 -0.0777   0.625 #> 4     4  1.48    -1.10  #> 5     5 -0.0968  -0.427 #> 6     6  1.26    -0.642

title: Calculate SDS
text: vars: character vector columns standardize; must present unique. ref: table variable/mean/sd; one row per var; sd must positive. na_strategy: keep (retain rows, NA SDS), omit (drop rows missing vars), error (abort missing vars). check_extreme + extreme_strategy: cap/warn/error/NA |SDS| > sds_cap; cap applied symmetrically. return: data (default tibble) list (adds summary warning strings). id_col: optional ID diagnostics .

title: Calculate SDS
text: Non-numeric vars coerced numeric warnings; non-finite become NA. Bypass vs drop vs fail controlled na_strategy.

title: Calculate SDS
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, ] demo$BMI[3] <- NA  a_keep <- calc_sds(demo, vars, ref, na_strategy = "keep") a_omit <- calc_sds(demo, vars, ref, na_strategy = "omit")  list(   keep_rows = nrow(a_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(a_omit),   preview = head(select(a_keep, BMI_sds, sbp_sds)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   BMI_sds sbp_sds #>     <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  0.294   -0.565 #> 2 -0.597    1.49  #> 3 NA        0.625 #> 4  1.48    -1.10  #> 5 -0.0968  -0.427 #> 6  1.26    -0.642

title: Calculate SDS
text: Cap, warn, error, set NA SDS beyond chosen limit.
code: demo2 <- demo demo2$sbp[5] <- 500  # extreme SBP  a_cap <- calc_sds(   data = demo2,   vars = vars,   ref = ref,   na_strategy = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_strategy = "cap",   sds_cap = 4 )  head(select(a_cap, BMI_sds, sbp_sds)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   BMI_sds sbp_sds #>     <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  0.294   -0.565 #> 2 -0.597    1.49  #> 3 NA        0.625 #> 4  1.48    -1.10  #> 5 -0.0968   4     #> 6  1.26    -0.642

title: Calculate SDS
text: Request per-variable missing/extreme counts warnings.
code: sds_list <- calc_sds(   data = sim_small,   vars = vars,   ref = ref,   na_strategy = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_strategy = "warn",   sds_cap = 5,   return = "list",   verbose = FALSE )  str(sds_list$summary) #> List of 5 #>  $ rows_in      : int 30 #>  $ rows_out     : int 30 #>  $ omitted_rows : int 0 #>  $ total_extreme: int 0 #>  $ per_var      :'data.frame':   2 obs. of  3 variables: #>   ..$ variable : chr [1:2] "BMI" "sbp" #>   ..$ n_missing: int [1:2] 0 0 #>   ..$ n_extreme: int [1:2] 0 0 unique(sds_list$warnings) #> character(0)

title: Calculate SDS
text: Added _sds columns (tibble default) Optional list: data, summary (row counts, omitted rows, extremes, per-variable missing/extreme), warnings Rows drop na_strategy = “omit” na_strategy = “error” aborts.

title: Calculate SDS
text: ref must align vars use units; sd must positive finite. Lower sds_cap limit leverage outliers plots; use extreme_strategy = “error” strict QC. Provide id_col make troubleshooting easier reviewing diagnostics.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Manual check: mean BMI 25 sd 4, BMI 29 yield (29-25)/4 = 1.0. Confirm rows missing BMI retained vs dropped according na_strategy. Verify capping clamps |SDS| sds_cap symmetrically.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — lists variable passed via vars. Example: calc_sds(): column map: BMI -> 'BMI', sbp -> 'sbp' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per _sds output column. Example: calc_sds(): results: BMI_sds 28/30, sbp_sds 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  ref_v <- data.frame(variable = c("BMI", "sbp"), mean = c(25, 120), sd = c(4, 15)) df_v  <- data.frame(id = 1:3, BMI = c(24, 29, 30), sbp = c(118, 121, 135)) calc_sds(   data = df_v,   vars = c("BMI", "sbp"),   ref  = ref_v,   id_col = "id",   na_strategy = "keep",   extreme_strategy = "cap",   sds_cap = 6,   verbose = TRUE ) #> calc_sds: starting on 3 row(s), 2 variable(s) (id: id) #> calc_sds(): column map: BMI -> 'BMI', sbp -> 'sbp' #> Computing SDS for 2 variable(s)... #> calc_sds: completed #> calc_sds(): results: BMI_sds 3/3, sbp_sds 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>      id   BMI   sbp BMI_sds sbp_sds #>   <int> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1     1    24   118   -0.25 -0.133  #> 2     2    29   121    1     0.0667 #> 3     3    30   135    1.25  1  options(old_opt)

title: Calculate SDS
text: infer_cols() infer data types prior scoring. calc_sds() used helpers; pair health_summary() broader panels.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Compute 19-condition Charlson Comorbidity Index (CCI) without age points. Handles binary indicators, optional bounds screening, NA policies.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: binary comorbidity indicators need canonical Charlson score (age points ). want explicit row-retention rules missing indicators optional bounds checks 0/1 flags. may need cap error --bounds inputs.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Data columns: binary 0/1 indicators 19 conditions. Column map: provide 19 keys -> columns; missing mappings abort. Row policy: na_action = keep (default), omit, error; warn/ignore act like keep warn. Optional bounds screening: check_extreme + extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore/NA); defaults use 0-1.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Illustrative-flags ; replace real comorbidity indicators.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  # Illustrative binary flags set.seed(123) sim_small <- sim_small %>% mutate(   mi = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   chf = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   pvd = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   stroke = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   dementia = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   copd = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.15),   rheum = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   ulcer = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   mild_liver = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   diabetes = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.2),   diab_comp = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   hemiplegia = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.02),   renal = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.08),   cancer = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.08),   leukemia = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01),   lymphoma = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01),   sev_liver = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.02),   metastatic_cancer = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01),   hiv = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01) )

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Provide 19 keys. Missing mappings error.
code: col_map <- list(   mi="mi", chf="chf", pvd="pvd", stroke="stroke", dementia="dementia", copd="copd",   rheum="rheum", ulcer="ulcer", mild_liver="mild_liver", diabetes="diabetes",   diab_comp="diab_comp", hemiplegia="hemiplegia", renal="renal", cancer="cancer",   leukemia="leukemia", lymphoma="lymphoma", sev_liver="sev_liver",   metastatic_cancer="metastatic_cancer", hiv="hiv" )

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Defaults keep rows missing indicators return NA scores .
code: cci_out <- charlson_index(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = "warn",   verbose = FALSE )  head(cci_out) #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>   charlson_index #>            <int> #> 1              4 #> 2              1 #> 3              0 #> 4              0 #> 5              1 #> 6              1

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: col_map: 19 keys required; missing empty entries abort. na_action: keep (default), omit (drop rows missing indicators), error (abort missing); warn/ignore behave like keep warn. check_extreme: FALSE default; TRUE enforces bounds. extreme_action: warn (default), cap, error, ignore, NA; default bounds 0-1 per indicator unless overridden via extreme_rules. verbose: emit step messages.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Non-numeric inputs coerced; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. Missing indicators yield NA scores unless na_action = “omit” “error”. --range (non 0/1) values warn; enable check_extreme cap/error/NA.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, names(col_map)] demo$renal[3] <- NA  a_keep <- charlson_index(demo, col_map, na_action = "keep", check_extreme = FALSE) a_omit <- charlson_index(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit", check_extreme = FALSE)  list(   keep_rows = nrow(a_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(a_omit),   preview = head(a_keep$charlson_index) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $preview #> [1]  4  1 NA  0  1  1

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Cap error inputs outside chosen ranges.
code: demo2 <- demo demo2$mi[5] <- 2  # out of bounds  a_cap <- charlson_index(   data = demo2,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )  head(a_cap$charlson_index) #> [1]  4  1 NA  0  1  1

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: charlson_index integer total Rows drop na_action = “omit”; na_action = “error” aborts missing. Optional bounds handling via check_extreme/extreme_action.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Indicators must truly binary; non-binary values warn can capped screening . Provide 19 mappings; missing keys error. Age points included ; add separately needed. Use extreme_rules adjust bounds inputs differ.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Set indicators 0: expected score 0. Single metastatic_cancer = 1, else 0: expected score 6. diabetes = 1 diab_comp = 1: expected diabetes contribution = 2 (max rule).

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column comorbidity key resolved . Example: charlson_index(): column map: mi -> 'mi', chf -> 'chf', ... Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: charlson_index(): results: charlson_index 30/30, charlson_index_age 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  patient <- tibble::tibble(   mi=0, chf=0, pvd=0, stroke=0, dementia=0, copd=0, rheum=0, ulcer=0,   mild_liver=0, diabetes=1, diab_comp=1, hemiplegia=0, renal=1,   cancer=0, leukemia=0, lymphoma=0, sev_liver=0, metastatic_cancer=0, hiv=0 ) cm_v <- as.list(stats::setNames(names(patient), names(patient))) charlson_index(patient, col_map = cm_v, verbose = TRUE) #> charlson_index(): preparing inputs #> charlson_index(): column map: mi -> 'mi', chf -> 'chf', pvd -> 'pvd', stroke -> 'stroke', dementia -> 'dementia', copd -> 'copd', rheum -> 'rheum', ulcer -> 'ulcer', mild_liver -> 'mild_liver', diabetes -> 'diabetes', diab_comp -> 'diab_comp', hemiplegia -> 'hemiplegia', renal -> 'renal', cancer -> 'cancer', leukemia -> 'leukemia', lymphoma -> 'lymphoma', sev_liver -> 'sev_liver', metastatic_cancer -> 'metastatic_cancer', hiv -> 'hiv' #> charlson_index(): results: charlson_index 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   charlson_index #>            <int> #> 1              4  options(old_opt)

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Columns: eGFR required; UACR optional. Units: eGFR mL/min/1.73 m^2; UACR mg/g. Column map: list(eGFR = …, UACR = …) UACR optional; mapping missing columns errors (except optional UACR). Row policy: na_action = keep (default), omit, error. Extreme screening: check_extreme + extreme_action (cap/NA/error); defaults cap eGFR 0-200, UACR 0-5000 enabled.

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage
text: Example uses 50-row slice simulated data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30) %>%   dplyr::select(eGFR, UACR)

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage
text: UACR optional; omit col_map unavailable.

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage
text: Defaults keep rows missing inputs return NA stages .
code: ckd_out <- ckd_stage(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = "cap" )  head(ckd_out) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   CKD_stage Albuminuria_stage KDIGO_risk #>   <fct>     <fct>             <fct>      #> 1 G1        A2                Moderate   #> 2 G2        A1                Low        #> 3 G2        A2                Moderate   #> 4 G3a       A1                Moderate   #> 5 G1        A1                Low        #> 6 G2        A2                Moderate

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage
text: col_map: eGFR required; UACR optional. UACR mapped missing data, albuminuria NA warning issued. na_action: keep (default, retain rows; stages become NA), omit (drop rows missing inputs), error (abort missing). check_extreme: FALSE default; TRUE enables bounds checks (eGFR 0-200; UACR 0-5000 mg/g default). extreme_action: cap (default), NA, error check_extreme = TRUE.

title: Chronic Kidney Disease Stage
text: Non-numeric inputs coerced; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. Missing eGFR yields NA stages unless na_action = omit/error. Missing UACR yields NA albuminuria; KDIGO risk treats missing albuminuria A1 mapping.

Example: ckd_stage(): column map: eGFR -> 'eGFR', UACR -> 'UACR' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: ckd_stage(): results: ckd_g_stage 30/30, ckd_a_stage 28/30, ckd_stage 28/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Apply Payne formula albumin-adjust serum calcium. Works conventional (mg/dL Ca, g/dL Alb) SI (mmol/L Ca, g/L Alb); can auto-detect units; supports optional extreme screening.

title: Corrected Calcium
text: total serum calcium albumin need albumin-corrected calcium. labs may mg/dL+g/dL mmol/L+g/L, want unit-aware handling. need row-retention rules missing inputs optional bounds checks.

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Columns: calcium albumin; units must consistent chosen units argument. Column map: list(calcium = …, albumin = …). Defaults assume Ca/Alb. Units: conventional (mg/dL, g/dL), si (mmol/L, g/L), auto (infers; warns assuming SI). Row policy: na_action = keep (default), omit, error; warn/ignore behave like keep warn. Optional screening: check_extreme + extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore/NA); defaults use Ca 4-15 mg/dL Alb 2-5 g/dL working units enabled.

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Simulated data lack Ca/Alb; generate illustrative valuesreplace real labs.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  set.seed(123) # Illustrative labs (mg/dL Ca, g/dL Alb) sim_small$Ca <- pmax(7.5, pmin(11.5, rnorm(nrow(sim_small), 9.2, 0.6))) sim_small$Alb <- pmax(2.5, pmin(5.0, rnorm(nrow(sim_small), 4.0, 0.4)))

title: Corrected Calcium
code: col_map <- list(calcium = "Ca", albumin = "Alb")

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Defaults keep rows missing inputs screen extremes.
code: cc_out <- corrected_calcium(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   units = "conventional",  # use "si" for mmol/L; "auto" to infer   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = "warn" )  head(cc_out) #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>   corrected_calcium #>               <dbl> #> 1              8.73 #> 2              9.16 #> 3              9.85 #> 4              8.96 #> 5              9.01 #> 6             10.0

title: Corrected Calcium
text: col_map: calcium albumin mappings required; empty missing mappings abort. units: conventional | si | auto. Auto infers based medians warns assuming SI. na_action: keep (rows retained, NA outputs missing), omit (drop rows missing inputs), error (abort missing). check_extreme: FALSE default; TRUE applies bounds working units (mg/dL, g/dL) correction. extreme_action: warn (default, change), cap, error, ignore, NA; override bounds via extreme_rules.

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Non-numeric Ca/Alb coerced; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. Missing inputs yield NA outputs unless na_action = omit/error.

code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("Ca", "Alb")] demo$Ca[3] <- NA  a_keep <- corrected_calcium(demo, col_map, units = "conventional", na_action = "keep") a_omit <- corrected_calcium(demo, col_map, units = "conventional", na_action = "omit")  list(   keep_rows = nrow(a_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(a_omit),   preview = head(a_keep) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>   corrected_calcium #>               <dbl> #> 1              8.73 #> 2              9.16 #> 3             NA    #> 4              8.96 #> 5              9.01 #> 6             10.0

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Cap error implausible inputs correction.
code: demo2 <- demo demo2$Ca[5] <- 2.0  # implausibly low if treated as mg/dL  a_cap <- corrected_calcium(   data = demo2,   col_map = col_map,   units = "conventional",   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   extreme_rules = list(ca_mgdl = c(4, 15), alb_gdl = c(2, 5)) )  head(a_cap) #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>   corrected_calcium #>               <dbl> #> 1              8.73 #> 2              9.16 #> 3             NA    #> 4              8.96 #> 5              3.74 #> 6             10.0

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Prefer explicit units; auto mode may warn convert SI inferred. Ensure Ca Alb units match chosen mode; mismatched units mis-scale results. Tighten extreme_rules lab ranges; use extreme_action = “error” strict QC. Downstream consumers expecting mg/dL keep units = “conventional” convert explicitly.

title: Corrected Calcium
code: # Payne formula spot check (conventional): Ca = 9.0, Alb = 3.0 => 9.0 + 0.8*(4-3) = 9.8 v1 <- corrected_calcium(data.frame(Ca = 9.0, Alb = 3.0), col_map, units = "conventional") stopifnot(abs(v1$corrected_calcium - 9.8) < 1e-10)  # SI round-trip: Ca = 2.25 mmol/L, Alb = 35 g/L # working: Ca_mg = 2.25*4 = 9.0, Alb_gdl = 35/10 = 3.5 # corrected_mg = 9.0 + 0.8*(4 - 3.5) = 9.4; corrected_mmol = 9.4/4 = 2.35 v2 <- corrected_calcium(data.frame(Ca = 2.25, Alb = 35), col_map, units = "si") stopifnot(abs(v2$corrected_calcium - 2.35) < 1e-10)  cat("Payne conventional:", v1$corrected_calcium, "mg/dL\n") #> Payne conventional: 9.8 mg/dL cat("Payne SI round-trip:", v2$corrected_calcium, "mmol/L\n") #> Payne SI round-trip: 2.35 mmol/L

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Compute 10 fasting insulin sensitivity/resistance indices fasting glucose (mmol/L) insulin (pmol/L). Internally converts glucose mg/dL (x18) insulin muU/mL (/6). Handles column mapping, numeric coercion, NA policy, optional extreme screening, optional normalization.

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: fasting glucose (mmol/L) insulin (pmol/L) need multiple sensitivity/resistance indices . want explicit NA handling (keep/omit/error) plus optional extreme-value screening normalization. column names differ defaults need mapping.

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Required columns (mapped via col_map): G0 (fasting glucose, mmol/L), I0 (fasting insulin, pmol/L). Units converted internally (mmol/L -> mg/dL; pmol/L -> muU/mL). Convert beforehand inputs already units avoid double-scaling. na_action: keep (default), omit, error governs required-input missingness. check_extreme: optional screening computed indices extreme_limit; actions: cap, NA, error. normalize: none (default) z, range, inverse, robust applied extreme handling.

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: G0: fasting plasma glucose (mmol/L). I0: fasting insulin (pmol/L). use different labels (e.g., glucose_mmol, insulin_pmol), remap accordingly; column names fine long hold right values.
code: col_map <- list(   G0 = "G0",   I0 = "I0" )

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Compute indices display newly calculated columns (full input data). preview shows newly computed indices. _inv columns inverted larger negatives indicate greater insulin resistance; QUICKI, Raynaud, Isi_basal decrease resistance worsens. rows retained na_action = "keep".
code: fis <- fasting_is(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   normalize = "none",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  fis_new <- setdiff(names(fis), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(fis, dplyr::all_of(fis_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   Fasting_inv Raynaud HOMA_IR_inv  FIRI QUICKI Belfiore_basal Ig_ratio_basal #>         <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1      -14.0     2.86       -58.1  52.3  0.139        0.00153        -0.149  #> 2      -16.0     2.49       -66.6  60.0  0.137        0.00133        -0.172  #> 3      -15.7     2.55       -61.6  55.5  0.138        0.00144        -0.178  #> 4       -6.75    5.93       -30.5  27.4  0.153        0.00291        -0.0664 #> 5      -13.6     2.94       -56.9  51.2  0.140        0.00156        -0.145  #> 6      -17.0     2.36       -82.7  74.4  0.133        0.00107        -0.155  #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Isi_basal <dbl>, Bennett <dbl>, HOMA_IR_rev_inv <dbl>

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Fasting_inv: inverted fasting insulin (higher negative = higher insulin) Raynaud: 40 / insulin HOMA_IR_inv, HOMA_IR_rev_inv: inverted HOMA formulations FIRI: fasting insulin resistance index QUICKI: insulin sensitivity index (higher = sensitive) Belfiore_basal: 2 / (insulin x glucose + 1) Ig_ratio_basal: -insulin/glucose (inverted) Isi_basal: 10000 / (glucose x insulin) Bennett: 1 / (log insulin x log glucose)

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Required columns (map via col_map): G0 glucose (mmol/L), I0 insulin (pmol/L). Internal conversions: glucose x18 -> mg/dL; insulin /6 -> muU/mL. Extremes: default check_extreme = FALSE; TRUE, outputs screened extreme_limit. extreme_action = "cap" trims +/-limit. Missingness: na_action controls row handling (keep/omit/error). Normalization: normalize = "none" default; options apply columnwise scaling extreme handling.

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Show row handling extreme scanning behave tiny slice. na_action = "keep" preserves six rows (NA-derived outputs), na_action = "omit" drops row missing G0. Extreme scanning extreme_action = "cap" trim outputs exceeding extreme_limit; none capped slice.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("G0", "I0")] demo$G0[2] <- NA  fis_keep <- fasting_is(demo, col_map, na_action = "keep", check_extreme = FALSE) fis_omit <- fasting_is(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit", check_extreme = FALSE)  fis_extreme <- fasting_is(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_limit = 1e3,   extreme_action = "cap",   normalize = "none",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(fis_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(fis_omit),   extreme_head = head(dplyr::select(fis_extreme, dplyr::all_of(fis_new))) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 5 #>  #> $extreme_head #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   Fasting_inv Raynaud HOMA_IR_inv  FIRI QUICKI Belfiore_basal Ig_ratio_basal #>         <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1      -14.0     2.86       -58.1  52.3  0.139        0.00153        -0.149  #> 2      -16.0     2.49        NA    NA   NA           NA              NA      #> 3      -15.7     2.55       -61.6  55.5  0.138        0.00144        -0.178  #> 4       -6.75    5.93       -30.5  27.4  0.153        0.00291        -0.0664 #> 5      -13.6     2.94       -56.9  51.2  0.140        0.00156        -0.145  #> 6      -17.0     2.36       -82.7  74.4  0.133        0.00107        -0.155  #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Isi_basal <dbl>, Bennett <dbl>, HOMA_IR_rev_inv <dbl>

text: required inputs must mapped present; missing mappings columns abort. na_action: keep leaves NA-derived outputs; omit drops rows; error stops missing input. check_extreme screens outputs magnitudes beyond extreme_limit; adjust extreme_limit choose extreme_action (cap/NA/error). normalize optional post-hoc scaling; set "none" preserve native scales.
