text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key resolved . Example: fasting_is(): column map: G0 -> 'G0', I0 -> 'I0' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: fasting_is(): results: Fasting_inv 28/30, HOMA_IR_inv 28/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  invisible(fasting_is(   data    = sim_small[1:5, ],   col_map = col_map,   verbose = TRUE )) #> fasting_is(): preparing inputs #> fasting_is(): column map: G0 -> 'G0', I0 -> 'I0' #> fasting_is(): results: Fasting_inv 5/5, Raynaud 5/5, HOMA_IR_inv 5/5, FIRI 5/5, QUICKI 5/5, Belfiore_basal 5/5, Ig_ratio_basal 5/5, Isi_basal 5/5, Bennett 5/5, HOMA_IR_rev_inv 5/5  options(old_opt)

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Non-numeric inputs coerced numeric; coercion introduces NAs triggers warning. pipelines, prefer na_action = "omit" "error" avoid silent gaps; use "keep" exploratory summaries. Enable check_extreme = TRUE working noisy simulated data; use extreme_action = "error" strict QA. Set normalize align scales across indices feeding distance-based models; leave "none" interpretability. See ?fasting_is full argument reference diagnostics.

title: Frailty index
text: Compute deficit-based frailty index (FI) via di::di() HealthMarkers validation, NA/extreme handling, optional rescaling, tidy output. default, numeric columns auto-selected deficits cols = NULL (excluding age present). Higher FI indicates deficits (roughly 0–1).

title: Frailty index
text: deficit variables (binary/logical [0,1]-scaled) need standard FI quickly. want guardrails: NA policy, --range screening/capping, optional rescaling FI computation. plan consume FI tibble (return = “data”) keep original di::di list (return = “list”).

title: Frailty index
text: Dependency: suggested package di must installed; examples calls skipped/stop otherwise. Deficits: supply via cols (character vector) allow auto-selection numeric columns cols = NULL (age excluded provided). Optional age: kept plotting/bins excluded deficit selection. na_action: ignore/warn/error (legacy) plus aliases keep (like ignore) omit (drops rows missing selected deficit). check_extreme: NULL (default; scans rescale = FALSE), TRUE (always scan <0 >1), FALSE (never scan). extreme_action: warn/ignore/error/cap/NA. rescale: default TRUE; FALSE, range scanning active default (check_extreme = NULL). return: list (di object) data (tibble di plus selected deficits [+ age]).

title: Frailty index
text: examples run di package installed. Expected inputs: deficits ideally scaled [0,1] (logical/binary). age optional; present excluded auto-selected deficits.
code: library(HealthMarkers)  has_di <- requireNamespace("di", quietly = TRUE)  demo_df <- data.frame(   age = c(70, 75, 80, 78, 74, 69, 72, 77),   d1  = c(0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1),   d2  = c(0.2, 0.8, 1.0, 0.4, 0.1, 0.9, 0.0, 0.7),   d3  = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE) )

title: Frailty index
text: can supply cols let function auto-select numeric deficits cols = NULL. name explicitly keep age optional plotting.

title: Frailty index
text: Return tidy tibble (return = "data") can preview FI alongside deficits. FI ranges 0 (deficits) toward 1 (deficits present). rescale = TRUE allows non-binary numeric inputs; na_action = "keep" retains rows even deficits contain NA (none slice).
code: if (has_di) {   fi_tbl <- frailty_index(     data = demo_df,     cols = cols,     age = "age",     rescale = TRUE,     na_action = "keep",     check_extreme = NULL,   # legacy: scan only if rescale = FALSE     extreme_action = "warn",     return = "data",     verbose = FALSE   )    head(dplyr::select(fi_tbl, di, dplyr::all_of(cols))) } else {   message("Install the 'di' package to run the examples.") } #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>       di    d1    d2 d3    #>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <lgl> #> 1 0.4        0   0.2 TRUE  #> 2 0.6        1   0.8 FALSE #> 3 1          1   1   TRUE  #> 4 0.467      0   0.4 TRUE  #> 5 0.0333     0   0.1 FALSE #> 6 0.967      1   0.9 TRUE

title: Frailty index
text: Deficits: binary/logical [0,1]-scaled; supply via data cols (let auto-select numeric deficits cols = NULL). age (optional): excluded auto-selected deficits; useful plotting/bins. na_action: keep/ignore retain rows missing deficits; omit drops rows; error stops missing selected deficit; warn emits warnings. check_extreme: TRUE scans <0 >1; NULL scans rescale = FALSE; FALSE skips scanning. extreme_action: cap truncates [0,1]; NA blanks; warn/ignore leave -; error stops. return: data yields tibble (di + selected deficits [+ age]); list returns di::di object.

title: Frailty index
text: Demonstrate row dropping, capping, tidy return. na_action = "keep", rows remain (missing deficits propagate FI). na_action = "omit", rows missing selected deficit dropped. extreme_action = "cap" trims --range deficits (e.g., 1.2) [0,1] FI calculation.
code: if (has_di) {   demo_miss <- demo_df   demo_miss$d2[3] <- NA       # introduce missing   demo_miss$d1[2] <- 1.2      # out-of-range to be capped    fi_keep <- frailty_index(     data = demo_miss,     cols = cols,     age = "age",     rescale = FALSE,          # triggers range scan when check_extreme is NULL     na_action = "keep",     check_extreme = NULL,     extreme_action = "cap",     return = "data",     verbose = FALSE   )    fi_omit <- frailty_index(     data = demo_miss,     cols = cols,     age = "age",     rescale = FALSE,     na_action = "omit",     check_extreme = NULL,     extreme_action = "cap",     return = "data",     verbose = FALSE   )    list(     keep_rows = nrow(fi_keep),     omit_rows = nrow(fi_omit),     capped_example = head(dplyr::select(fi_keep, di, dplyr::all_of(cols)))   ) } else {   message("Install the 'di' package to run the examples.") } #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $capped_example #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>       di    d1    d2 d3    #>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <lgl> #> 1 0.4      0     0.2 TRUE  #> 2 0.667    1.2   0.8 FALSE #> 3 1        1    NA   TRUE  #> 4 0.467    0     0.4 TRUE  #> 5 0.0333   0     0.1 FALSE #> 6 0.967    1     0.9 TRUE

title: Frailty index
text: di package must installed; otherwise calls stop (examples skip missing). Provide deficits via cols rely auto-selection numeric columns (age excluded present). Choose na_action control row retention vs. dropping missing deficits. Use check_extreme/extreme_action scan cap blank values outside [0,1] desired. return = "data" gives tibble (di + selected deficits [+ age]); return = "list" returns raw di::di output.

title: Frailty index
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit structured messages. function uses cols vector rather named col_map, column map message lists selected deficit columns directly: Preparing inputs — row count, deficit count, whether age provided. Column map — lists selected deficit column names. Example: frailty_index(): column map: d1, d2, d3 Results summary (return = "data") — shows many rows di non-NA. Example: frailty_index(): results: di 8/8 return = "list", range FI vector reported instead. verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: if (has_di) {   old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")    invisible(frailty_index(     data    = demo_df,     cols    = cols,     age     = "age",     return  = "data",     verbose = TRUE   ))    options(old_opt) } else {   message("Install the 'di' package to run the examples.") } #> frailty_index(): preparing inputs (8 rows, 3 deficits, age provided) #> frailty_index(): column map: d1, d2, d3 #> frailty_index(): results: di 8/8

title: Frailty index
text: Keep deficits close [0,1]; rescale = TRUE can normalize mild deviations, extreme outliers may still need capping. Use extreme_action = "cap" check_extreme = TRUE (rescale = FALSE + check_extreme = NULL) deficits might stray outside [0,1]. Choose na_action = "omit" "error" stricter pipelines; keep/ignore exploratory summaries. return = "data" pairs FI selected deficits quick binding downstream tibbles. Set verbose = TRUE see selected deficits, NA scan counts, completion range.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Compute simplified, non-validated approximation FRAX 10-year fracture risk (major osteoporotic hip). Normalizes sex labels, applies HM-CS missing/extreme policies, returns placeholder risks; proprietary FRAX algorithm.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: need placeholder fracture-risk proxy demos pipelines acknowledging clinical FRAX. want explicit NA extreme-value handling (age/BMD bounds) column mapping flexibility. plan swap real FRAX later; keeps interfaces stable meantime.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Required columns (map via col_map): age (years), sex (codes starting m/f 1/0). Optional binary risk factors: prior fracture, parent fracture, glucocorticoids, rheumatoid, secondary osteoporosis, current smoker, high alcohol. Optional: femoral neck BMD T-score (bmd). na_action: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts. check_extreme: TRUE, scan age BMD; default bounds age 40–90, BMD T -6 2 (override extreme_rules). extreme_action: warn (default) leaves values; cap trims bounds; NA blanks; error aborts; ignore skips messages.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Illustrative demo data ; replace cohort.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Provide column names; left-hand keys fixed.
code: col_map <- list(   age = "Age",   sex = "Sex",   prior_fracture  = "prior_fx",   parent_fracture = "parent_fx",   steroids        = "steroids",   rheumatoid      = "rheumatoid",   secondary_op    = "secondary",   smoker          = "smoker",   alcohol         = "alcohol",   bmd             = "bmd" )

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: age: age years (required). sex: sex code; first letter m/f 1/0 (required; normalized male/female). Optional binary risk factors: prior fracture, parent fracture, glucocorticoids, rheumatoid, secondary osteoporosis, current smoker, high alcohol. bmd: femoral neck T-score (optional); placeholder adjustment present.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Return placeholder risks normalized age/sex/BMD used. Interpretation: frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent placeholder probabilities (clinical). frax_sex_norm shows normalized sex; frax_age_used frax_bmd_tscore reflect capping/blanking applied extreme checks.
code: frax_out <- frax_score(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "warn",   verbose = FALSE )  head(dplyr::select(frax_out, frax_major_percent, frax_hip_percent, frax_sex_norm, frax_age_used, frax_bmd_tscore)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent frax_sex_norm frax_age_used #>                <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>                 <dbl> #> 1               15.5              8.5 female                   65 #> 2               14.4              8.9 male                     72 #> 3               19               13.3 female                   80 #> 4               10.4              6   female                   68 #> 5               11.8              7.6 male                     74 #> 6                7                3.5 female                   60 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: frax_bmd_tscore <dbl>

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Required: age (years) sex codes mapped col_map. Optional: binary risk factors BMD T-score. Non-numeric inputs coerced; non-finite values become NA. na_action: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts. check_extreme: TRUE, scan age BMD; defaults bounds age 40–90, BMD T -6 2 (override extreme_rules). extreme_action: warn (default) leaves values; cap trims bounds; NA blanks; error aborts; ignore skips messages.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Show row handling, capping, dropping small slice. na_action = "keep" retains rows even missing/unknown sex age (outputs become NA). na_action = "omit" drops rows missing required inputs. extreme_action = "cap" trims age/BMD allowed ranges risk calculation.
code: demo_miss <- demo demo_miss$Age[3] <- NA      # missing age demo_miss$Sex[4] <- "X"    # unknown sex -> NA demo_miss$Age[5] <- 105     # extreme age demo_miss$bmd[2] <- -7      # extreme BMD  keep_cap <- frax_score(   data = demo_miss,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = FALSE )  omit_cap <- frax_score(   data = demo_miss,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(keep_cap),   omit_rows = nrow(omit_cap),   capped_preview = head(dplyr::select(keep_cap, frax_age_used, frax_bmd_tscore, frax_major_percent, frax_hip_percent)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 4 #>  #> $capped_preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   frax_age_used frax_bmd_tscore frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent #>           <dbl>           <dbl>              <dbl>            <dbl> #> 1            65            -2                 15.5              8.5 #> 2            72            -6                 14.4              8.9 #> 3            NA            -2.5               NA               NA   #> 4            68            -1.5               NA               NA   #> 5            90            -0.8               15               12   #> 6            60            NA                  7                3.5

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Placeholder approximation ; validated FRAX calculator clinical use. must map age sex; missing mappings columns abort. Choose na_action enforce relax completeness; error strict, omit drops, keep retains. Use check_extreme/extreme_action cap, blank, reject --range age/BMD (defaults age 40–90, BMD T -6 2). verbose = TRUE surfaces progress QA messages.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- data.frame(Age = c(65, 72), Sex = c("F", "M"),                    bmd = c(-2.0, -1.0)) frax_score(   df_v,   col_map = list(age = "Age", sex = "Sex", bmd = "bmd"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> frax_score(): preparing inputs #> frax_score(): column map: age -> 'Age', sex -> 'Sex' #> frax_score(): results: frax_major_percent 2/2, frax_hip_percent 2/2, frax_sex_norm 2/2, frax_age_used 2/2, frax_bmd_tscore 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent frax_sex_norm frax_age_used #>                <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>                 <dbl> #> 1                8.5              4.7 female                   65 #> 2                5.4              3.3 male                     72 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: frax_bmd_tscore <dbl> options(old_opt)

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Normalize sex coding M/F 1/0 calling avoid NA normalization. Adjust extreme_rules bounds differ; set check_extreme = FALSE skip scanning. Use na_action = "error" strict pipelines; omit filter incomplete rows; keep/warn exploratory use. Outputs illustrative ; replace validated FRAX implementation clinical workflows.

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Load bundled simulated dataset, pick markers compute, view outputs care . examples use shipped data; swap data ready.

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: want quick tour core helpers all_health_markers() orchestrator. need see column inference vs explicit mapping data. prefer ready--run code snippets (optional dependencies required examples).

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Many clinical/metabolic markers: glycemic, lipid, renal, inflammatory, liver, anthropometric/obesity, atherogenic indices, frailty/Charlson, sweat/urine panels, micronutrients, pulmonary indices, . Single-purpose helpers (e.g., cvd_marker_aip(), sweat_markers(), fasting_is()) plus all_health_markers() run multiple groups . Column inference infer_cols() override via col_map labels differ. Light missing-data utilities (impute_missing()) prep data computing markers.

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Targeted helper: cvd_marker_aip(sim_small) renal_markers(sim_small). Multiple groups: all_health_markers(sim_small, = c("glycemic", "lipid")). Everything: all_health_markers(sim_small, = "") (wide output; filter afterward). Insulin sensitivity/resistance: fasting_is(), ogtt_is(), adipo_is(), include insulin groups all_health_markers() col_map glucose/insulin.

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: all_health_markers(data, = ...) — run multiple groups markers . cvd_marker_aip(data, col_map = list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c")) — atherogenic index plasma. renal_markers(data) — renal eGFR-related outputs. glycemic_markers(data) — glycemic metrics; pair fasting_is() / ogtt_is() / adipo_is() insulin indices. sweat_markers(data, check_extreme = TRUE) urine_markers(data) — alternate biofluid panels.

what: Load the package and simulated data
title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Inputs: package plus bundled RDS file inst/extdata/simulated_hm_data.rds. Outputs: two data frames—sim (full) sim_small (first 30 rows fast examples).
code: if (!requireNamespace("HealthMarkers", quietly = TRUE)) {   if (requireNamespace("pkgload", quietly = TRUE)) {     pkgload::load_all("..")   } else {     stop("Install HealthMarkers (or pkgload for development) before knitting.", call. = FALSE)   } } library(HealthMarkers)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path)  # Work with a small subset using slice_head sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  dim(sim_small) #> [1]  30 282

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Input: sim_small inferred columns. : all_health_markers() auto-detects columns computes requested groups. Output: tibble computed markers. print new columns.

what: Data readiness: columns at a glance
title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: functions infer columns automatically. names differ, provide col_map. Typical defaults: Column inference usually enough; data use different labels, provide col_map override. Tip: inspect package infer data without running calculations, use:
code: infer_cols(sim_small)

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Check columns: infer_cols(your_data) see picked . Handle missing values: impute_missing(your_data, method = "mice" | "missForest" | "mean" | "median" | "constant") use completed data calculators. Normalize (insulin helpers): insulin-related functions accept normalization choices (e.g., normalize = "z", normalize = "range"); leave defaults unless need scaled outputs.

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Input: data explicit column aliases. : provides TG/HDL names compute AIP columns differ. Output: tibble AIP result.
code: lipid_cols <- list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c")  aip <- cvd_marker_aip(sim_small, col_map = lipid_cols, na_action = "keep") aip_new <- setdiff(names(aip), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(aip, dplyr::all_of(aip_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   model   value #>   <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 AIP   -0.159  #> 2 AIP    0.175  #> 3 AIP   -0.0214 #> 4 AIP    0.310  #> 5 AIP    0.152  #> 6 AIP   -0.169

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: functions accept na_action. shows outputs differ required inputs missing. na_action aliases: "ignore" backward-compatible alias "keep" (behavior; retained older code continues work). "warn" also alias "keep" additionally emits missingness warning. reading another function’s help page see "ignore" listed first choices, behaves identically "keep". want impute computing markers:
code: missing_demo <- sim_small[1:5, c("TG", "HDL_c")] missing_demo$TG[2] <- NA  # Keep rows (produces NA where inputs are missing) cvd_marker_aip(missing_demo, col_map = lipid_cols, na_action = "keep") #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   model   value #>   <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 AIP   -0.159  #> 2 AIP   NA      #> 3 AIP   -0.0214 #> 4 AIP    0.310  #> 5 AIP    0.152  # Drop rows with required NA cvd_marker_aip(missing_demo, col_map = lipid_cols, na_action = "omit") #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   model   value #>   <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 AIP   -0.159  #> 2 AIP   -0.0214 #> 3 AIP    0.310  #> 4 AIP    0.152 imputed <- impute_missing(sim_small, method = "mice", m = 1, maxit = 3) head(imputed)

what: Imputation options (what happens under the hood)
title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: impute_missing() preprocessing helper; marker functions impute internally. Run first, call calculators. method = "mice": multiple imputation chained equations via mice; control m (imputations) maxit (iterations). method = "missForest": nonparametric random-forest imputation via missForest; good mixed data types. method = "mean" | "median" | "constant": simple deterministic fills numeric columns. Output: data frame imputed values replacing missing entries; pass all_health_markers() helper (e.g., cvd_marker_aip(), renal_markers()).

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Scan extreme sweat values warn without stopping. Output: sweat markers warnings emitted.
code: sweat <- sweat_markers(sim_small, check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_action = "warn") sweat_new <- setdiff(names(sweat), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(sweat, dplyr::all_of(sweat_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   Na_K_ratio sweat_rate #>        <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1       7.36     0.594  #> 2       4.51     0.579  #> 3       4.67     0.128  #> 4      14.2      0.0497 #> 5      10.6      0.131  #> 6       5.89     0.0629

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: function supports optional inputs (columns extend outputs present), set verbose = TRUE see optional markers computed columns absent data. helps distinguish “column found” “marker intentionally absent”. informational message lists optional keys (glucose, HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin) matching column, caller knows derived metrics (e.g., prediabetes flag, HOMA_CP, LAR) NA output. add columns data, message disappears metrics computed automatically.
code: # Provide only required columns; optional columns are absent glyc_min <- sim_small[, c("HDL_c", "TG", "BMI")]  # verbose = TRUE lists which optional markers were skipped glyc_out <- glycemic_markers(glyc_min, verbose = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Input: sim_small; groups: glycemic, lipid, renal, inflammatory. Output: tibble markers (first rows shown).

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
