title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Compute SPISE, METS_IR, prediabetes/diabetes flags (HbA1c), HOMA_CP, LAR, ASI, TyG_index fasting labs anthropometry. Required inputs: HDL_c (mmol/L), TG (mmol/L), BMI (kg/m^2); optional glucose/HbA1c/C-peptide/insulin/leptin/adiponectin present. Uses packaged simulated data runnable examples.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: fasting lipids BMI want quick insulin resistance/sensitivity proxies. can supply glucose/HbA1c/C-peptide/insulin/adipokines available enrich outputs. need explicit NA handling optional extreme-value screening.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Required (mapped via col_map): HDL_c (mmol/L), TG (mmol/L), BMI (kg/m^2). Optional: glucose, HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin. Unit notes: TyG converts TG mg/dL (x88.57) glucose mg/dL (x18); HOMA_CP uses (G0 * (C_peptide/6)) / 22.5. na_action: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing used inputs; error aborts. check_extreme: TRUE, scans inputs default broad ranges; extreme_action = warn/cap/NA/error/ignore.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Use small subset simulated data (30-50 rows). Swap sim_small data frame practice.
code: library(HealthMarkers)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Map required optional inputs column names; left-hand keys fixed.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: HDL_c: HDL cholesterol (mmol/L) required TG: triglycerides (mmol/L) required BMI: body mass index (kg/m^2) required glucose: fasting glucose (mmol/L) HbA1c: hemoglobin A1c (mmol/mol) C_peptide: C-peptide (pmol/L) G0: fasting glucose (mmol/L) HOMA_CP I0: fasting insulin (pmol/L) ASI leptin: leptin (ng/mL) adiponectin: adiponectin (ng/mL)
code: col_map <- list(   HDL_c = "HDL_c",   TG = "TG",   BMI = "BMI",   glucose = "glucose",   HbA1c = "HbA1c",   C_peptide = "C_peptide",   G0 = "G0",   I0 = "I0",   leptin = "leptin",   adiponectin = "adiponectin" )

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Compute markers show newly created columns. Interpretation: SPISE METS_IR summarize insulin sensitivity/resistance; TyG_index rises higher TG/glucose; HOMA_CP approximates insulin resistance via C-peptide glucose; LAR ASI relate adipokines insulin; HbA1c flags populate prediabetes/diabetes available. Rows retained na_action = "keep".
code: gm <- glycemic_markers(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  gm_new <- setdiff(names(gm), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(gm, dplyr::all_of(gm_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   SPISE METS_IR prediabetes HOMA_CP   LAR   ASI TyG_index #>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  6.54    175.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.39 #> 2  7.04    163.           0      NA    NA    NA      9.13 #> 3  6.74    342.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.42 #> 4  4.13  -1870.           0      NA    NA    NA      9.07 #> 5  6.25    271.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.95 #> 6  5.34    347.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.37

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Required: HDL_c (mmol/L), TG (mmol/L), BMI (kg/m^2). Optional present/mapped: glucose, HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin. TyG converts TG mg/dL (88.57) glucose mg/dL (18) internally. HOMA_CP uses (G0 * (C_peptide/6)) / 22.5; verify units C-peptide. Missingness: na_action controls row handling (keep/omit/error/warn/ignore). Extreme screening: check_extreme = TRUE can warn/cap/NA/error using default broad ranges; override via extreme_rules.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Show row handling extreme screening tiny slice. na_action = "keep" preserves rows (missing inputs flow NA outputs); na_action = "omit" drops rows missing/invalid used inputs. extreme_action = "cap" trims --range values (e.g., TG = 30) computation.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("HDL_c", "TG", "BMI")] demo$glucose <- c(5.5, 6.2, 7.0, 5.8, 6.0, 5.4) demo$HbA1c <- c(42, 39, 41, 45, 44, 40) demo$C_peptide <- c(300, 450, 500, 400, 380, 360) demo$G0 <- demo$glucose demo$I0 <- c(60, 90, 120, 80, 75, 70) demo$leptin <- c(10, 12, 15, 11, 9, 13) demo$adiponectin <- c(8, 7, 6, 9, 10, 7) demo$TG[3] <- 30       # extreme TG demo$HbA1c[2] <- NA    # missing HbA1c  gm_keep <- glycemic_markers(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = FALSE )  gm_omit <- glycemic_markers(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(gm_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(gm_omit),   capped_preview = head(dplyr::select(gm_keep, dplyr::all_of(gm_new))) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 5 #>  #> $capped_preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   SPISE METS_IR prediabetes HOMA_CP   LAR    ASI TyG_index #>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  6.54    179.           1    12.2  1.25 0.133       8.45 #> 2  7.04    173.          NA    20.7  1.71 0.0778      9.31 #> 3  3.82    504.           0    25.9  2.5  0.05       11.6  #> 4  4.13  -1886.           1    17.2  1.22 0.112       9.10 #> 5  6.25    284.           1    16.9  0.9  0.133       9.09 #> 6  5.34    332.           0    14.4  1.86 0.1         8.25

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Required inputs must present/mapped; missing required columns abort. na_action governs whether missing/invalid inputs drop rows, warn, error. check_extreme/extreme_action can cap, blank, warn, abort --range inputs. Optional columns drive optional markers; absent inputs yield NA markers.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- data.frame(HDL_c = 1.0, TG = 1.3, BMI = 24, glucose = 5.6) glycemic_markers(   df_v,   col_map = list(HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG", BMI = "BMI", glucose = "glucose"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> glycemic_markers(): preparing inputs #> glycemic_markers(): column map: HDL_c -> 'HDL_c', TG -> 'TG', BMI -> 'BMI' #> glycemic_markers(): optional markers not computed (columns absent): HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin #> glycemic_markers(): results: SPISE 1/1, METS_IR 0/1, prediabetes 0/1, diabetes 0/1, HOMA_CP 0/1, LAR 0/1, ASI 0/1, TyG_index 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   SPISE METS_IR prediabetes diabetes HOMA_CP   LAR   ASI TyG_index #>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>    <int>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  8.10      NA          NA       NA      NA    NA    NA      8.67 options(old_opt)

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Start fasting lipids BMI; add glucose/HbA1c/C-peptide/insulin/adipokines available richer markers. Convert units beforehand data expected mmol/L/pmol/L/ng/mL units. stricter pipelines, use na_action = "omit" "error"; keep/warn exploratory work. Turn check_extreme = TRUE extreme_action = "cap" working noisy simulated inputs. See ?glycemic_markers full argument details marker definitions.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Guide three aggregators: - all_insulin_indices(): fasting/OGTT/adipose/tracer insulin sensitivity/resistance panels. - metabolic_markers(): selectable bundle (insulin, adiposity_sds, cardio, lipid, liver, glycemic, MetS) appended data. - all_health_markers(): registry-driven dispatcher across marker groups optional column inference insulin inclusion. wrappers use guards skip missing inputs/optional deps instead crashing verbose = FALSE.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: want one call run multiple marker groups, optional insulin panels. need column inference default can supply col_map nonstandard names. prefer graceful skipping (hard errors) inputs optional packages absent.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: data: data.frame/tibble columns required groups select. col_map: optional mapping; recommended insulin/lipid groups nonstandard names. : groups compute. metabolic_markers() supports insulin, adiposity_sds, cardio, lipid, liver, glycemic, mets. all_health_markers() covers larger registry (lipid, renal, inflammatory, liver, obesity, insulin panels, etc.). include_insulin: whether add insulin panels all_health_markers(); requires glucose/insulin columns. na_action: keep/omit/error (plus ignore/warn supported) forwarded underlying helpers. Optional dependencies: groups rely suggested packages (e.g., CVD risk backends). Missing deps skipped NA outputs.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Use packaged simulated dataset runnable examples. Swap sim_small data frame.
code: library(HealthMarkers)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Health marker aggregators
text: groups infer columns automatically. Provide col_map names differ using insulin panels.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: TG, HDL_c, LDL_c, TC: lipid inputs (mmol/L) BMI: body mass index (kg/m^2) G0, I0: fasting glucose (mmol/L) insulin (pmol/L) insulin panels ALT, AST: liver enzymes (U/L) age, sex: demographics used several groups
code: col_map <- list(   TG = "TG",   HDL_c = "HDL_c",   LDL_c = "LDL_c",   TC = "TC",   BMI = "BMI",   G0 = "G0",   I0 = "I0",   ALT = "ALT",   AST = "AST",   age = "age",   sex = "sex" )

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Run metabolic_markers() small subset, focusing glycemic, lipid, liver groups (insulin panel excluded keep inputs minimal). Show new columns. Interpretation: outputs include lipid ratios, TyG-based metrics, liver-derived markers. Rows retained na_action = "keep" propagates missing inputs NA outputs.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: all_health_markers() can auto-infer columns use col_map. request multi-panel set skip insulin minimal inputs. Verbose mode reports inferred/user mappings groups computed skipped. Skipped groups leave input untouched.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Include insulin indices alongside small panel. Missing OGTT/adipose/tracer inputs skipped safely; fasting indices compute G0/I0 present. verbose summary note insulin sub-panels ran (fasting) skipped due missing OGTT/adipose/tracer inputs.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: na_action controls row retention. Demonstrate tiny slice containing missing lipid value. keep propagates missing inputs NA markers; omit drops rows required-field NAs selected groups.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("TG", "HDL_c", "BMI", "ALT", "AST")] demo$TG[2] <- NA  mm_keep <- metabolic_markers(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   which = c("glycemic", "lipid", "liver"),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  mm_omit <- metabolic_markers(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   which = c("glycemic", "lipid", "liver"),   na_action = "omit",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(mm_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(mm_omit) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 6

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Required inputs chosen groups must present/mapped; missing required columns abort skip depending underlying helper. na_action chooses whether missing inputs lead retained rows (keep), dropped rows (omit), immediate errors (error). normalize mode affect insulin outputs; ignored groups use . all_health_markers() infers columns col_map missing/NULL; inference fails required keys found. Verbose mode summarizes mappings plus computed/skipped groups can audit run.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column mapping summary, group execution summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- data.frame(TC = 200, HDL_c = 50, TG = 150, LDL_c = 120, BMI = 25) col_v <- list(TC = "TC", HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG", LDL_c = "LDL_c", BMI = "BMI") all_health_markers(   df_v, col_v,   which = c("lipid"),   include_insulin = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE ) #> all_health_markers(): preparing inputs #> Column mapping summary: TC->TC (user), HDL_c->HDL_c (user), TG->TG (user), LDL_c->LDL_c (user), BMI->BMI (user) #> health_markers(): calling lipid #> lipid_markers(): preparing inputs #> lipid_markers(): column map: TC -> 'TC', HDL_c -> 'HDL_c', TG -> 'TG' #> lipid_markers(): results: non_HDL_c 1/1, remnant_c 1/1, ratio_TC_HDL 1/1, ratio_TG_HDL 1/1, ratio_LDL_HDL 1/1, ApoB_ApoA1 0/1 #> all_health_markers(): summary - computed: lipid #>    TC HDL_c  TG LDL_c BMI non_HDL_c remnant_c ratio_TC_HDL ratio_TG_HDL #> 1 200    50 150   120  25       150        30            4            3 #>   ratio_LDL_HDL ApoB_ApoA1 #> 1           2.4         NA options(old_opt)

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Start narrow: pick groups metabolic_markers(= ...) running full registry. Supply explicit col_map column names differ, especially insulin lipid inputs. Set include_insulin = TRUE all_health_markers() glucose/insulin columns available (G0/I0 OGTT inputs mapped). Use na_action = "error" strict pipelines; omit dropping incomplete rows; keep exploratory runs. computing, use dplyr::select() keep markers care ; outputs can wide.

Compute nine hormone ratios built-QA: FAI, LH_FSH, E2_P, T3_T4, ARR, Ins_Glu, GH_IGF1, PRL_T, CAR_slope. Inputs coerced numeric, non-finite become NA, optional extreme screening can warn, cap, blank, error --range labs.

complete hormone panel (mapped via col_map) need quick ratio features. want explicit NA handling optional extreme-value screening avoid crashes bad labs. need outputs preserve row counts unless explicitly omit missing rows.

keys required: total testosterone, SHBG, LH, FSH, estradiol, progesterone, free T3, free T4, aldosterone, renin, insulin, glucagon, GH, IGF1, prolactin, cortisol_0, cortisol_30. col_map: map required key column names; missing mappings columns abort. Units: keep units consistent within ratio (e.g., cortisol nmol/L; aldosterone/renin matching units). na_action: keep/ignore retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts. check_extreme: optional; extreme_action = warn/cap/NA/error/ignore.
Use small synthetic tibble (covers required inputs) chunk runs anywhere. Replace horm_demo lab data.

Map every required key column names; mandatory computation. total_testosterone, SHBG LH, FSH estradiol, progesterone free_T3, free_T4 aldosterone, renin insulin, glucagon GH, IGF1 prolactin cortisol_0, cortisol_30 (nmol/L; slope uses 30-minute delta / 30)
code: col_map <- list(   total_testosterone = "tt",   SHBG = "shbg",   LH = "lh",   FSH = "fsh",   estradiol = "e2",   progesterone = "p4",   free_T3 = "ft3",   free_T4 = "ft4",   aldosterone = "aldo",   renin = "renin",   insulin = "ins",   glucagon = "gluc",   GH = "gh",   IGF1 = "igf1",   prolactin = "prl",   cortisol_0 = "cort0",   cortisol_30 = "cort30" )

text: Compute ratios preview outputs. Non-finite values set NA; rows retained na_action = "keep"/"ignore".
code: hm_out <- hormone_markers(   data = horm_demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  hm_out #> # A tibble: 4 × 10 #>     FAI LH_FSH  E2_P  E2_T T3_T4   ARR Ins_Glu GH_IGF1 PRL_T CAR_slope #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  16.7  0.75   60    24   0.281  12.5   0.188 0.00667  2         6    #> 2  17.5  0.778  46.7  20   0.32   12     0.286 0.00562  1.71      5.67 #> 3  16.1  0.714  61.1  24.4 0.253  12.7   0.24  0.00588  2.44      6    #> 4  17.7  0.75   54    21.8 0.288  14.4   0.218 0.00667  1.45      6.33

text: Show difference keeping rows (propagating NA) omitting rows required inputs missing.
code: horm_demo_na <- horm_demo horm_demo_na$ft3[2] <- NA  keep_res <- hormone_markers(   data = horm_demo_na,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE )  omit_res <- hormone_markers(   data = horm_demo_na,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = FALSE )  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_res), omit_rows = nrow(omit_res)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 4 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 3

text: Enable range scanning cap --range inputs allowed bounds. extreme_action = "cap" trims inputs allowed ranges; warn error emit warnings abort instead.
code: horm_demo_ext <- horm_demo horm_demo_ext$aldo[1] <- 2000  # intentionally extreme  cap_res <- hormone_markers(   data = horm_demo_ext,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE )  cap_res[1, c("ARR", "FAI")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>     ARR   FAI #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  83.3  16.7

text: required inputs must mapped present; missing mappings/columns abort. na_action: keep/ignore retains rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error stops missingness. Extreme screening optional; enabled, choose handle flagged values extreme_action. Outputs numeric ratios; divisions zero/non-finite converted NA.
Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- data.frame(   total_testosterone = 10, SHBG = 2, LH = 8, FSH = 4,   estradiol = 100, progesterone = 50, free_T3 = 5, free_T4 = 10,   aldosterone = 20, renin = 4, insulin = 20, glucagon = 10,   GH = 2, IGF1 = 4, prolactin = 12, cortisol_0 = 200, cortisol_30 = 260 ) hormone_markers(df_v,   col_map = setNames(as.list(names(df_v)), names(df_v)),   verbose = TRUE ) #> hormone_markers(): skipping 1 ratio(s) with unmapped inputs: TSH_fT4 #> hormone_markers(): preparing inputs #> hormone_markers(): column map: total_testosterone -> 'total_testosterone', SHBG -> 'SHBG', LH -> 'LH', FSH -> 'FSH', estradiol -> 'estradiol', progesterone -> 'progesterone', free_T3 -> 'free_T3', free_T4 -> 'free_T4', aldosterone -> 'aldosterone', renin -> 'renin', insulin -> 'insulin', glucagon -> 'glucagon', GH -> 'GH', IGF1 -> 'IGF1', prolactin -> 'prolactin', cortisol_0 -> 'cortisol_0', cortisol_30 -> 'cortisol_30' #> hormone_markers(): results: FAI 1/1, LH_FSH 1/1, E2_P 1/1, E2_T 1/1, T3_T4 1/1, ARR 1/1, Ins_Glu 1/1, GH_IGF1 1/1, PRL_T 1/1, CAR_slope 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>     FAI LH_FSH  E2_P  E2_T T3_T4   ARR Ins_Glu GH_IGF1 PRL_T CAR_slope #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1   500      2     2    10   0.5     5       2     0.5   1.2         2 options(old_opt)

text: Keep units consistent across rows (e.g., cortisol nmol/L, aldosterone/renin matching units) make ratios meaningful. Use na_action = "omit" prefer complete-case ratios; keep/ignore want row counts preserved. Tighten extreme_rules lab reference ranges narrower broad defaults. Turn verbose = TRUE see coercion, missingness scan, extreme handling summaries.

title: Imputing missing values
text: Three imputation helpers preparing data marker calculations: impute_missing() — fast deterministic imputation (mean, median, zero, constant). Operates column column numeric columns least one NA. impute_mice() — multiple imputation via mice::mice(). Requires mice package. impute_missforest() — random-forest imputation via missForest::missForest(). Requires missForest package. Falls back mean imputation forest fails. three: (1) leave non-numeric columns untouched, (2) respect optional cols argument limit columns imputed, (3) warn high-missingness columns, (4) emit structured progress messages verbose = TRUE.

title: Imputing missing values
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   glucose  = c(5.2, NA, 6.0, 5.8, NA),   insulin  = c(60,  80, NA,  90, 110),   age      = c(45,  52, 38,  60,  NA),   sex      = c("M", "F", "M", "F", "M")   # non-numeric; never touched )

title: Imputing missing values
text: Replace NAs column--column column mean. sex untouched.
code: out_mean <- impute_missing(df, method = "mean") out_mean #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   glucose insulin   age sex   #>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1    5.2       60  45   M     #> 2    5.67      80  52   F     #> 3    6         85  38   M     #> 4    5.8       90  60   F     #> 5    5.67     110  48.8 M

title: Imputing missing values
code: # Median out_med <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "median")) # Zero out_zero <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "zero", cols = "glucose")) # Constant out_const <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "constant", constant = -1))  list(   median_glucose  = out_med$glucose,   zero_glucose    = out_zero$glucose,   const_glucose   = out_const$glucose ) #> $median_glucose #> [1] 5.2 5.8 6.0 5.8 5.8 #>  #> $zero_glucose #> [1] 5.2 0.0 6.0 5.8 0.0 #>  #> $const_glucose #> [1]  5.2 -1.0  6.0  5.8 -1.0

title: Imputing missing values
code: # Only impute insulin; leave glucose NA out_sel <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "mean", cols = "insulin")) list(glucose = out_sel$glucose, insulin = out_sel$insulin) #> $glucose #> [1] 5.2  NA 6.0 5.8  NA #>  #> $insulin #> [1]  60  80  85  90 110

title: Imputing missing values
text: Requires least two numeric columns missing values. Returns one completed dataset m imputations.
code: if (requireNamespace("mice", quietly = TRUE)) {   out_mice <- suppressWarnings(impute_mice(df, m = 5))   print(out_mice) } else {   message("mice package not installed; skipping example.") } #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   glucose insulin   age sex   #>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1     5.2      60    45 M     #> 2    NA        80    52 F     #> 3     6        80    38 M     #> 4     5.8      90    60 F     #> 5    NA       110    45 M

title: Imputing missing values
text: missForest errors (e.g., unique values), mean-imputation fallback applied automatically.
code: if (requireNamespace("missForest", quietly = TRUE)) {   out_rf <- suppressWarnings(impute_missforest(df, ntree = 50))   print(out_rf) } else {   message("missForest package not installed; skipping example.") } #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   glucose insulin   age sex   #>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1    5.2       60  45   M     #> 2    5.67      80  52   F     #> 3    6         85  38   M     #> 4    5.8       90  60   F     #> 5    5.67     110  48.8 M

title: Imputing missing values
text: three functions warn numeric column missing values na_warn_prop (default 0.2 = 20 %). warning carries class healthmarkers_impute_warn_high_missing.
code: df_hi <- tibble::tibble(x = c(1, NA, NA, NA), y = c(2, NA, 4, 5)) suppressWarnings(   tryCatch(     impute_missing(df_hi, method = "mean", verbose = FALSE),     warning = function(w) { message("High-missingness warning: ", conditionMessage(w)); NULL }   ) ) #> NULL

title: Imputing missing values
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface structured messages call: preparing inputs results summary. results message reports many values imputed per column: impute_missing(): results: imputed N values across M columns [col1=n1, col2=n2, ...]
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- tibble::tibble(a = c(1, NA, 3), b = c(4, 5, NA), c = c(NA, 2, 2)) suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df_v, method = "mean", verbose = TRUE)) #> impute_missing(): preparing inputs (3 rows, 3 column(s), method='mean') #> impute_missing(): results: imputed 3 values across 3 columns [a=1, b=1, c=1] #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>       a     b     c #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     1   4       2 #> 2     2   5       2 #> 3     3   4.5     2  options(old_opt)

title: Imputing missing values
text: Run imputation calling marker functions data-level NA handling insufficient (e.g., need complete matrix PCA multivariate ML upstream). Prefer impute_missing(method = "median") skewed biomarker distributions. impute_mice() impute_missforest() provide MAR-robust estimates cost speed; use final analysis datasets, exploratory runs. Non-numeric columns (factors, characters) always passed unchanged.

Compute simplified inflammatory age proxy (iAge) weighted sum CRP, IL6, TNFa. Supports NA policies, high-missingness warnings, optional extreme scanning (warn/cap/NA/error). Units assumed: CRP mg/L; IL6, TNFa pg/mL (internal conversions).
Synthetic data one missing value show NA handling.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   CRP  = c(1.2, 3.5, NA),  # mg/L   IL6  = c(2.0, 4.1, 1.5), # pg/mL   TNFa = c(1.0, 1.8, 0.9)  # pg/mL )

text: Map marker column names; three mandatory.
code: col_map <- list(CRP = "CRP", IL6 = "IL6", TNFa = "TNFa")

text: Default behavior (na_action = "omit"/ignore/warn): NAs ignored weighted sum (treated 0 contribution), preserving row count.
code: ia_default <- iAge(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  ia_default #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1 1.40  #> 2 3.12  #> 3 0.801

text: na_action = "keep" returns NA required marker missing row. na_action = "error" stops missing required inputs.
code: ia_keep <- iAge(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE )  ia_keep #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  1.40 #> 2  3.12 #> 3 NA

text: Scan --range inputs cap allowed bounds. extreme_action = "warn" warn; error aborts; NA blanks flagged values computing.
code: df_ext <- df df_ext$CRP[2] <- 500  # extreme on purpose  ia_cap <- iAge(   data = df_ext,   col_map = col_map,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE )  ia_cap #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>     iAge #>    <dbl> #> 1   1.40 #> 2 101.   #> 3  NA

text: Default weights: CRP 0.33, IL6 0.33, TNFa 0.34 (sum 1). Named weights reordered CRP/IL6/TNFa. cohorts one marker informative, adjust weights (ensure sum 1):
code: iAge(df, col_map = col_map, weights = c(CRP = 0.5, IL6 = 0.25, TNFa = 0.25)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  1.35 #> 2  3.22 #> 3 NA

text: three markers must present numeric; coercion numeric error NAs introduced. omit/ignore/warn: missing markers contribute 0 sum; rows preserved. keep: missing required marker yields NA row. error: abort required marker missing/NA. Extreme scan uses broad defaults (CRP 0–300, IL6 0–1000, TNFa 0–2000); adjust via extreme_rules lab ranges. Output: one-column tibble iAge; rows match input unless subset beforehand.
Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- tibble::tibble(CRP = 1.2, IL6 = 2.0, TNFa = 1.0) iAge(df_v, col_map = list(CRP = "CRP", IL6 = "IL6", TNFa = "TNFa"), verbose = TRUE) #> iAge(): preparing inputs #> iAge(): column map: CRP -> 'CRP', IL6 -> 'IL6', TNFa -> 'TNFa' #> iAge(): results: iAge 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  1.40 options(old_opt)

text: Keep units consistent (CRP mg/L; IL6/TNFa pg/mL). Convert calling needed. Prefer na_action = "keep" want see rows incomplete; use omit want continuity score despite single missing markers. Tighten extreme_rules cleaner QA; use verbose = TRUE setup see mapping warnings. Impute upstream (e.g., impute_missing()) prefer -weight missing markers zero.

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Compute inflammatory ratios/panels. Panels: - Classic: NLR, PLR, LMR, dNLR, SII, SIRI, AISI, CRP_category. - Eosinophil: NLR, PLR, LMR, NER, SII, SIRI, PIV, CLR, CAR, PCR, mGPS, ESR passthrough. - : union classic + eos metrics. Inputs coerced numeric; non-finite become NA. Zero denominators warn. Optional extreme handling can warn/cap/blank/error.

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Synthetic counts run panels.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   neutrophils = c(4, 2, 3.5),   lymphocytes = c(2, 1.0, 1.5),   monocytes = c(0.5, 0.3, 0.4),   platelets = c(200, 150, 180),   WBC = c(7, 4.5, 6),   CRP = c(2.5, 0.8, 12),   albumin = c(40, 42, 33),   eosinophils = c(0.2, 0.1, 0.15),   ESR = c(12, 15, 18) )

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Classic: neutrophils, lymphocytes (monocytes/platelets/WBC/CRP improve coverage). Eos: classic keys plus eosinophils, CRP, albumin (CLR/CAR/mGPS); ESR optional passthrough.
code: cm <- list(   neutrophils = "neutrophils",   lymphocytes = "lymphocytes",   monocytes = "monocytes",   platelets = "platelets",   WBC = "WBC",   CRP = "CRP",   albumin = "albumin",   eosinophils = "eosinophils",   ESR = "ESR" )

title: Inflammatory markers
code: classic_out <- inflammatory_markers(   data = df,   col_map = cm,   panel = "classic",   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  classic_out #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR  dNLR   SII  SIRI  AISI CRP_category #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <ord>        #> 1  2      100  4     1.33   400 1       200 moderate     #> 2  2      150  3.33  0.8    300 0.6      90 low          #> 3  2.33   120  3.75  1.4    420 0.933   168 high

title: Inflammatory markers
text: extreme_action = "cap" trims --range inputs; warn warns; error aborts; NA blanks flagged values.
code: df_ext <- df df_ext$CRP[3] <- 400  # intentional extreme  eos_out <- inflammatory_markers(   data = df_ext,   col_map = cm,   panel = "eos",   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE )  eos_out #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR   NER   SII  SIRI   PIV    CLR    CAR   PCR  mGPS   ESR #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl> #> 1  2      100  4      4     400 1       200   1.25 0.0625  80       0    12 #> 2  2      150  3.33   2     300 0.6      90   0.8  0.0190 188.      0    15 #> 3  2.33   120  3.75   3.5   420 0.933   168 200    9.09     0.6     2    18

title: Inflammatory markers
text: na_action = "keep" preserves rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts required input missing/non-finite.
code: df_na <- df df_na$lymphocytes[2] <- NA  keep_out <- inflammatory_markers(df_na, cm, panel = "classic", na_action = "keep") omit_out <- inflammatory_markers(df_na, cm, panel = "classic", na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 3 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 2

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Provide required keys per panel; missing mappings abort. Inputs coerced numeric; non-finite become NA; zero denominators warn. Extreme scan uses broad defaults blood counts/CRP/albumin; adjust via extreme_action built-ranges. Panel selection: auto uses eos eosinophils mapping present; returns union.

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- tibble::tibble(neutrophils = 4, lymphocytes = 2, monocytes = 0.5, platelets = 200, CRP = 2.5) cm_v <- list(neutrophils = "neutrophils", lymphocytes = "lymphocytes",              monocytes = "monocytes", platelets = "platelets", CRP = "CRP") inflammatory_markers(df_v, cm_v, panel = "classic", verbose = TRUE) #> inflammatory_markers(): preparing inputs #> inflammatory_markers(): column map: neutrophils -> 'neutrophils', lymphocytes -> 'lymphocytes' #> inflammatory_markers(): results: NLR 1/1, PLR 1/1, LMR 1/1, dNLR 0/1, SII 1/1, SIRI 1/1, AISI 1/1, CRP_category 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR  dNLR   SII  SIRI  AISI CRP_category #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <ord>        #> 1     2   100     4    NA   400     1   200 moderate options(old_opt)

title: Inflammatory markers
text: lymphocyte/eosinophil counts can zero, consider na_action = "keep" avoid dropping rows; zeros still trigger divide--zero warnings. Tighten ranges using capping match lab references. data use different units (e.g., CRP mg/dL), convert calling keep ratios interpretable.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: 4-variable Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE) 2- 5-year ESKD risk. Includes NA policies, high-missingness warnings, optional extreme scanning warn/cap/NA/error behaviors, verbose summaries. Units: age (years), sex coded 1=male/2=female, eGFR (mL/min/1.73 m^2), UACR (mg/g); unit conversion performed.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   age  = c(65, 72, 80),   sex  = c(1, 2, 1),   eGFR = c(45, 22, 10),   UACR = c(300, 1200, 8000) )

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
code: col_map <- list(age = "age", sex = "sex", eGFR = "eGFR", UACR = "UACR")

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: Default na_action = "keep": missing inputs propagate NA risks; extreme checks.
code: kfre_default <- kidney_failure_risk(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  kfre_default #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr #>      <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1    0.329    0.523 #> 2    0.536    0.759 #> 3    0.986    1.000

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: Enable range checks cap --range inputs. extreme_action = "warn" warns; error aborts; NA blanks flagged values calculation.
code: kfre_cap <- kidney_failure_risk(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "warn",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE )  kfre_cap #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr #>      <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1    0.329    0.523 #> 2    0.536    0.759 #> 3    0.986    1.000

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: keep propagates NA risks; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error stops missingness.
code: df_na <- df df_na$eGFR[2] <- NA  keep_out <- kidney_failure_risk(df_na, col_map, na_action = "keep") omit_out <- kidney_failure_risk(df_na, col_map, na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 3 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 2

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: four predictors must mapped present; sex must 1/2. Inputs must numeric; non-finite become NA may propagate depending na_action. Extreme defaults: age 18–120, eGFR 1–200, UACR 0.1–10000 mg/g. Adjust via extreme_rules needed. Output: tibble KFRE_2yr, KFRE_5yr; row count follows na_action row filtering apply.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") kidney_failure_risk(   data    = df,   col_map = col_map,   verbose = TRUE ) #> kidney_failure_risk(): preparing inputs #> kidney_failure_risk(): column map: age -> 'age', sex -> 'sex', eGFR -> 'eGFR', UACR -> 'UACR' #> kidney_failure_risk(): results: KFRE_2yr 3/3, KFRE_5yr 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr #>      <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1    0.329    0.523 #> 2    0.536    0.759 #> 3    0.986    1.000 options(old_opt)

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: Check units running (e.g., UACR mg/g vs mg/mmol); convert upstream needed. Use na_action = "error" strict QA; warn surface high missingness retaining rows. Tighten extreme bounds cohort; keep verbose = TRUE setting .

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Compute kynurenine/tryptophan ratio (KTR), marker IDO activity immune activation. Supports NA policies, optional extreme screening (warn/cap/NA/error), verbose progress. Inputs must already Kyn (nmol/L) Trp (µmol/L); unit conversion.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   Kyn_nM = c(1800, 2500, 3200),   Trp_uM = c(50, 42, 12) )

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
code: col_map <- list(kynurenine = "Kyn_nM", tryptophan = "Trp_uM")

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Default na_action = "keep": ratios NA inputs missing; extreme scan.
code: ktr <- kyn_trp_ratio(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  ktr #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   kyn_trp_ratio #>           <dbl> #> 1          36   #> 2          59.5 #> 3         267.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Scan cap default ranges. extreme_action = "warn" warns; error aborts; NA blanks flagged values.
code: df_ext <- df df_ext$Kyn_nM[2] <- 50000  # extreme  ktr_cap <- kyn_trp_ratio(   data = df_ext,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "warn",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE )  ktr_cap #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   kyn_trp_ratio #>           <dbl> #> 1           36  #> 2          476. #> 3          267.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA ratios; omit drops rows missing inputs; error stops missingness.
code: df_na <- df df_na$Trp_uM[3] <- NA  keep_out <- kyn_trp_ratio(df_na, col_map, na_action = "keep") omit_out <- kyn_trp_ratio(df_na, col_map, na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 3 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 2

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: markers must mapped present; non-numeric inputs coerced warnings NAs introduced. Trp must positive; nonpositive values set NA warning. Extreme defaults: Kyn 100–20000 nmol/L, Trp 10–150 µmol/L, ratio 0–200; adjust extreme_rules. Output: one-column tibble kyn_trp_ratio; row count follows na_action omit rows.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") kyn_trp_ratio(   data    = df,   col_map = col_map,   verbose = TRUE ) #> kyn_trp_ratio(): preparing inputs #> kyn_trp_ratio(): column map: kynurenine -> 'Kyn_nM', tryptophan -> 'Trp_uM' #> kyn_trp_ratio(): results: kyn_trp_ratio 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   kyn_trp_ratio #>           <dbl> #> 1          36   #> 2          59.5 #> 3         267. options(old_opt)

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Re-check units ratios high (>100); Kyn nmol/L Trp µmol/L. Use extreme_action = "cap" sparingly adjust ranges cohort. modeling, consider scaling/transforming KTR separately computation.

title: lipid_markers
text: Compute lipid ratios composite indices (non-HDL-C, remnant-C, TC/HDL, TG/HDL, LDL/HDL, ApoB/ApoA1, VAI, LAP, TyG-BMI) optional LDL estimation NA/extreme handling.

title: lipid_markers
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  sim <- readRDS(system.file("extdata/simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers")) lipids <- sim |>   dplyr::slice_head(n = 30) |>   dplyr::mutate(glucose = G0) |>   dplyr::select(TC, HDL_c, TG, LDL_c, ApoB, ApoA1, waist, BMI, glucose)

title: lipid_markers
text: Required: TC, HDL_c, TG (mmol/L). Optional present/mapped: LDL_c (mmol/L), ApoB, ApoA1, waist (cm), BMI (kg/m^2), glucose (mmol/L; needed TyG-BMI). Use col_map rename; numeric-like inputs coerced non-finite set NA policies.

title: lipid_markers
code: lm_out <- lipid_markers(   data = lipids,   col_map = list(     TC = "TC", HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG", LDL_c = "LDL_c",     ApoB = "ApoB", ApoA1 = "ApoA1", waist = "waist", BMI = "BMI", glucose = "glucose"   ),   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE ) lm_out |> dplyr::slice_head(n = 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   non_HDL_c remnant_c ratio_TC_HDL ratio_TG_HDL ratio_LDL_HDL ApoB_ApoA1 VAI_Men #>       <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      2.81    -0.171         2.83        0.693          1.94      0.473   0.689 #> 2      4.41     1.81          3.95        1.49           1.74      0.637   2.08  #> 3      3.31     0.960         3.70        0.952          1.92      0.392   1.28  #> 4      3.72    -0.198         4.92        2.04           4.12      0.804   2.67  #> 5      4.03     0.789         4.09        1.42           2.48      0.516   2.08  #> # ℹ 4 more variables: VAI_Women <dbl>, LAP_Men <dbl>, LAP_Women <dbl>, #> #   TyG_BMI <dbl>

title: lipid_markers
text: na_action: keep/ignore retain rows missing inputs (derived outputs become NA); omit drops ; error aborts; warn retains warnings. na_warn_prop flags high missingness warning. check_extreme extreme_action (warn, cap, NA, error, ignore) scans broad defaults (TC/HDL/TG/LDL 0-50 mmol/L; ApoB/ApoA1 0-10; waist 30-250 cm; BMI 10-80 kg/m^2; glucose 0-50 mmol/L). Override via extreme_rules. LDL_c absent, LDL estimated via Friedewald (TC - HDL - TG/5) TG available.

title: lipid_markers
text: Outputs: non_HDL_c, remnant_c, ratio_TC_HDL, ratio_TG_HDL, ratio_LDL_HDL, ApoB_ApoA1 (needs ApoB & ApoA1), VAI_Men/Women (needs waist & BMI), LAP_Men/Women (needs waist), TyG_BMI (needs glucose & BMI). Units follow inputs; TyG-BMI converts TG/glucose mg/dL internally.

title: lipid_markers
text: Provide waist BMI enable VAI/LAP; supply glucose BMI enable TyG-BMI. Use check_extreme = TRUE cap bound implausible lipid anthropometric values. Set na_action = "omit" need complete-case derived lipid indices.

title: lipid_markers
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") lipid_markers(   data    = dplyr::slice_head(lipids, n = 5),   col_map = list(TC = "TC", HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG",                  LDL_c = "LDL_c", ApoB = "ApoB", ApoA1 = "ApoA1",                  waist = "waist", BMI = "BMI"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> lipid_markers(): preparing inputs #> lipid_markers(): column map: TC -> 'TC', HDL_c -> 'HDL_c', TG -> 'TG' #> lipid_markers(): results: non_HDL_c 5/5, remnant_c 5/5, ratio_TC_HDL 5/5, ratio_TG_HDL 5/5, ratio_LDL_HDL 5/5, ApoB_ApoA1 5/5, VAI_Men 5/5, VAI_Women 5/5, LAP_Men 5/5, LAP_Women 5/5, TyG_BMI 5/5 #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   non_HDL_c remnant_c ratio_TC_HDL ratio_TG_HDL ratio_LDL_HDL ApoB_ApoA1 VAI_Men #>       <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      2.81    -0.171         2.83        0.693          1.94      0.473   0.689 #> 2      4.41     1.81          3.95        1.49           1.74      0.637   2.08  #> 3      3.31     0.960         3.70        0.952          1.92      0.392   1.28  #> 4      3.72    -0.198         4.92        2.04           4.12      0.804   2.67  #> 5      4.03     0.789         4.09        1.42           2.48      0.516   2.08  #> # ℹ 4 more variables: VAI_Women <dbl>, LAP_Men <dbl>, LAP_Women <dbl>, #> #   TyG_BMI <dbl> options(old_opt)

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: liver_fat_markers() computes two non-invasive surrogate indexes hepatic steatosis: HSI (Hepatic Steatosis Index): 8 × (ALT/AST) + BMI + 2 (female) + 2 (diabetes) NAFLD-LFS (NAFLD Liver Fat Score): requires metabolic syndrome status fasting insulin indexes returned tibble columns HSI NAFLD_LFS.

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: ALT, AST, BMI strictly required. Without sex/diabetes female diabetes terms default 0.
code: df <- data.frame(ALT = c(30, 45, 60), AST = c(20, 30, 25), BMI = c(24, 28, 33))  liver_fat_markers(   df,   col_map = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI") ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  36          NA #> 2  40          NA #> 3  52.2        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: Providing sex, diabetes, insulin enables full NAFLD-LFS formula.
code: df2 <- data.frame(   ALT      = c(30, 45, 60),   AST      = c(20, 30, 25),   BMI      = c(24, 28, 33),   sex      = c("female", "male", "female"),   diabetes = c(FALSE, TRUE, FALSE),   insulin  = c(10, 22, 15) )  liver_fat_markers(   df2,   col_map = list(     ALT      = "ALT",     AST      = "AST",     BMI      = "BMI",     sex      = "sex",     diabetes = "diabetes",     insulin  = "insulin"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  38          NA #> 2  42          NA #> 3  54.2        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: MetS available directly, function derives via NCEP-ATP III criteria waist, TG, HDL_c, blood pressure column, glucose provided.
code: df3 <- data.frame(   ALT = 55, AST = 30, BMI = 31,   sex = "female", diabetes = FALSE, insulin = 18,   waist = 92, TG = 1.9, HDL_c = 1.1, bp_sys = 135, glucose = 6.0 )  liver_fat_markers(   df3,   col_map = list(     ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI",     sex = "sex", diabetes = "diabetes", insulin = "insulin",     waist = "waist", TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c",     bp_sys = "bp_sys", glucose = "glucose"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  47.7      1.68

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: Use na_action control rows missing required values treated.
code: df_na <- data.frame(ALT = c(30, NA, 50), AST = c(20, 25, 30), BMI = c(25, 28, 31))  # omit: return only complete rows liver_fat_markers(   df_na,   col_map  = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI"),   na_action = "omit" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  37          NA #> 2  44.3        NA  # keep: return all rows, NA for incomplete ones liver_fat_markers(   df_na,   col_map  = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI"),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  37          NA #> 2  NA          NA #> 3  44.3        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: Enable step--step messages trace exactly columns mapped many rows computed successfully.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- data.frame(ALT = c(30, 45), AST = c(20, 30), BMI = c(24, 28),                    sex = c("male", "female"), diabetes = c(FALSE, TRUE),                    insulin = c(10, 22))  liver_fat_markers(   df_v,   col_map = list(     ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI",     sex = "sex", diabetes = "diabetes", insulin = "insulin"   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> liver_fat_markers(): preparing inputs #> liver_fat_markers(): column map: ALT -> 'ALT', AST -> 'AST', BMI -> 'BMI' #> liver_fat_markers(): results: HSI 2/2, NAFLD_LFS 0/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    36        NA #> 2    44        NA  options(old_opt)

title: Liver Markers
text: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) configurable handling missing data extreme values.

title: Liver Markers
text: routine liver labs anthropometry (BMI, waist, TG, GGT, AST, ALT, platelets, albumin, bilirubin, creatinine, diabetes flag) want standard steatosis/fibrosis scores. need built-NA policy controls, numeric coercion, optional extreme-value scanning/capping. want MELD-XI, ALBI, common non-invasive fibrosis indices one call screening modeling.

title: Liver Markers
text: data: data.frame/tibble required inputs. col_map: named list mapping required keys columns (units assumed, conversion): BMI (kg/m^2), waist (cm), TG (mg/dL), GGT (U/L), age (years), AST (U/L), ALT (U/L), platelets (10^9/L), albumin (g/L), diabetes (0/1 logical), bilirubin (mg/dL), creatinine (mg/dL). na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts; ignore treated keep; warn keeps issues high-missingness warnings via na_warn_prop. check_extreme: set TRUE scan inputs extreme_action (warn, cap, error, ignore, NA) using default bounds unless extreme_rules overrides . Defaults: BMI 10-70; waist 40-200 cm; TG 10-1500 mg/dL; GGT 1-2000 U/L; age 18-120; AST/ALT 1-5000; platelets 10-1000 (10^9/L); albumin 15-60 g/L; bilirubin 0.1-40 mg/dL; creatinine 0.2-20 mg/dL. Numeric coercion: mapped columns (except diabetes) coerced numeric; non-finite values become NA policies. diabetes coerced integer 0/1 warning values outside {0,1,TRUE,FALSE}.

title: Liver Markers
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  liver <- tibble::tibble(   BMI           = c(24, 30, 33),   waist         = c(80, 100, 110),   triglycerides = c(150, 260, 90),   # mg/dL   GGT           = c(30, 55, 18),   age           = c(45, 58, 67),   AST           = c(25, 60, 42),   ALT           = c(20, 45, 28),   platelets     = c(250, 190, 210),  # 10^9/L   albumin       = c(42, 38, 40),     # g/L   diabetes      = c(FALSE, TRUE, 1),   bilirubin     = c(1.0, 0.9, 1.3),  # mg/dL   creatinine    = c(0.9, 1.1, 1.0)   # mg/dL )  liver_markers(   data = liver,   col_map = list(     BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist", TG = "triglycerides", GGT = "GGT", age = "age",     AST = "AST", ALT = "ALT", platelets = "platelets", albumin = "albumin", diabetes = "diabetes",     bilirubin = "bilirubin", creatinine = "creatinine"   ),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>     FLI   NFS  APRI  FIB4  BARD  ALBI MELD_XI #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  27.9 -27.5 0.25   1.01     1 -2.76    8.20 #> 2  87.0 -21.8 0.789  2.73     3 -2.45   10.0  #> 3  73.8 -22.6 0.5    2.53     3 -2.52   10.8

title: Liver Markers
text: Missing values: keep/ignore propagate NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; warn keeps logs high-missingness column; error aborts missing required inputs. Extreme values: check_extreme = TRUE, scan defaults (extreme_rules); choose extreme_action = "warn"|"cap"|"error"|"ignore"|"NA" warn, cap -range, abort, skip, blank-flagged inputs. Non-finite values become NA checks.

title: Liver Markers
text: Returns tibble : FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB4, BARD, ALBI, MELD_XI. Bilirubin converted umol/L internally ALBI; inputs use provided units. Zero nonpositive platelets, TG, GGT, bilirubin, creatinine, negative ALT trigger warnings yield NA affected indices.

title: Liver Markers
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") liver_markers(   data = liver,   col_map = list(     BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist", TG = "triglycerides", GGT = "GGT", age = "age",     AST = "AST", ALT = "ALT", platelets = "platelets", albumin = "albumin", diabetes = "diabetes",     bilirubin = "bilirubin", creatinine = "creatinine"   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> liver_markers(): preparing inputs #> liver_markers(): column map: BMI -> 'BMI', waist -> 'waist', TG -> 'triglycerides', GGT -> 'GGT', age -> 'age', AST -> 'AST', ALT -> 'ALT', platelets -> 'platelets', albumin -> 'albumin', diabetes -> 'diabetes', bilirubin -> 'bilirubin', creatinine -> 'creatinine' #> liver_markers(): results: FLI 3/3, NFS 3/3, APRI 3/3, FIB4 3/3, BARD 3/3, ALBI 3/3, MELD_XI 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>     FLI   NFS  APRI  FIB4  BARD  ALBI MELD_XI #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  27.9 -27.5 0.25   1.01     1 -2.76    8.20 #> 2  87.0 -21.8 0.789  2.73     3 -2.45   10.0  #> 3  73.8 -22.6 0.5    2.53     3 -2.52   10.8 options(old_opt)

title: Liver Markers
text: Confirm triglycerides mg/dL (mmol/L) albumin g/L. Enable check_extreme = TRUE extreme_action = "cap" bound implausible values scoring; use warn QA see flags. modeling-ready outputs, prefer na_action = "omit"; use keep warn inspecting data quality.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Create pediatric-friendly binary risk flags (dyslipidemia, insulin resistance, hyperglycemia, hypertension) routine labs BP z-scores configurable NA extreme handling.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: lipid panel, glucose/HbA1c, BP z-scores, age, z-scored HOMA-IR want quick binary risk features. need NA policies, high-missingness warnings, optional extreme-value screening/capping built . working pediatrics/adolescents triglyceride thresholds differ age BP already standardized.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: data: data.frame/tibble required numeric inputs. col_map: named list mapping required keys (defaults names): chol_total, chol_ldl, chol_hdl, triglycerides, age_year, z_HOMA, glucose, HbA1c, bp_sys_z, bp_dia_z. Units assumed: lipids mmol/L; glucose mmol/L; HbA1c mmol/mol (IFCC); BP inputs already z-scores; age years; z_HOMA z-scored HOMA-IR. na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts; ignore acts like keep; warn acts like keep plus high-missingness warnings via na_warn_prop (default 0.2). check_extreme: set TRUE scan using extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore/NA) default bounds unless overridden extreme_rules (defaults: chol_total 0.5–20; chol_ldl 0.1–15; chol_hdl 0.1–5; triglycerides 0.1–30; age_year 0–120; z_HOMA -5–5; glucose 2–40; HbA1c 15–200; bp_sys_z/bp_dia_z -5–5).

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  peds <- tibble::tibble(   chol_total = c(5.4, 4.8),   chol_ldl   = c(3.6, 2.9),   chol_hdl   = c(0.9, 1.2),   triglycerides = c(1.6, 1.0),   age_year   = c(15, 9),   z_HOMA     = c(1.5, 0.8),   glucose    = c(5.8, 5.1),   HbA1c      = c(40, 35),   bp_sys_z   = c(1.2, 1.8),   bp_dia_z   = c(0.5, 1.7) )  metabolic_risk_features(   data = peds,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",     age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",     bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             1

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: extreme <- peds extreme$triglycerides[1] <- 40  # high extreme$bp_sys_z[2] <- 9        # out of range  metabolic_risk_features(   data = extreme,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",     age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",     bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   na_action = "warn",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             1

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: missing <- peds missing$glucose[2] <- NA  metabolic_risk_features(   data = missing,   col_map = list(chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",                  age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",                  bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"),   na_action = "omit" ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0  metabolic_risk_features(   data = missing,   col_map = list(chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",                  age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",                  bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"),   na_action = "warn" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  NA            1

what: Expanded example: cap extremes, drop incomplete rows
title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: ext_cap <- peds ext_cap$triglycerides[1] <- 40   # extreme high ext_cap$bp_sys_z[2] <- 9         # extreme high ext_cap$HbA1c[2] <- NA           # missing HbA1c to trigger drop  out_cap <- metabolic_risk_features(   data = ext_cap,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",     age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",     bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE )  list(rows_returned = nrow(out_cap), flags = out_cap) #> $rows_returned #> [1] 1 #>  #> $flags #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Missingness: keep/ignore propagate NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; warn logs high-missingness; error aborts missing required inputs. Extreme values: check_extreme = TRUE, scan defaults extreme_rules; choose extreme_action warn, cap, NA, ignore, error. Outputs: tibble factor columns dyslipidemia, insulin_resistance, hyperglycemia, hypertension (levels 0/1). Age-based TG cutoffs: >1.1 mmol/L (0–9 y) >1.5 mmol/L (10–19 y) dyslipidemia; BP z > 1.64 hypertension; z_HOMA > 1.28 insulin resistance; glucose 5.6–6.9 HbA1c 39–47 hyperglycemia.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") metabolic_risk_features(   data = peds,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl",     triglycerides = "triglycerides", age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA",     glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c", bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> metabolic_risk_features(): preparing inputs #> metabolic_risk_features(): column map: chol_total -> 'chol_total', chol_ldl -> 'chol_ldl', chol_hdl -> 'chol_hdl', triglycerides -> 'triglycerides', age_year -> 'age_year', z_HOMA -> 'z_HOMA', glucose -> 'glucose', HbA1c -> 'HbA1c', bp_sys_z -> 'bp_sys_z', bp_dia_z -> 'bp_dia_z' #> metabolic_risk_features(): results: dyslipidemia 2/2, insulin_resistance 2/2, hyperglycemia 2/2, hypertension 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             1 options(old_opt)

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Keep lipids/glucose mmol/L HbA1c mmol/mol; BP inputs must already z-scores. Tighten extreme_rules cohort using extreme_action = "cap" "error". Use na_action = "omit" analysis-ready flags; keep/warn QA inspect missingness. Watch HbA1c reported % (values <=14 suggest percent) lipids/glucose mg/dL (appear ~×38.7 ×18 higher); standardize units calling. BP inputs must already z-scores; large magnitude (|z| > 5) usually indicates scaling issues.

title: MetSS
text: Compute Metabolic Syndrome Severity Score (MetSSS) using sex- race-specific coefficients, configurable NA/extreme handling plausibility diagnostics.

title: MetSS
text: waist, blood pressure, fasting lipids/glucose, sex, race want continuous MetS severity score. need built-NA policies, high-missingness warnings, optional extreme-value scanning/capping. okay single parameter key (race/sex) applied rows—matching original implementation.

title: MetSS
text: data: data.frame/tibble waist (cm), bp_sys/bp_dia (mmHg), TG/HDL_c/glucose (mmol/L), sex (1=male, 2=female), race {NHW, NHB, HW, HA} accepted synonyms. params: named list keyed RACE_SEX (e.g., NHW_M), intercept component vectors (waist, TG, HDL, glucose, MAP) containing mean, sd, coef. na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts; ignore/warn act like keep (warn also adds high-missingness warnings via na_warn_prop). check_extreme: set TRUE scan inputs; extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore/NA) controls handling. Defaults (cm, mmHg, mmol/L): waist 40–200; bp_sys 60–300; bp_dia 30–200; TG 0–20; HDL_c 0–5; glucose 0–40. Override extreme_rules. diagnostics: TRUE (default), warns implausible ranges invalid sex/race encodings. verbose: log progress summary.

title: MetSS
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  mets <- tibble::tibble(   waist = c(95, 108),   bp_sys = c(120, 138),   bp_dia = c(80, 86),   TG = c(1.5, 2.1),   HDL_c = c(1.2, 0.9),   glucose = c(5.5, 6.2),   sex = c(1, 1),   race = c("NHW", "NHW") )  metss(   data = mets,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1  -2.43 #> 2   1.01

title: MetSS
code: extreme <- mets extreme$TG[2] <- 25       # high TG extreme$bp_sys[1] <- 400  # out of range  metss(   data = extreme,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "warn",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   diagnostics = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1  0.109 #> 2 21.9

title: MetSS
code: missing <- mets missing$HDL_c[1] <- NA  metss(   data = missing,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "omit" ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1   1.01  metss(   data = missing,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "warn" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1  NA    #> 2   1.01

title: MetSS
text: Returns tibble MetSSS using single race/sex key derived first row (warns multiple keys present). na_action = "omit" drops rows missing required inputs; otherwise missing inputs propagate NA MetSSS. Extreme handling can cap/NA/warn/error; diagnostics run independently can still warn capping.

title: MetSS
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") metss(   data = mets,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> metss(): preparing inputs #> metss(): column map: waist -> 'waist', bp_sys -> 'bp_sys', bp_dia -> 'bp_dia', TG -> 'TG', HDL_c -> 'HDL_c', glucose -> 'glucose', sex -> 'sex', race -> 'race' #> metss(): results: MetSSS 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1  -2.43 #> 2   1.01 options(old_opt)

title: MetSS
text: Confirm sex encoded 1/2 race matches params key; first-row key used. Keep units consistent (waist cm, BP mmHg, lipids/glucose mmol/L); unit conversion occurs . Tighten extreme_rules cohort using extreme_action = "cap" "error". Leave diagnostics = TRUE QA catch implausible ranges; silence needed tests.

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Incorporate neurofilament light chain (NfL) concentration measurement analysis pipeline. NfL biomarker neuroaxonal injury; elevated plasma CSF levels indicate neurodegeneration typically increase age neurological disease. function validates passes input values (assumed plasma pg/mL) optional NA handling extreme-value scanning, returning tidy tibble ready downstream analysis.

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   NfL = c(8.5, 14.2, 22.1, NA, 35.0) )

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: required key nfl.
code: cm <- list(nfl = "NfL")

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
code: out <- nfl_marker(   data = df,   col_map = cm,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  out #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   nfl_value #>       <dbl> #> 1       8.5 #> 2      14.2 #> 3      22.1 #> 4      NA   #> 5      35

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: na_action = "keep" preserves rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts required input missing; warn keeps rows emits warning.
code: df_na <- tibble::tibble(NfL = c(8.5, NA, 22.1))  keep_out <- nfl_marker(df_na, cm, na_action = "keep") omit_out <- nfl_marker(df_na, cm, na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 3 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 2

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: check_extreme = TRUE scans values broad default range [0, 1e6] pg/mL.extreme_action controls response: "warn" emits warning without altering values, "cap" clips range boundary, "NA" blanks flagged values, "error" aborts. Custom range override:
code: df_ext <- tibble::tibble(NfL = c(8.5, -5, 22.1))  # negative value is out of range  nfl_marker(   data = df_ext,   col_map = cm,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "warn" ) #> Warning: nfl_marker(): negative NfL values detected; check input data. #> Warning: nfl_marker(): detected 1 extreme input values (not altered). #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   nfl_value #>       <dbl> #> 1       8.5 #> 2      -5   #> 3      22.1 nfl_marker(   data = tibble::tibble(NfL = c(8.5, 5000, 22.1)),   col_map = cm,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   extreme_rules = list(nfl = c(0, 1000)) ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   nfl_value #>       <dbl> #> 1       8.5 #> 2    1000   #> 3      22.1

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") nfl_marker(tibble::tibble(NfL = c(8.5, 14.2, 22.1)), cm, verbose = TRUE) #> nfl_marker(): preparing inputs #> nfl_marker(): column map: nfl -> 'NfL' #> nfl_marker(): results: nfl_value 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   nfl_value #>       <dbl> #> 1       8.5 #> 2      14.2 #> 3      22.1 options(old_opt)

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Units preserved -; ensure values unit (e.g., pg/mL) calling. Age-adjusted reference ranges applied function; use downstream tools stratified z-scores age normalisation. batch processing imputed datasets, combine impute_missing() upstream handle systematic missingness running nfl_marker().

title: Nutrient Markers
text: Compute nutrient-related ratios/products (FerritinTS, AGR, Omega3Index, Mg_Cr_Ratio, GlycatedAlbuminPct, UA_Cr_Ratio, BUN_Cr_Ratio, Ca_x_Phosphate, AnionGap, Tyr_Phe_Ratio) NA/extreme handling safe divisions; unit conversion.

title: Nutrient Markers
text: routine nutrient labs (iron, protein, omega-3, renal, electrolytes, amino acids) want quick derived ratios. need built-NA policies, high-missingness diagnostics, optional extreme-value scanning/capping. can supply inputs expected units accept missing inputs return NA dependent markers.

title: Nutrient Markers
text: data: data frame/tibble containing subset recognized inputs. col_map: optional named list mapping keys (defaults identity): ferritin, transferrin_sat, albumin, total_protein, EPA, DHA, Mg, creatinine, glycated_albumin, uric_acid, BUN, phosphate, calcium, Na, K, Cl, HCO3, Tyr, Phe. Unrecognized keys ignored. Non-numeric values coerced warnings; non-finite set NA. na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows NA used inputs; error aborts used inputs contain NA. na_warn_prop: threshold high-missingness diagnostics (default 0.2; shown verbose/debug). check_extreme: set TRUE scan inputs; extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore/NA) controls handling. Defaults broad (e.g., ferritin 0–2000 ng/mL; transferrin_sat 0–100%; albumin 10–60 g/L; total_protein 40–100 g/L; EPA/DHA 0–20%; Mg 0.2–3 mmol/L; creatinine 20–2000 umol/L; glycated_albumin 0–60 g/L; uric_acid 50–1000 umol/L; BUN 1–150 mg/dL; phosphate 0.1–5 mmol/L; calcium 0.5–4 mmol/L; Na 100–200 mmol/L; K 2–8 mmol/L; Cl 70–130 mmol/L; HCO3 5–45 mmol/L; Tyr 10–300 umol/L; Phe 20–300 umol/L). verbose: optional progress summary logging.

title: Nutrient Markers
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   ferritin = c(50, 100), transferrin_sat = c(30, 50),   albumin = c(45, 40), total_protein = c(70, 75),   EPA = c(2.0, 2.5), DHA = c(4.0, 4.5),   Mg = c(0.85, 0.90), creatinine = c(80, 90),   glycated_albumin = c(12, 14), uric_acid = c(300, 400), BUN = c(14, 16),   phosphate = c(1.0, 1.2), calcium = c(2.3, 2.4),   Na = c(140, 138), K = c(4.2, 4.0), Cl = c(100, 102), HCO3 = c(24, 26),   Tyr = c(60, 70), Phe = c(50, 55) )  nutrient_markers(   data = df,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = "keep",   check_extreme = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   FerritinTS   AGR Omega3Index Mg_Cr_Ratio GlycatedAlbuminPct UA_Cr_Ratio #>        <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>              <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1       1.67  1.8            6      0.0106               26.7        3.75 #> 2       2     1.14           7      0.01                 35          4.44 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: BUN_Cr_Ratio <dbl>, Ca_x_Phosphate <dbl>, AnionGap <dbl>, #> #   Tyr_Phe_Ratio <dbl>

title: Nutrient Markers
code: nutrient_markers(   data = df,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = "omit",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   FerritinTS   AGR Omega3Index Mg_Cr_Ratio GlycatedAlbuminPct UA_Cr_Ratio #>        <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>              <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1       1.67  1.8            6      0.0106               26.7        3.75 #> 2       2     1.14           7      0.01                 35          4.44 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: BUN_Cr_Ratio <dbl>, Ca_x_Phosphate <dbl>, AnionGap <dbl>, #> #   Tyr_Phe_Ratio <dbl>

title: Nutrient Markers
code: try(   nutrient_markers(     data = df,     col_map = NULL,     na_action = "error"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   FerritinTS   AGR Omega3Index Mg_Cr_Ratio GlycatedAlbuminPct UA_Cr_Ratio #>        <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>              <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1       1.67  1.8            6      0.0106               26.7        3.75 #> 2       2     1.14           7      0.01                 35          4.44 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: BUN_Cr_Ratio <dbl>, Ca_x_Phosphate <dbl>, AnionGap <dbl>, #> #   Tyr_Phe_Ratio <dbl>

title: Nutrient Markers
text: Returns tibble : FerritinTS, AGR, Omega3Index, Mg_Cr_Ratio, GlycatedAlbuminPct, UA_Cr_Ratio, BUN_Cr_Ratio, Ca_x_Phosphate, AnionGap, Tyr_Phe_Ratio. marker computed inputs present; otherwise NA returned marker. Zero/non-finite denominators set NA consolidated warnings; unit harmonization applied.

title: Nutrient Markers

text: Provide inputs ; missing inputs yield NA dependent markers. Omega3Index expects EPA/DHA percentages; AGR uses globulin = total_protein - albumin. Tighten extreme_rules lab ranges using extreme_action = "cap" "error". Use na_action = "omit" prefer row-complete outputs; use keep QA inspect missingness.

title: Obesity Indices
text: Compute anthropometric adiposity/shape indices (BMI + category, WHR/WHtR, AVI, BAI, ABSI, BRI, Conicity Index, waist/BMI weight/height ratios, optional WHRadjBMI RFM) unit conversion, NA/extreme handling, safe-division guards.

title: Obesity Indices
text: weight/height/waist/hip (optionally sex) want comprehensive set body composition proxies one call. need built-unit conversion (kg/lb, cm/m), NA policy controls, optional extreme-value scanning/capping. want optional BMI-adjusted WHR Relative Fat Mass sex coding available.

title: Obesity Indices
text: data: data frame/tibble numeric columns weight, height, waist, hip; sex needed include_RFM = TRUE (coded 0=male, 1=female). Column arguments unquoted: weight, height, waist, hip, optional sex. Units: weight kg (lb weight_unit = "lb"), height m (cm height_unit = "cm"), waist/hip cm. weight_unit / height_unit: convert kg/m internally. adjust_WHR: add BMI-adjusted WHR residuals. include_RFM: compute Relative Fat Mass (requires sex). na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts required inputs contain NA. na_warn_prop: threshold high-missingness debug messages (default 0.2). check_extreme: set TRUE scan weight_kg/height_m/waist/hip; extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore) controls handling. Defaults: weight_kg 20–400; height_m 1.2–2.5; waist 30–200 cm; hip 30–200 cm. verbose: optional progress summary logging.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Units: Inputs must already mmol/L (glucose) pmol/L (insulin); weight kg; BMI kg/m^2; age years; sex coded 1/2 (0/1 coerced). function internally converts glucose mg/dL (x18) insulin muU/mL (/6) specific formulas require .

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Validation: required columns must exist; non-numeric inputs coerced warnings; non-finite values become NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA (computation). High-missing diagnostics: na_warn_prop controls debug/verbose messages missingness; enable verbose = TRUE see . Unit handling: inputs expected mmol/L (glucose) pmol/L (insulin); internally converted mg/dL (*18) muU/mL (/6) formulas need . Extreme screening (optional): applied computing indices; flags |value| > extreme_limit; handling via extreme_action (warn/cap/error/ignore/NA). Normalization (optional): applied extreme handling via normalize_vec(); use “none” raw interpretable values. Sex coding: expects 1 = male, 2 = female; 0 treated female coercion; strings become NA.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Returns tibble : Isi_120, Cederholm_index, Gutt_index, Avignon_Si0, Avignon_Si120, Avignon_Sim, Modified_stumvoll, Stumvoll_Demographics, Matsuda_AUC, Matsuda_ISI, BigttSi, Ifc_inv, HIRI_inv, Belfiore_isi_gly. NA indicates missing/invalid inputs, zero/negative arguments logs, values blanked extreme handling.

title: Psychometric markers
text: Map canonical item IDs dataset columns. Keys BIS/SPQ stay user-supplied (item text package).
code: col_map <- list(   phq9 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("phq9_%02d", 1:9), sprintf("phq9_%02d", 1:9))),   gad7 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("gad7_%02d", 1:7), sprintf("gad7_%02d", 1:7))),   k6   = list(items = setNames(sprintf("k6_%02d", 1:6), sprintf("k6_%02d", 1:6))),   k10  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("k10_%02d", 1:10), sprintf("k10_%02d", 1:10))),   ghq12 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("ghq12_%02d", 1:12), sprintf("ghq12_%02d", 1:12))),   who5 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("who5_%02d", 1:5), sprintf("who5_%02d", 1:5))),   isi  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("isi_%02d", 1:7), sprintf("isi_%02d", 1:7))),   mdq  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("mdq_%02d", 1:13), sprintf("mdq_%02d", 1:13)), cluster = "mdq_cluster", impairment = "mdq_impair"),   asrs = list(items = setNames(sprintf("asrs_%02d", 1:18), sprintf("asrs_%02d", 1:18))),   bis  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("bis_%02d", 1:12), sprintf("bis_%02d", 1:12))),   spq  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("spq_%02d", 1:4), sprintf("spq_%02d", 1:4))),   cognitive = list(tasks = list(rt = "task_rt", memory = "task_mem", attention = "task_att")),   dx_flags = list(dx = list(mdd = "dx_mdd", anxiety = "dx_anxiety", adhd = "dx_adhd", bipolar = "dx_bipolar", scz = "dx_scz", sud = "dx_sud")),   med_flags = list(med = list(ssri = "med_ssri", snri = "med_snri", antipsychotic = "med_antipsychotic", mood_stabilizer = "med_mood_stabilizer", anxiolytic = "med_anxiolytic")) )  bis_key <- list(   name = "BIS_demo",   items = sprintf("bis_%02d", 1:12),   min_val = 1, max_val = 4,   reverse = c("bis_02","bis_03"),   subscales = list(attention = c("bis_01","bis_05"), motor = c("bis_02","bis_06")) )  spq_key <- list(   name = "SPQ_demo",   items = sprintf("spq_%02d", 1:4),   min_val = 0, max_val = 1 )

title: Psychometric markers
text: Select scales want via . Rowwise mean imputation optional; keep defaults missingness tolerance 20% per scale.
code: out <- psych_markers(   data = psych_df,   col_map = col_map,   which = c("phq9","gad7","k6","k10","ghq12_binary","who5","isi","mdq","asrs","bis","spq","cognitive","dx_flags","med_flags"),   na_action = "keep",   missing_prop_max = 0.2,   impute = "mean",   bis_key = bis_key,   spq_key = spq_key,   cognitive_method = "z_mean" )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(out), names(psych_df)) dplyr::select(out, dplyr::all_of(new_cols)) #> # A tibble: 1 × 33 #>   PHQ9_total PHQ9_severity GAD7_total GAD7_severity K6_total K6_case K10_total #>        <dbl> <fct>              <dbl> <fct>            <dbl> <lgl>       <dbl> #> 1         11 moderate               4 minimal              9 FALSE          10 #> # ℹ 26 more variables: GHQ12_total_binary <dbl>, GHQ12_case_binary <lgl>, #> #   WHO5_raw <dbl>, WHO5_percent <dbl>, WHO5_low_wellbeing <lgl>, #> #   ISI_total <dbl>, ISI_severity <fct>, MDQ_symptom_count <dbl>, #> #   MDQ_clustering <lgl>, MDQ_impairment <lgl>, MDQ_positive_screen <lgl>, #> #   ASRS_total <dbl>, ASRS_partA_count <dbl>, ASRS_partA_positive <lgl>, #> #   BIS_total <dbl>, BIS_attention <dbl>, BIS_motor <dbl>, SPQ_total <dbl>, #> #   cog_z_mean <dbl>, dx_any_psych <lgl>, dx_internalizing <lgl>, …

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Compute spirometry-based pulmonary markers using GLI/GLIgl/NHANES3 reference equations rspiro: predicted normals, z-scores, percent-predicted, LLN FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC ratio. Handles missing-data policies, basic height unit detection, verbose diagnostics.

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: need standardized pulmonary markers (predicted, z, percent-predicted, LLN) FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC using established reference equations. dataset age/sex/height/ethnicity plus measured FEV1 FVC (liters). want controlled handling missingness clear warnings implausible inputs (e.g., unmapped sex/ethnicity, zero/negative heights).

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Required columns (names must match exactly): age (years), sex (“male”/“female” 1/2 0/1), height (cm m; auto-detected), ethnicity (text), fev1 (L), fvc (L). col_map needed; use names. Units: FEV1/FVC liters. Height auto-detected cm value > 3; otherwise treated meters. unit conversions performed.

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns checked; age/height/fev1/fvc coerced numeric warnings NAs introduced. Missingness: keep = propagate NA; omit = drop rows required NA; error = abort required NA. Height handling: height > 3 treated cm divided 100; non-positive heights warn yield NA outputs. Sex/Ethnicity mapping: case-insensitive mapping; unmapped values warn become NA (sex) /Mixed (ethnicity code 5). Division zero/non-finite: ratios set NA denominator zero non-finite. Dependency: requires rspiro; aborts missing chosen equation unsupported.

title: Pulmonary Markers

text: Interpretation: Mixed height units handled; missingness (none ) propagate. Outputs include predicted, z, percent-predicted, ratios.

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Missing package: install rspiro absent. Unsupported equation: ensure equation GLI, GLIgl, NHANES3. Unit issues: heights must cm m; volumes liters. Convert inches units upstream. Unmapped sex/ethnicity: check spelling/casing; unmapped values warn become NA/. High missingness: heed na_warn_prop warnings; consider na_action = "omit" modeling. NA outputs: likely missing required fields, zero/negative heights, failed mapping.
