title: Renal Markers
text: Compute renal function, excretion, injury markers: CKD-EPI 2009 creatinine eGFR (race factor retained), optional cystatin C combined eGFR, BUN/Cr ratio, fractional excretion urea (FE_Urea), plus pass-urine injury markers (NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP). Includes mapping validation, missingness policies, optional extreme screening/capping, zero-denominator warnings. unit conversion performed.

title: Renal Markers
text: need eGFR (creatinine ± cystatin C) FE_Urea renal function AKI assessment. data includes required labs (creatinine, age, sex, race, BUN) optionally cystatin C urine assays. want clear handling missingness, extreme-value screening, transparent NA propagation.

title: Renal Markers
text: Required columns (col_map): creatinine (mg/dL), age (years), sex (1/0 “male”/“female”), race (aliases white/black/), BUN (mg/dL). Optional columns: cystatin_C (mg/L), urea_serum (mg/dL), creatinine_urine (mg/dL), urea_urine (mg/dL), NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro (outputs Beta2Micro), IL18, L_FABP. Units: conversion. Serum creatinine/BUN/urea mg/dL; cystatin C mg/L; urine creatinine/urea mg/dL. FE_Urea requires urea_serum + creatinine_urine + urea_urine.

title: Renal Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; numeric coercion applied warnings NAs introduced. Missingness: keep = propagate NA; omit = drop rows required NA; error = abort required NA. High-missingness diagnostics: na_warn_prop governs debug warnings appear (verbose/debug). Extreme screening (optional): check_extreme uses default bounds; extreme_action controls warn/cap/error/ignore; extreme_rules overrides per key. Zero denominators: divisions NA consolidated warning (e.g., BUN/Cr, FE_Urea components). Race factor: CKD-EPI 2009 race coefficient retained (race-free applied ). Injury markers: NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro, IL18, L_FABP passed .numeric without transformation range checks.

title: Renal Markers
text: Default extreme bounds (used check_extreme = TRUE overrides): creatinine 0.1–15 mg/dL; BUN 1–200 mg/dL; cystatin_C 0.2–8 mg/L; urea_serum 1–300 mg/dL; creatinine_urine 1–500 mg/dL; urea_urine 1–2000 mg/dL. Sex mapping: accepts 1/0, 1/2, male/female strings; unmapped values become NA warning. Race mapping: common aliases map “black” “white”; everything else becomes “” (NA). Zero denominators: consolidated warning lists ratios hit zero (e.g., BUN_Cr_ratio, FE_Urea components) many. NA diagnostics: high-missingness messages emitted debug/verbose levels; set verbose = TRUE QC see . Empty result: na_action = "omit" removes rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Renal Markers
text: Returns tibble (rows follow na_action). Columns: eGFR_cr, eGFR_cys (cystatin_C provided), eGFR_combined (Cys provided), BUN_Cr_ratio, FE_Urea (needs urea_serum + creatinine_urine + urea_urine), NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP (pass-mapped). NA inputs missing/invalid, denominators zero, optional inputs absent.

title: Renal Markers
text: Interpretation: eGFR_cr BUN/Cr returned; third row yields NA outputs creatinine missing.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   Cr = c(1.0, 1.3, NA),   Age = c(40, 72, 55),   Sex = c(1, 0, 1),   Race = c("white", "black", "other"),   BUN = c(14, 22, 18) )  renal_markers(   data = df,   col_map = list(     creatinine = "Cr",     age = "Age",     sex = "Sex",     race = "Race",     BUN = "BUN"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 11 #>   eGFR_cr eGFR_cys eGFR_combined BUN_Cr_ratio FE_Urea  NGAL  KIM1   NAG #>     <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1    93.7       NA            NA         14        NA    NA    NA    NA #> 2    46.6       NA            NA         16.9      NA    NA    NA    NA #> 3    NA         NA            NA         NA        NA    NA    NA    NA #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Beta2Micro <dbl>, IL18 <dbl>, L_FABP <dbl>

title: Saliva Markers
text: Compute saliva-based stress glycemic markers: safe-log waking cortisol, cortisol awakening response AUC (trapezoidal 3 samples), safe-log salivary alpha-amylase, pass-salivary glucose. Includes mapping validation, missingness policies, optional extreme screening/capping inputs, safe handling non-positive values logs.

title: Saliva Markers
text: three salivary cortisol measures around wake (e.g., 0/30/60 minutes) want CAR AUC log cortisol. also collect salivary alpha-amylase (stress marker) /salivary glucose need cleaned, log-safe outputs. want explicit NA policies, optional extreme truncation, flexible sampling times AUC.

title: Saliva Markers
text: Required columns (col_map): cort1, cort2, cort3 (cortisol nmol/L), amylase (U/mL), glucose (mg/dL). Mapped columns must exist. Units: conversion performed. Supply cortisol nmol/L, alpha-amylase U/mL, salivary glucose mg/dL.

title: Saliva Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; non-numeric coerced warnings NAs introduced; non-finite become NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Extreme screening (optional): checks inputs, outputs. Uses defaults unless extreme_rules provided; extreme_action controls warn/cap/error/ignore. Safe logs: cortisol amylase log-transformed setting non-positive NA. CAR AUC: trapezoidal three cortisol values using times; non-finite cortisol triplet yields NA row. Times: must numeric length 3, non-decreasing; customize protocol. unit conversion: ensure assays consistent units calling.

title: Saliva Markers
text: Default extreme bounds (check_extreme = TRUE overrides): cortisol () 0–2000, amylase 0–50000, glucose 0–1000 (keyed mapped column names). Extreme rules can keyed input names (cort1/2/3/amylase/glucose) actual column names; remapped internally. NA diagnostics appear verbose/debug logging; set verbose = TRUE QC. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Saliva Markers
text: Returns tibble (rows follow na_action) : log_cortisol_wake, CAR_AUC, log_amylase, saliva_glucose. NA indicates missing/invalid inputs, non-positive values log, NA cortisol CAR_AUC, values dropped via na_action.

what: Worked example 1: Standard times, keep NAs
title: Saliva Markers
text: Interpretation: CAR_AUC log cortisol/amylase compute inputs present positive; third row yields NA log_cortisol_wake CAR_AUC due missing cort1.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   saliva_cort1 = c(12.5, 9.0, NA),   saliva_cort2 = c(18.0, 11.0, 7.5),   saliva_cort3 = c(16.2, 10.5, 6.8),   saliva_amylase = c(85, 120, 95),   saliva_glucose = c(4.2, 5.0, 4.8) )  saliva_markers(   data = df,   col_map = list(     cort1 = "saliva_cort1",     cort2 = "saliva_cort2",     cort3 = "saliva_cort3",     amylase = "saliva_amylase",     glucose = "saliva_glucose"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   log_cortisol_wake CAR_AUC log_amylase saliva_glucose #>               <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1              2.53    970.        4.44            4.2 #> 2              2.20    622.        4.79            5   #> 3             NA        NA         4.55            4.8

title: Saliva Markers
text: YAML/header placement: keep YAML top; stray text breaks knitting. Missing columns: ensure every required col_map key mapped existing column. Non-positive cortisol/amylase: become NA logging; expect NA outputs values 0 negative. Extreme handling: runs check_extreme = TRUE; defaults broad—override extreme_rules needed. Times validation: must numeric length 3 non-decreasing; otherwise function aborts. NA outputs: typically due missing cortisol triplets, non-positive cortisol/amylase, aggressive na_action = "omit".

Set na_action = "omit" modeling datasets; keep better exploratory review. Provide accurate sampling times minutes CAR AUC protocol differs 0/30/60.

title: Saliva Markers
text: CAR_AUC trapezoidal: ∑=12ci+ci+12×(ti+1−ti)\sum_{=1}^{2} \frac{c_i + c_{+1}}{2} \times (t_{+1} - t_i) using three cortisol values times gaps. Safe logs: log(x)\log(x) taken x>0x > 0 finite; otherwise NA. Extreme capping (enabled) happens inputs log/AUC computation.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Compute 5-item SARC-F sarcopenia screening score (Strength, Assistance walking, Rise chair, Climb stairs, Falls). Items expected 0–2 scale; total ranges 0–10. Flags sarc_f_high_risk total ≥ 4. Includes mapping validation, NA policies, optional extreme screening/capping, non-numeric coercion safeguards.

title: SARC-F Score
text: SARC-F questionnaire responses need total score plus high-risk flag. want controlled handling missing miscoded responses (0–2 expected) optional capping. need outputs suitable downstream modeling screening summaries.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Required columns (col_map): strength, walking, chair, stairs, falls (expected 0/1/2). Mapped columns must exist. Units: None. Inputs already coded 0–2; transformation beyond coercion/capping/NA handling.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Validation & coercion: mapped columns must exist; non-numeric inputs coerced numeric warnings NAs introduced; non-finite values become NA. Missingness: keep/ignore/warn propagate NA total; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts missing. --range check: even without check_extreme, warning issued values fall outside 0–2. check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_action decides warn/cap/error/ignore/NA. Extreme rules: defaults cap item 0–2; can overridden per item via extreme_rules. Scoring: sarc_f_score sums five items; sarc_f_high_risk TRUE score ≥ 4, FALSE otherwise, NA score NA. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Returns tibble sarc_f_score (0–10, NA) sarc_f_high_risk (TRUE/FALSE/NA). NA arises missing inputs, NA introduced coercion, NA set extreme handling, dropped rows omit.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Interpretation: Rows complete items return scores; row NA item yields sarc_f_score = NA sarc_f_high_risk = NA.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   Strength = c(0, 1, 2, NA),   Walking  = c(0, 0, 2, 1),   Chair    = c(0, 1, 2, 1),   Stairs   = c(0, 1, 2, 1),   Falls    = c(0, 0, 1, 0) )  sarc_f_score(   data = df,   col_map = list(     strength = "Strength",     walking  = "Walking",     chair    = "Chair",     stairs   = "Stairs",     falls    = "Falls"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   sarc_f_score sarc_f_high_risk #>          <dbl> <lgl>            #> 1            0 FALSE            #> 2            3 FALSE            #> 3            9 TRUE             #> 4           NA NA

title: SARC-F Score
text: YAML/header: keep YAML top; text breaks knitting. Missing columns: ensure every required col_map key mapped existing column. Non-numeric inputs: coerced warnings; resulting NAs propagate unless omit used. --range values: warning emitted even without check_extreme; set check_extreme = TRUE + extreme_action = "cap" "NA" enforce bounds. NA scores: usually due missing items, coercion NA, NA introduced extreme handling extreme_action = "NA".

code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") sarc_f_score(   data.frame(Strength = 1, Walking = 1, Chair = 1, Stairs = 0, Falls = 0),   col_map = list(strength="Strength", walking="Walking", chair="Chair", stairs="Stairs", falls="Falls"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> sarc_f_score(): preparing inputs #> sarc_f_score(): column map: strength -> 'Strength', walking -> 'Walking', chair -> 'Chair', stairs -> 'Stairs', falls -> 'Falls' #> sarc_f_score(): results: sarc_f_score 1/1, sarc_f_high_risk 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   sarc_f_score sarc_f_high_risk #>          <dbl> <lgl>            #> 1            3 FALSE options(old_opt)

Treat sarc_f_high_risk screening flag (score ≥ 4); follow clinical assessment appropriate.

title: Spirometry Markers

Includes mapping validation, NA policies, optional extreme screening/capping volumes, safe handling zero/negative values ratios > 1 warnings.

title: Spirometry Markers

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Units: Inputs must liters. unit conversion performed. GLI inputs (optional): age (years), height (cm), sex (male/female 1/0/2), ethnicity (GLI-compatible strings). Requires rspiro; otherwise debug fallback reference used GLI fields stay NA.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; non-numeric volumes coerced warnings; non-finite become NA. Missingness: keep/ignore/warn propagate NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required NA. Extreme screening (optional): bounds fev1/fvc; extreme_action controls warn/cap/error/ignore/NA. Defaults cap 0–8/0–10 enabled. Domain warnings: zero FVC -> ratio NA warning; negative volumes warn; ratios > 1 warn check units. GLI references: used age/height/sex/ethnicity provided rspiro available; else simple fallback used (debug) GLI outputs remain NA. Ethnicity mapped GLI categories; sex mapped first letter/1/0/2. GOLD grade: computed obstruction_lln TRUE; uses fev1 percent-predicted thresholds. Bronchodilator response: percent change (post - pre) / pre * 100; requires pre post columns. Padding: rows omitted na_action = "omit", outputs padded back input row count NA policy keeps rows.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Default extreme bounds: fev1 0–8 L, fvc 0–10 L (applied check_extreme = TRUE). GLI mapping: sex strings starting m/1 -> Male; f/0/2 -> Female; ethnicity regex maps GLI categories (Caucasian, African American, North East Asian, South East Asian, ). Fallback reference (rspiro unavailable): rough height/age equations testability; clinical—aimed avoid -NA outputs tests. High-missing diagnostics emitted unless verbose/debug logging enabled via hm_inform. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Columns: ratio_pre, ratio_post, copd_flag_fixed, obstruction_lln, fev1_pp, fvc_pp, fev1_z, fvc_z, ratio_z, gold_grade, bdr_fev1, bdr_fvc. NA arises required inputs missing, ratios undefined (e.g., zero FVC), GLI inputs absent/invalid, post values missing deltas.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Interpretation: Returns pre FEV1/FVC ratios fixed-ratio flags; NA propagates inputs missing.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   FEV1 = c(2.8, 1.6, NA),   FVC  = c(3.6, 2.1, 3.0) )  spirometry_markers(   data = df,   col_map = list(fev1 = "FEV1", fvc = "FVC"),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>   ratio_pre ratio_post copd_flag_fixed obstruction_lln fev1_pp fvc_pp fev1_z #>       <dbl>      <dbl> <lgl>           <lgl>             <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1     0.778         NA FALSE           NA                   NA     NA     NA #> 2     0.762         NA FALSE           NA                   NA     NA     NA #> 3    NA             NA NA              NA                   NA     NA     NA #> # ℹ 5 more variables: fvc_z <dbl>, ratio_z <dbl>, gold_grade <chr>, #> #   bdr_fev1 <dbl>, bdr_fvc <dbl>

what: Worked example 2: With post-BD, GLI inputs, cap extremes
title: Spirometry Markers
text: Interpretation: Extremes capped ratios; GLI fields computed rspiro available, otherwise fall back remain NA. Bronchodilator deltas percent changes pre→post.
code: df2 <- tibble::tibble(   fev1_pre = c(2.1, 0.9, 7.5),   fvc_pre  = c(3.0, 1.8, 11.0),   fev1_post = c(2.4, 1.1, 7.2),   fvc_post  = c(3.2, 1.9, 10.5),   age = c(55, 72, 45),   height = c(170, 160, 182),   sex = c("male", "female", "male"),   ethnicity = c("Caucasian", "African American", "Other") )  cm2 <- list(   fev1 = "fev1_pre",   fvc = "fvc_pre",   fev1_post = "fev1_post",   fvc_post = "fvc_post",   age = "age",   height = "height",   sex = "sex",   ethnicity = "ethnicity" )  spirometry_markers(   data = df2,   col_map = cm2,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>   ratio_pre ratio_post copd_flag_fixed obstruction_lln fev1_pp fvc_pp fev1_z #>       <dbl>      <dbl> <lgl>           <lgl>             <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1      0.7       0.75  FALSE           FALSE              40.8   42.1  -5.92 #> 2      0.5       0.579 TRUE            TRUE               23.7   31.2  -7.63 #> 3      0.75      0.686 TRUE            TRUE              126.   125.    2.65 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: fvc_z <dbl>, ratio_z <dbl>, gold_grade <chr>, #> #   bdr_fev1 <dbl>, bdr_fvc <dbl>

text: Interpretation: Aborts required inputs contain NA.
code: try(   spirometry_markers(     data = df,     col_map = list(fev1 = "FEV1", fvc = "FVC"),     na_action = "error"   ) ) #> Error in spirometry_markers(data = df, col_map = list(fev1 = "FEV1", fvc = "FVC"),  :  #>   spirometry_markers(): required inputs contain missing values (na_action='error').

title: Spirometry Markers
text: YAML/header: keep YAML top; stray text breaks knitting. Units: volumes must liters; convert upstream. Ratios > 1 often indicate unit issues. Missing columns: ensure fev1 fvc mapped; post GLI inputs optional. GLI availability: requires rspiro plus age/height/sex/ethnicity; otherwise GLI outputs may NA use fallback approximations. Zero/negative volumes: produce warnings; zero FVC yields NA ratios. Outliers: enable check_extreme extreme_action = "cap" "NA" constrain implausible volumes.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Provide demographics install rspiro GLI-based percent-predicted GOLD grades needed; otherwise rely fixed 0.70 flag. Use na_action = "omit" modeling datasets require complete spirometry; keep/warn exploratory review. Review ratios > 1 negative volumes—usually indicate unit entry errors. bronchodilator response, supply pre post volumes; otherwise response outputs NA.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Ratio: FEV1 / FVC; fixed obstruction flag uses 0.70 (prefers post values present). Bronchodilator response: 100 * (post - pre) / pre FEV1 FVC. GOLD stage uses fev1 percent-predicted obstruction_lln TRUE; relies GLI fallback references. Fallback references non-clinical intended avoid -NA outputs rspiro absent.

title: Sweat Markers
text: Compute sweat chloride, sodium/potassium ratio, sweat lactate, sweat rate (L/m²/h) sweat assays anthropometrics. Includes mapping validation, NA policies, optional extreme screening/capping inputs, safe handling zero/invalid denominators.

title: Sweat Markers
text: sweat chloride, sodium, potassium, lactate, anthropometrics (weight /, duration, body surface area) want derive sweat rate ratios. want explicit NA handling, optional extreme-value capping, consolidated zero-denominator warnings.

title: Sweat Markers
text: Required columns (col_map): sweat_chloride, sweat_Na, sweat_K, sweat_lactate (mmol/L), weight_before, weight_after (kg), duration (h), body_surface_area (m²). Units: conversion performed. Supply values expected units .

title: Sweat Markers
text: Validation & coercion: mapped columns must exist; non-numeric inputs coerced warnings NAs introduced; non-finite become NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Extreme screening (optional): checks inputs; defaults broad (chloride/Na 0–200, K 0–40, lactate 0–50, weights 10–400, duration 0.05–48, BSA 0.3–3.0); extreme_action controls warn/cap/error/ignore. Safe division: ratios use helper sets NA zero/invalid denominators emits consolidated warning. Sweat rate: (weight_before−weight_after)/duration/body_surface_area(\text{weight_before} - \text{weight_after}) / \text{duration} / \text{body_surface_area} (1 kg ≈ 1 L water). Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Sweat Markers
text: Returns tibble (rows follow na_action) : sweat_chloride, Na_K_ratio, sweat_lactate, sweat_rate. NA arises missing/invalid inputs, zero/invalid denominators, dropped rows omit.

what: Worked example 1: Standard usage, keep NAs
title: Sweat Markers
text: Interpretation: Returns outputs; NA propagates inputs missing denominators zero (e.g., sweat_K = 0).
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   sweat_chloride = c(45, 60, NA),   sweat_Na = c(55, 70, 60),   sweat_K = c(5, 4, 0),   sweat_lactate = c(4.8, 5.2, 6.0),   weight_before = c(70.0, 80.0, 75.0),   weight_after = c(69.5, 79.2, 74.8),   duration = c(1.0, 1.5, 0.5),   body_surface_area = c(1.9, 2.1, 2.0) )  sweat_markers(   data = df,   col_map = list(     sweat_chloride = "sweat_chloride",     sweat_Na = "sweat_Na",     sweat_K = "sweat_K",     sweat_lactate = "sweat_lactate",     weight_before = "weight_before",     weight_after = "weight_after",     duration = "duration",     body_surface_area = "body_surface_area"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   sweat_chloride Na_K_ratio sweat_lactate sweat_rate #>            <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1             45       11             4.8      0.263 #> 2             60       17.5           5.2      0.254 #> 3             NA       NA             6        0.200

what: Worked example 2: Cap extremes, drop incomplete
title: Sweat Markers
text: Interpretation: Extremes capped computing ratios/rates; incomplete rows dropped.
code: df2 <- tibble::tibble(   cl = c(180, 250),       # extreme chloride   na = c(60, 10),   k = c(4, 0.5),   lac = c(6, 60),         # extreme lactate   w_before = c(75, 90),   w_after = c(74.2, 89.0),   dur = c(1, 0.2),   bsa = c(2.0, 1.8) )  sweat_markers(   data = df2,   col_map = list(     sweat_chloride = "cl",     sweat_Na = "na",     sweat_K = "k",     sweat_lactate = "lac",     weight_before = "w_before",     weight_after = "w_after",     duration = "dur",     body_surface_area = "bsa"   ),   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   sweat_chloride Na_K_ratio sweat_lactate sweat_rate #>            <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1            180         15             6      0.400 #> 2            200         20            50      2.78

title: Sweat Markers
text: Use check_extreme = TRUE extreme_action = "cap" hard-limit inputs plausible ranges. Choose na_action = "omit" modeling datasets require complete cases; use keep exploratory review. Review warnings zero denominators capped values; often indicate data entry assay issues.

title: Sweat Markers
text: Na_K_ratio = sweat_Na / sweat_K; sweat_rate = (weight_before - weight_after) / duration / body_surface_area (1 kg ≈ 1 L). divisions safe: zero/invalid denominators yield NA warning. Default bounds: chloride/Na 0–200, K 0–40, lactate 0–50, weights 10–400, duration 0.05–48, BSA 0.3–3.0 (applied check_extreme = TRUE).

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices: LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv (adipose-mode), , OGTT lipid data present, I_AUC, FFA_AUC, tracer_palmitate_SI, tracer_glycerol_SI. Includes mapping validation, NA/extreme handling, safe division/logs, built-unit conversions insulin, TG, HDL-c.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: tracer infusion rates, DXA fat mass, optionally OGTT/lipid data want validated insulin sensitivity indices. want explicit NA/extreme handling, safe log/division, automatic mode selection based available columns.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Adipose-required keys: I0, rate_glycerol, rate_palmitate, fat_mass, weight, HDL_c, bmi. Full mode additional required keys: G0, G30, G120, I30, I120, TG, FFA. Units: - Glucose: mmol/L (conversion except formulas needing mg/dL) - Insulin: pmol/L (internally /6 microU/mL) - TG: mmol/L (×88.57 mg/dL) - HDL-c: mmol/L (×38.67 mg/dL) - Tracer rates: micromol/min - Fat mass, weight: kg; BMI: kg/m²

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Validation & coercion: mapped columns must exist; non-numeric inputs coerced warnings NAs introduced; non-finite become NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Extreme screening (optional): checks inputs; defaults broad (see ); extreme_action controls warn/cap/error/ignore. Safe division/log: ratios/logs use helpers set NA zero/invalid denominators non-positive log arguments, consolidated warnings. Mode selection: OGTT/lipid keys present, full mode used; otherwise, adipose-mode. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: I0/I30/I120: 0–5000 pmol/L G0/G30/G120: 0–40 mmol/L TG: 0–50 mmol/L; HDL_c: 0–10 mmol/L; FFA: 0–5 mmol/L rate_glycerol/rate_palmitate: 0–10000 micromol/min fat_mass: 0.1–200 kg; weight: 1–400 kg; bmi: 5–100 kg/m² Safe logs: positive, finite values logged; others become NA. Consolidated zero-denominator warnings emitted divisions.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Adipose-: LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv. Full mode: I_AUC, FFA_AUC, tracer_palmitate_SI, tracer_glycerol_SI, LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv. NA arises missing/invalid inputs, zero/invalid denominators, dropped rows omit.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Interpretation: Returns adipose indices; NA propagates required inputs missing invalid.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   I0 = c(60, 80, NA),              # pmol/L   rate_glycerol = c(150, 180, 160), # micromol/min   rate_palmitate = c(120, 140, 130),   fat_mass = c(25, 30, 28),        # kg   weight = c(70, 82, 75),          # kg   HDL_c = c(1.2, 1.0, 1.1),        # mmol/L   bmi = c(24, 27, 25) )  tracer_dxa_is(   data = df,   col_map = list(     I0 = "I0",     rate_glycerol = "rate_glycerol",     rate_palmitate = "rate_palmitate",     fat_mass = "fat_mass",     weight = "weight",     HDL_c = "HDL_c",     bmi = "bmi"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   LIRI_inv Lipo_inv ATIRI_inv #>      <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    -1.08    -1500    -1200  #> 2    -1.19    -2400    -1867. #> 3    NA          NA       NA

title: Urine Markers
text: Compute urine-renal tubular markers: UACR, KDIGO albuminuria stage (A1–A3), microalbuminuria flag, UPCR (protein provided), urine Na/K ratio (electrolytes provided), creatinine-normalized tubular markers (NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, A1Micro, IL18, L_FABP). Includes mapping validation, NA policies, optional extreme screening/capping, safe division consolidated zero-denominator warnings. unit conversion applied.

title: Urine Markers
text: urine albumin creatinine (mg/L, mg/dL) want UACR albuminuria staging. also urine protein, Na/K, tubular markers need creatinine-normalized ratios. want robust NA/extreme handling safe divisions avoid Inf/NaN.

title: Urine Markers
text: Required columns: urine_albumin (mg/L), urine_creatinine (mg/dL). Optional columns: urine_protein (mg/L), urine_Na (mmol/L), urine_K (mmol/L), NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro (Beta2Micro output), a1_micro (A1Micro output), IL18, L_FABP. Units: conversion. Ensure albumin/protein mg/L, creatinine mg/dL, Na/K mmol/L; tubular marker units consistent across markers.

title: Urine Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; numeric coercion warnings NAs introduced; non-finite set NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Extreme screening (optional): defaults broad (albumin/protein/markers 0–100000 mg/L; creatinine 0.01–500 mg/dL; Na 0–400; K 0–200); extreme_action controls warn/cap/error/ignore. Safe division: zero/invalid denominators set outputs NA consolidated warning. Albuminuria staging: A1 < 30 mg/g, A2 30–300 mg/g, A3 > 300 mg/g; microalbuminuria flag micro 30–300 mg/g, else normal. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Urine Markers
text: UACR, albuminuria_stage (A1/A2/A3), microalbuminuria (normal/micro), UPCR (urine_protein present), U_Na_K_ratio (Na & K present), NGAL_per_gCr, KIM1_per_gCr, NAG_per_gCr, Beta2Micro_per_gCr, A1Micro_per_gCr, IL18_per_gCr, L_FABP_per_gCr. NA arises missing/invalid inputs, zero denominators, dropped rows omit.

what: Worked example 1: Standard inputs, keep NAs
title: Urine Markers
text: Interpretation: Computes UACR, staging, micro flag, UPCR, U_Na_K_ratio, NGAL_per_gCr inputs exist; NA propagates missing zero denominators (e.g., urine_K = 0).
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   urine_albumin = c(25, 450, NA),      # mg/L   urine_creatinine = c(1.0, 0.8, 0.5), # mg/dL   urine_protein = c(120, NA, 90),      # mg/L   urine_Na = c(100, 80, 90),   urine_K = c(40, 30, 0),   NGAL = c(15, 40, 20) )  urine_markers(   data = df,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>     UACR albuminuria_stage microalbuminuria  UPCR U_Na_K_ratio NGAL_per_gCr #>    <dbl> <fct>             <fct>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1  25000 A3                normal           12000         2.5          1500 #> 2 562500 A3                normal              NA         2.67         5000 #> 3     NA NA                normal           18000        NA            4000 #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl>

what: Worked example 2: Cap extremes, drop incomplete
title: Urine Markers
text: Interpretation: Extremes capped ratios; incomplete rows dropped; zero-denominator warnings list counts.
code: df2 <- tibble::tibble(   urine_albumin = c(20, 120000),    # extreme albumin   urine_creatinine = c(1.2, 0.005), # very low creatinine   urine_protein = c(80, 90000),   urine_Na = c(90, 500),            # extreme Na   urine_K = c(35, 210),             # extreme K   NGAL = c(10, 200000) )  urine_markers(   data = df2,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 12 #>         UACR albuminuria_stage microalbuminuria   UPCR U_Na_K_ratio NGAL_per_gCr #>        <dbl> <fct>             <fct>             <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1    1.67e 4 A3                normal           6.67e3         2.57         833. #> 2    1   e10 A3                normal           9   e8         2     1000000000  #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl>

title: Urine Markers
text: YAML/header: keep YAML top; stray text breaks knitting. Units: ensure albumin/protein mg/L, creatinine mg/dL; Na/K mmol/L; tubular marker units consistent across markers. Missing columns: required columns must present; optional markers emit outputs supplied. Zero denominators: check urine_creatinine, urine_K; warnings list many cases; outputs set NA. Outliers: enable check_extreme extreme_action = "cap" "NA" constrain implausible values. NA outputs: often due missing required inputs, zero denominators, aggressive na_action = "omit".

title: Urine Markers
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") urine_markers(   data = tibble::tibble(urine_albumin = 30, urine_creatinine = 2),   verbose = TRUE ) #> urine_markers(): preparing inputs #> urine_markers(): column map: urine_albumin -> 'urine_albumin', urine_creatinine -> 'urine_creatinine' #> urine_markers(): results: UACR 1/1, albuminuria_stage 1/1, microalbuminuria 1/1, UPCR 0/1, U_Na_K_ratio 0/1, NGAL_per_gCr 0/1, KIM1_per_gCr 0/1, NAG_per_gCr 0/1, Beta2Micro_per_gCr 0/1, A1Micro_per_gCr 0/1, IL18_per_gCr 0/1, L_FABP_per_gCr 0/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 12 #>    UACR albuminuria_stage microalbuminuria  UPCR U_Na_K_ratio NGAL_per_gCr #>   <dbl> <fct>             <fct>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 15000 A3                normal              NA           NA           NA #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl> options(old_opt)

title: Urine Markers
text: Start check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_action = "warn" gauge outliers; switch cap truncate. Use na_action = "omit" downstream analyses need complete cases; keep exploratory review. Monitor zero-denominator warnings—extremely low creatinine K values blank ratios per-gCr markers.

title: Urine Markers
text: UACR = (urine_albumin / urine_creatinine) * 1000 (mg/g); UPCR analogous protein present. U_Na_K_ratio = urine_Na / urine_K. Tubular markers per g creatinine: marker / (urine_creatinine * 0.01) denominator g/L. Divisions safe: non-finite/zero denominators yield NA consolidated warning. Default bounds (applied check_extreme = TRUE): albumin/protein/markers 0–100000 mg/L; creatinine 0.01–500 mg/dL; Na 0–400; K 0–200.

title: Urine Markers
text: Function docs: ?urine_markers Related vignettes: renal_markers, health_markers, corrected_calcium

title: Vitamin D Status
text: vitamin_d_status() classifies serum 25(OH)D (assumed ng/mL) Deficient (<20), Insufficient (20-29), Sufficient (>=30). validates mappings, enforces missing-data policy, can scan/cap implausible values.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: Standardize vitamin D categorization dashboards, cohorts, QC summaries. Guard ETL steps: abort omit rows missing/implausible 25(OH)D downstream models. Spot potential unit mix-ups: nmol/L inputs often appear ~2.5x higher ng/mL.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: data: data frame/tibble containing 25(OH)D column. col_map: named list mapping either vitd vitamin_d column name. column must exist coercible numeric; coercion introduces NAs emits warning.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: na_action = c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn") (default “keep”): policy missing 25(OH)D. warn/ignore behave like keep; omit drops rows; error aborts. check_extreme = FALSE: turn plausible-range screening. extreme_action = c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") (default “warn”): response values outside bounds. extreme_rules = NULL: override default bounds vitamin D (defaults 0 250 ng/mL). verbose = FALSE: emit progress messages.

what: How missingness and extremes are handled
title: Vitamin D Status
text: Missing 25(OH)D follows na_action; omit shrinks output; error aborts. Non-numeric inputs coerced numeric; NAs introduced trigger warning. check_extreme = TRUE, values outside bounds handled per extreme_action: warn , cap range, abort, ignore, blank NA. Negative values high medians (suggesting nmol/L) warn extreme scanning .

title: Vitamin D Status
text: Tibble ordered factor vitamin_d_status (Deficient < Insufficient < Sufficient). Rows drop na_action = "omit"; otherwise output aligns input row count.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: Quick categorization Screen cap extremes
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(VitD = c(12, 25, 36, NA))  vitamin_d_status(   data = df,   col_map = list(vitd = "VitD"),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 1 #>   vitamin_d_status #>   <ord>            #> 1 Deficient        #> 2 Insufficient     #> 3 Sufficient       #> 4 NA df2 <- tibble::tibble(d = c(15, 280, 8, 65, NA))  # 280 is extreme by default (0-250)  vitamin_d_status(   data = df2,   col_map = list(vitd = "d"),   na_action = "warn",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   vitamin_d_status #>   <ord>            #> 1 Deficient        #> 2 Sufficient       #> 3 Deficient        #> 4 Sufficient       #> 5 NA

title: Vitamin D Status
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") vitamin_d_status(   data = tibble::tibble(VitD = c(15, 32)),   col_map = list(vitamin_d = "VitD"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> vitamin_d_status(): preparing inputs #> vitamin_d_status(): column map: vitamin_d -> 'VitD' #> vitamin_d_status(): results: vitamin_d_status 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   vitamin_d_status #>   <ord>            #> 1 Deficient        #> 2 Sufficient options(old_opt)

title: Vitamin D Status
text: medians ~150-250 without obvious outliers, verify units (nmol/L divided 2.5 get ng/mL). Set na_action = "omit" downstream steps handle NA statuses. Tighten extreme_rules assay range, leave check_extreme = FALSE values already QCed. Keep col_map named; missing empty mappings abort classed errors can assert tests.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: vitamin_markers() builds composite vitamin/endocrine ratios z-scores (VitD_Z, B12_Fol_Ratio, Ferr_TSat_R, Cort_DHEA_R, T_E2_Ratio, TSH_fT4_R, Retinol_Z, Toco_Lip_R, Mg_Zn_R, Cu_Zn_R) passes PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA. validates mappings, enforces missing-data policy, can scan/cap extremes, uses safe division avoid inf/NaN.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Create harmonized nutrient/endocrine summaries cohorts dashboards. QC pipelines missingness/extremes handled uniformly across many analytes. Downstream modeling expects standardized ratios/z-scores rather raw labs.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: keys required; defaults. col_map must name every key column: VitD, VitD_ref_mean, VitD_ref_sd B12, Folate Ferritin, TSat Cortisol, DHEAS Testosterone, Estradiol TSH, free_T4 Retinol, Retinol_ref_mean, Retinol_ref_sd Tocopherol, Total_lipids PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA Magnesium, Zinc, Copper Columns must exist coercible numeric. Non-finite values become NA; coercion introduces NAs warns.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: na_action = c("keep","omit","error") (default “keep”): policy missing required inputs. omit drops rows; error aborts. na_warn_prop = 0.2: debug-level verbose enabled, flags columns >=20% missing. check_extreme = FALSE: enable plausible-range scanning (broad defaults per analyte). extreme_action = c("warn","cap","error","ignore") (default “warn”): --bounds values. cap truncates range warns; error aborts. extreme_rules = NULL: override bounds keyed required keys column names. verbose = FALSE: progress summaries via hm_inform().

what: How inputs are validated and cleaned
title: Vitamin Markers
text: hm_validate_inputs() ensures data data frame keys mapped; missing maps abort scoped errors. mapped column coerced numeric; NA introduced warned; non-finite set NA. NA policy applied across required columns; omit output shrinks rows fully observed required inputs. Extreme scanning (enabled) uses key- column-based bounds; depending extreme_action, outliers warned, capped, aborted, ignored. Safe division sets zero denominators NA emits consolidated warning listing affected ratios.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Tibble : - Ratios/z-scores: VitD_Z, B12_Fol_Ratio, Ferr_TSat_R, Cort_DHEA_R, T_E2_Ratio, TSH_fT4_R, Retinol_Z, Toco_Lip_R, Mg_Zn_R, Cu_Zn_R - Pass-: PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA Output row count matches input unless na_action = "omit" removed rows.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Basic run Scan cap extremes
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   VitD = c(50, 30), VitD_ref_mean = c(40, 40), VitD_ref_sd = c(5, 5),   B12 = c(300, 280), Folate = c(15, 12),   Ferritin = c(100, 80), TSat = c(0.25, 0.22),   Cortisol = c(200, 180), DHEAS = c(100, 90),   Testosterone = c(12, 10), Estradiol = c(120, 110),   TSH = c(2, 2.5), free_T4 = c(14, 13),   Retinol = c(0.8, 0.7), Retinol_ref_mean = c(0.9, 0.9), Retinol_ref_sd = c(0.2, 0.2),   Tocopherol = c(30, 28), Total_lipids = c(3, 2.8),   PIVKA_II = c(5, 6), VitC = c(60, 55), Homocysteine = c(10, 11), MMA = c(0.3, 0.35),   Magnesium = c(0.8, 0.78), Zinc = c(15, 14), Copper = c(15, 14) )  cm <- as.list(names(df)); names(cm) <- names(df)  vitamin_markers(   data = df,   col_map = cm,   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 14 #>   VitD_Z B12_Fol_Ratio Ferr_TSat_R Cort_DHEA_R T_E2_Ratio TSH_fT4_R Retinol_Z #>    <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1      2          20          400            2     0.1        0.143      -0.5 #> 2     -2          23.3        364.           2     0.0909     0.192      -1   #> # ℹ 7 more variables: Toco_Lip_R <dbl>, PIVKA_II <dbl>, VitC <dbl>, #> #   Homocysteine <dbl>, MMA <dbl>, Mg_Zn_R <dbl>, Cu_Zn_R <dbl> df2 <- df # Force a few implausible values df2$TSat[2] <- 2.0 df2$Zinc[1] <- 2000  vitamin_markers(   data = df2,   col_map = cm,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 14 #>   VitD_Z B12_Fol_Ratio Ferr_TSat_R Cort_DHEA_R T_E2_Ratio TSH_fT4_R Retinol_Z #>    <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1      2          20           400           2     0.1        0.143      -0.5 #> 2     -2          23.3          80           2     0.0909     0.192      -1   #> # ℹ 7 more variables: Toco_Lip_R <dbl>, PIVKA_II <dbl>, VitC <dbl>, #> #   Homocysteine <dbl>, MMA <dbl>, Mg_Zn_R <dbl>, Cu_Zn_R <dbl>

title: Vitamin Markers
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") vitamin_markers(   data = df,   col_map = cm,   verbose = TRUE ) #> vitamin_markers(): preparing inputs #> vitamin_markers(): column map: VitD -> 'VitD', VitD_ref_mean -> 'VitD_ref_mean', VitD_ref_sd -> 'VitD_ref_sd', B12 -> 'B12', Folate -> 'Folate', Ferritin -> 'Ferritin', TSat -> 'TSat', Cortisol -> 'Cortisol', DHEAS -> 'DHEAS', Testosterone -> 'Testosterone', Estradiol -> 'Estradiol', TSH -> 'TSH', free_T4 -> 'free_T4', Retinol -> 'Retinol', Retinol_ref_mean -> 'Retinol_ref_mean', Retinol_ref_sd -> 'Retinol_ref_sd', Tocopherol -> 'Tocopherol', Total_lipids -> 'Total_lipids', PIVKA_II -> 'PIVKA_II', VitC -> 'VitC', Homocysteine -> 'Homocysteine', MMA -> 'MMA', Magnesium -> 'Magnesium', Zinc -> 'Zinc', Copper -> 'Copper' #> vitamin_markers(): results: VitD_Z 2/2, B12_Fol_Ratio 2/2, Ferr_TSat_R 2/2, Cort_DHEA_R 2/2, T_E2_Ratio 2/2, TSH_fT4_R 2/2, Retinol_Z 2/2, Toco_Lip_R 2/2, PIVKA_II 2/2, VitC 2/2, Homocysteine 2/2, MMA 2/2, Mg_Zn_R 2/2, Cu_Zn_R 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 14 #>   VitD_Z B12_Fol_Ratio Ferr_TSat_R Cort_DHEA_R T_E2_Ratio TSH_fT4_R Retinol_Z #>    <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1      2          20          400            2     0.1        0.143      -0.5 #> 2     -2          23.3        364.           2     0.0909     0.192      -1   #> # ℹ 7 more variables: Toco_Lip_R <dbl>, PIVKA_II <dbl>, VitC <dbl>, #> #   Homocysteine <dbl>, MMA <dbl>, Mg_Zn_R <dbl>, Cu_Zn_R <dbl> options(old_opt)

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Incomplete unnamed col_map aborts; ensure every key mapped. denominators zero (e.g., TSat = 0), expect warning NA ratios; address upstream unexpected. Align units across inputs; unit conversion performed ratios/z-scores. Narrow extreme_rules lab ranges catch cap assay-specific outliers. Use na_action = "omit" downstream models handle propagated NAs.

what: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Installation Package overview use HealthMarkers Function--function guide Column mapping Handle missing data computing Verbose diagnostics information Vignettes Contributing Citation License

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: HealthMarkers comprehensive R toolkit computing, standardising, summarising clinical research biomarkers routine laboratory phenotypic data. provides 50 specialist functions covering insulin sensitivity indices, cardiovascular risk scores, inflammatory aging clocks, frailty indices, psychiatric rating scales, alternate-biofluid panels, much . accessible unified dispatcher, all_health_markers(). Full documentation, function reference, vignettes available package website:https://sufyansuleman.github.io/HealthMarkers/ One interface, many markers. single all_health_markers() call returns glycaemic, lipid, liver, renal, pulmonary, inflammatory, hormonal, bone, psychiatric, nutritional markers one wide tibble. Safe default. NA handling, input validation, column-name inference, range-capping built . Failed marker groups skipped warning, never crashing pipeline. Reproducible. Explicit col_map arguments map column names expected internal keys, silent renaming. Reference-backed. Every function cites primary paper. Full bibliography inst/REFERENCES.bib. 46 vignettes worked clinical examples included.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Optional packages unlock additional marker groups: optional packages absent, dependent groups skipped safely; running verbose = TRUE shows groups computed skipped () summary message.
code: # From CRAN install.packages("HealthMarkers")  # Development version from GitHub remotes::install_github("sufyansuleman/HealthMarkers") install.packages(c("CVrisk", "rspiro", "PooledCohort", "QRISK3",                    "RiskScorescvd", "di", "mice", "missForest"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Use want compute many marker groups one call receive everything back single wide tibble appended original data. argument accepts following group keys: Pass = "" run every group (groups requiring unavailable optional packages silently skipped).
code: library(HealthMarkers)  labs <- data.frame(   age  = 52,  sex  = "M",   G0   = 5.8, I0   = 14.2,   TC   = 5.6, LDL_c = 3.4, HDL_c = 1.1, TG = 2.1,   ALT  = 38,  AST  = 30,   BMI   = 30.1,   SBP  = 138, DBP  = 88,   WC    = 98,   eGFR = 74,  UACR = 18 )  results <- all_health_markers(   data    = labs,   which   = c("glycemic", "lipid", "liver", "renal", "kidney_kfre",               "mets", "obesity_metrics"),   verbose = TRUE )  # results is the original data + all computed columns as one wide tibble names(results) insulin_fasting     insulin_ogtt        insulin_adipose     insulin_tracer_dxa glycemic            lipid               atherogenic         cvd_aip cvd_risk            cvd_ldl_particles   cvd_ascvd           cvd_qrisk3 cvd_scorescvd       cvd_stroke          liver               liver_fat mets metabolic_risk      pulmo               spirometry          bode saliva              sweat               urine               renal kidney_kfre         ckd_stage           nutrient            vitamin vitamin_d_status    hormone             inflammatory        iAge bone                frax                allostatic_load     oxidative frailty_index       charlson            sarc_f              psych nfl                 inflammatory_age    calcium_corrected   kyn_trp adiposity_sds       adiposity_sds_strat obesity_metrics     alm_bmi

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Use need fine-grained control, working specialist data (e.g. OGTT time-series, DXA outputs, spirometry), want inspect one marker family detail.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: fasting glucose + insulin data available; OGTT data multiple time points; DXA body-composition data; tracer clamp data.
code: # Fasting indices (HOMA-IR, QUICKI, Bennett, FIRI, ...) # Needs: G0 (fasting glucose mmol/L), I0 (fasting insulin mU/L) fasting_is(data, col_map = list(G0 = "glucose", I0 = "insulin"))  # OGTT indices (Matsuda, Stumvoll, Gutt, Avignon, ...) # Needs: G0/G30/G60/G120 and I0/I30/I60/I120 (mmol/L and mU/L) ogtt_is(data, col_map = list(G0="G0", G30="G30", G60="G60", G120="G120",                               I0="I0", I30="I30", I60="I60", I120="I120"))  # Adipose-tissue indices (LIRI, SPISE, VAI, LAP, ...) # Needs: BMI, WC, TG, HDL_c adipo_is(data, col_map = list(BMI="BMI", WC="WC", TG="TG", HDL_c="HDL_c"))  # DXA / tracer-based indices # Needs: fat mass, lean mass, Ra (palmitate/glycerol rates) tracer_dxa_is(data, col_map = list(fat_mass="FM_kg", lean_mass="LM_kg"))  # All insulin indices at once (fasting + OGTT + adipose + DXA) all_insulin_indices(data, col_map = list(...), normalize = "none",                     mode = "both",  # "IS" = sensitivity only, "IR" = resistance only                     na_action = "keep")

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: primary prevention cohorts; assessing 10-year MACE risk; comparing risk algorithms side--side.
code: # ASCVD Pooled Cohort Equations (10-yr or 30-yr) # Needs: age, sex, race, total_chol, HDL_c, sbp, bp_treated, smoker, diabetes, bmi # Requires: PooledCohort package cvd_risk_ascvd(data, year = 10)  # QRISK3 (UK population) # Requires: QRISK3 package cvd_risk_qrisk3(data)  # SCORE2 / SCORE2-OP (European) # Requires: RiskScorescvd package cvd_risk_scorescvd(data)  # 10-year stroke risk (Pooled Cohort) # Requires: PooledCohort package cvd_risk_stroke(data)  # Atherogenic index of plasma (log TG/HDL) cvd_marker_aip(data, col_map = list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c"))  # LDL particle number from ApoB cvd_marker_ldl_particle_number(data, col_map = list(ApoB = "ApoB"))  # Run all CVD algorithms at once and pick one model cvd_risk(data, model = "ALL")   # or "ASCVD", "QRISK3", "Stroke", etc.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: nephrology studies; CKD cohorts; monitoring progression.
code: # Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE) 2-year and 5-year probability # Needs: age, sex, eGFR (CKD-EPI, mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (mg/g) kidney_failure_risk(data, col_map = list(age="age", sex="sex",                                           eGFR="eGFR", UACR="UACR"))  # eGFR, creatinine ratios, BUN/creatinine, FE-Urea, etc. renal_markers(data, col_map = list(creatinine="Creat", age="age", sex="sex"))  # KDIGO CKD staging (G1–G5 × A1–A3) ckd_stage(data, col_map = list(eGFR="eGFR", UACR="UACR"))  # Urine panel: protein/creatinine ratio, microalbumin, osmolality urine_markers(data, col_map = list(urine_creat="UCr", urine_protein="UPr"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: respiratory epidemiology; COPD staging; lung-function studies.
code: # Individual spirometry z-scores and % predicted (GLI 2012) # Needs: FEV1, FVC; optionally age, height, sex, ethnicity # Requires: rspiro package spirometry_markers(data, col_map = list(fev1="FEV1", fvc="FVC",                                          age="age", height="ht_cm", sex="sex"))  # Simpler pulmonary ratios (FEV1/FVC, FEF25-75, etc.) no extra packages needed pulmo_markers(data)  # BODE index for COPD prognosis # Needs: FEV1% predicted, 6-minute walk distance, mMRC dyspnoea score, BMI bode_index(data, col_map = list(fev1_pct="FEV1pct", sixmwd="Walk6m",                                   mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: geriatric studies; surgical risk; comorbidity adjustment.
code: # Rockwood deficit-accumulation frailty index # Needs: a data frame of binary deficit columns (0/1) # Requires: di package frailty_index(data, deficit_cols = c("hypertension","diabetes","mobility_loss",...))  # Charlson Comorbidity Index (predicts 1-year mortality) # Needs: binary columns for each condition charlson_index(data, col_map = list(mi="MI", chf="CHF", diabetes="DM",...))  # SARC-F muscle function screening (5-item questionnaire) # Needs: 5 SARC-F item columns (strength, assistance walking, rise from chair, #         climb stairs, falls) sarc_f_score(data, col_map = list(strength="Q1", walking="Q2",...))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: mental health research; epidemiological surveys standardised questionnaires.
code: # Score one or many scales from item columns # Supported: PHQ-9, GAD-7, K6, K10, GHQ-12, WHO-5, ISI, MDQ, #            ASRS, BIS-11, SPQ, cognitive composite # # col_map is a nested list keyed by instrument name. # Internal item keys use zero-padded names (phq9_01 ... phq9_09). psych_markers(   data,   col_map = list(     phq9 = list(items = list(phq9_01 = "Q1", phq9_02 = "Q2", ...)),     gad7 = list(items = list(gad7_01 = "G1", gad7_02 = "G2", ...))   ),   which   = c("phq9", "gad7", "k10")  # choose scales to score )  # If your columns are already named phq9_01 ... phq9_09 etc., no col_map needed: phq9_score(data) gad7_score(data) k10_score(data)

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: paediatric cohorts (SDS); obesity epidemiology; sarcopenia assessment.
code: # Common obesity and adiposity indices # Needs: height, weight, WC, hip circumference; optionally age, sex obesity_indices(data)  # SDS z-scores from user-supplied reference mean and SD calc_sds(x = data$BMI, mean_ref = 22.5, sd_ref = 3.8)  # Sex-stratified SDS for multiple adiposity variables simultaneously adiposity_sds_strat(data, col_map = list(sex = "sex"),                     var_cols = c("BMI","WC","WHR"),                     ref_male = list(BMI = c(mean=25, sd=4)),                     ref_female = list(BMI = c(mean=24, sd=3.8)))  # Appendicular lean mass / BMI index (sarcopenia screening) alm_bmi_index(data, col_map = list(alm="ALM_kg", bmi="BMI", sex="Sex"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: osteoporosis research; fracture risk assessment; bone turnover monitoring.
code: # Bone turnover markers: P1NP, osteocalcin, CTX, NTX ratios bone_markers(data, col_map = list(P1NP="p1np", CTX="ctx_s"))  # FRAX 10-year fracture probability (hip and major osteoporotic fractures) # Needs: age, sex, BMI; optionally BMD T-score, prior fracture, steroid use, etc. frax_score(data, col_map = list(age="Age", sex="Sex", bmd_t="TScore"),            country = "UK")

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: immunology studies; biological age estimation; chronic disease research.
code: # Blood count-derived inflammatory ratios # Needs: neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, platelets inflammatory_markers(data, col_map = list(neut="NEUT", lymph="LYMPH",                                            mono="MONO", plt="PLT"))  # iAge inflammatory aging clock # Needs: a panel of inflammatory proteins (IL-6, CXCL9, etc.) iAge(data, col_map = list(IL6="IL6", CXCL9="CXCL9"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: stress research (saliva); cystic fibrosis / sweat testing; nephrology urine panels.
code: saliva_markers(data, col_map = list(cortisol_wake="C_wake",                                      cortisol_30="C_30min")) sweat_markers(data,  col_map = list(sweat_chloride="Cl_mmol")) urine_markers(data,  col_map = list(urine_creat="UCr", urine_na="UNa"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Every function accepts col_map argument named list mapping internal keys (function expects) actual column names (). multi-domain calls, use infer_cols() let package auto-map pattern matching:
code: # Internal key = "G0", your column is called "fasting_glucose_mmol" fasting_is(   data    = my_data,   col_map = list(G0 = "fasting_glucose_mmol", I0 = "insulin_uU_mL") ) # See what gets mapped and what's missing inferred <- infer_cols(my_data) str(inferred)  # Pass directly the dispatcher will use it all_health_markers(data = my_data, col_map = inferred)

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Impute missing values passing data marker function:
code: # Multiple imputation (mice) recommended for inference completed <- impute_mice(my_data, m = 5, seed = 42)  # Random-forest imputation (missForest) recommended for prediction completed <- impute_missforest(my_data)  # Simple mean / median / mode quick exploratory use completed <- impute_missing(my_data, method = "median")

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Set verbose = TRUE function see progress, NA counts, elapsed time: Enable globally entire session:
code: results <- all_health_markers(data = labs, which = c("lipid","liver"),                                verbose = TRUE) #> Column mapping summary: TC->TC (user), HDL_c->HDL_c (user) ... #> -> lipid #> -> liver #> all_health_markers(): summary - computed: lipid, liver | skipped/failed: none # levels: "none" (default), "inform" (progress only), "debug" (all internal steps) options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")

what: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices (QUICKI, VAI, LAP, TyG, TG/HDL, Belfiore) — adipo_is
title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices (QUICKI, VAI, LAP, TyG, TG/HDL, Belfiore) — adipo_is
text: Computes adipose-related insulin sensitivity/resistance indices fasting inputs. Expected input units (converted internally): Glucose G0 mmol/L -> mg/dL (* 18) Insulin I0 pmol/L -> muU/mL (/ 6) TG mmol/L -> mg/dL (* 88.57) HDL mmol/L -> mg/dL (* 38.67) Reported indices (higher magnitude negative "_inv" values implies worse adipose IR): Revised_QUICKI = 1 / (log10(I0 (muU/mL)) + log10(G0 (mg/dL)) + log10(FFA (mmol/L))) VAI (sex-specific; inverted VAI_*_inv larger negative = worse) TG_HDL_C_inv = -(TG/HDL) mg/dL TyG_inv = -ln(TG (mg/dL) * G0 (mg/dL) / 2) LAP (sex-specific; inverted) McAuley_index = exp(2.63 - 0.28 ln(I0 (muU/mL)) - 0.31 ln(TG (mmol/L))) Adipo_inv = -(FFA * I0 (muU/mL)) Belfiore_inv_FFA = - 2 / (I0 (muU/mL) * FFA + 1)

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices (QUICKI, VAI, LAP, TyG, TG/HDL, Belfiore) — adipo_is

Reserved

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices (QUICKI, VAI, LAP, TyG, TG/HDL, Belfiore) — adipo_is
text: Katz , Nambi SS, Mather K, Baron AD, Follmann DA, Sullivan G, Quon MJ (2000). “Quantitative Insulin Sensitivity Check Index: Simple, Accurate Method Assessing Insulin Sensitivity Humans.” Journal Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 85(7), 2402–2410. doi:10.1210/jcem.85.7.6661 . ; Amato MC, Giordano C, Galia M, Criscimanna , Vitabile S, Midiri M, Galluzzo (2010). “Visceral Adiposity Index: Reliable Indicator Visceral Fat Function Associated Cardiometabolic Risk.” Diabetes Care, 33(4), 920–922. doi:10.2337/dc09-1825 . ; Kahn HS (2005). “Lipid Accumulation Product Performs Better Body Mass Index Indicator Cardiovascular Risk Women.” BMC Cardiovascular Disorders, 5(1), 26. doi:10.1186/1471-2261-5-26 . ; Guerrero-Romero F, Simental-Mendía LE, González-Ortiz M, Martínez-Abundis E, Ramos-Zavala MG, Hernández-González , Jacques-Camarena O, Rodríguez-Morán M (2010). “Product Triglycerides Glucose, Simple Measure Insulin Sensitivity. Comparison Euglycemic-Hyperinsulinemic Clamp.” Journal Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 95(7), 3347–3351. doi:10.1210/jc.2010-0288 . ; Dobiášová M, Frohlich JJ (2001). “Plasma Parameter Log(TG/HDL-C) Atherogenic Index: Correlation Lipoprotein Particle Size Esterification Rate ApoB-Lipoprotein-Depleted Plasma.” Clinical Biochemistry, 34(7), 583–588. doi:10.1016/S0009-9120(01)00263-6 . ; Belfiore F, Iannello S, Volpicelli G (1998). “Insulin Sensitivity Indices Calculated Basal OGTT-Related Insulin Glucose Levels.” Molecular Genetics Metabolism, 63(2), 134–141. doi:10.1006/mgme.1997.2658 . ; Raynaud E, Pérez-Martin , Brun J, Benhaddad AA, Mercier J (1999). “Fasting Plasma Insulin Insulin Resistance Indices.” Diabetes & Metabolism, 25(6), 524–532. DOI identified Crossref/PubMed 2026-03-16; see URL, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Fasting+Plasma+Insulin++Insulin+Resistance+Indices.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices (QUICKI, VAI, LAP, TyG, TG/HDL, Belfiore) — adipo_is
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   G0 = c(5.2, 6.1),      # mmol/L   I0 = c(60, 110),       # pmol/L   TG = c(1.2, 1.8),      # mmol/L   HDL_c = c(1.3, 1.0),   # mmol/L   FFA = c(0.4, 0.6),     # mmol/L   waist = c(85, 102),    # cm   bmi = c(24, 31)        # kg/m^2 ) cm <- as.list(names(df)); names(cm) <- names(df) out <- adipo_is(df, cm, verbose = FALSE, na_action = "keep") head(out) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   Revised_QUICKI VAI_Men_inv VAI_Women_inv TG_HDL_C_inv TyG_inv LAP_Men_inv #>            <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1          0.389       -1.18         -1.80        -2.11   -8.51       -24   #> 2          0.324       -2.38         -3.62        -4.12   -9.08       -66.6 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: LAP_Women_inv <dbl>, McAuley_index <dbl>, #> #   Adipo_inv <dbl>, Belfiore_inv_FFA <dbl>

what: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: Computes standard deviation (z) scores anthropometric variables relative single (non-sex-stratified) reference set means standard deviations. Includes input validation, optional raw-value extreme screening/capping, configurable handling extreme SDS values, NA row policies, optional concise summary output, optional verbose progress messages.

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
code: adiposity_sds(   data,   col_map = NULL,   ref,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   extreme_action = c("cap", "NA", "error", "warn", "ignore"),   sds_cap = 6,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_rules = NULL,   diagnostics = FALSE,   warn_thresholds = list(na_prop = 0.05, extreme_prop = 0.01),   id_col = NULL,   return_summary = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   na_strategy = NULL,   extreme_strategy = NULL,   check_raw_extreme = NULL,   raw_extreme_rules = NULL )

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: data data.frame tibble containing measurement columns. col_map Optional named list mapping reference variable names column names data. NULL, identity mapping assumed (names(ref) must data). ref Named list element numeric vector names mean sd, e.g. list(BMI = c(mean = 23, sd = 4)). na_action One c("keep","omit","error") row handling mapped variable missing. extreme_action One c("cap","NA","error","warn","ignore") SDS values exceeding sds_cap. sds_cap Positive numeric; absolute cap used extreme_action = "cap". check_extreme Logical; TRUE run raw-value extreme screening using extreme_rules SDS computation. extreme_rules Optional named list c(min, max) ranges raw variables; NULL, broad defaults used common anthropometric measures. diagnostics Logical; TRUE emit informational/warning messages (coercions, missingness, extremes). FALSE suppresses non-critical warnings. warn_thresholds Named list optional elements na_prop (default 0.05) extreme_prop (default 0.01) used proportion-based diagnostic warnings. id_col Optional column name used verbose summaries. return_summary Logical; TRUE return list elements data, summary, warnings instead just SDS tibble. verbose Logical; TRUE print progress completion summaries. na_strategy Soft-deprecated alias na_action (provided na_action missing, used). extreme_strategy Soft-deprecated alias extreme_action (provided extreme_action missing, used). check_raw_extreme Soft-deprecated alias check_extreme. raw_extreme_rules Soft-deprecated alias extreme_rules.

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: tibble one <var>_SDS column per reference variable, list return_summary = TRUE.

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: SDS computed : (observed - mean) / sd. Rows removed na_action = 'omit'. Raw-value extreme screening (enabled) applied SDS computation. Extreme SDS handling (cap / warn / error / ignore) controlled extreme_action. Legacy argument aliases (na_strategy, extreme_strategy) soft-deprecated still accepted.

title: Compute sex-stratified standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds_strat
text: Computes sex-specific SDS (z-scores) selected anthropometric variables using reference means SDs provided separately males females.

title: Compute sex-stratified standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds_strat
code: adiposity_sds_strat(   data,   col_map,   ref,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("cap", "NA", "error"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   allow_partial = FALSE,   prefix = "",   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute sex-stratified standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds_strat
text: data Data frame tibble variables sex column col_map Named list mapping: sex: column name sex values ("M","F","m","f", 1, 2) vars: optional named list mapping reference variable names -> data column names. omitted, expects reference variable names exist data. ref Named list elements "M" "F". named list numeric vectors c(mean=, sd=) keyed variable name, e.g.: list( M = list(BMI = c(mean=23, sd=3.5), waist = c(mean=85, sd=10)), F = list(BMI = c(mean=21, sd=3.0), waist = c(mean=75, sd=9)) ) na_action One : "keep"  - keep rows NA (propagates outputs) "omit"  - drop rows NA required variable "error" - abort required variable NA check_extreme Logical; TRUE, screen raw variables extremes SDS extreme_action One : "cap"   - winsorize bounds "NA"    - set --range NA "error" - abort --range extreme_rules Optional named list c(min, max) per variable (raw scale). NULL, broad defaults applied common measures (BMI, waist, weight, height, hip, WC, HC). allow_partial TRUE, skip variables absent data (warning); FALSE error prefix Optional prefix output columns (default "") verbose Logical; TRUE emit progress via package logger; default logging controlled options(healthmarkers.verbose)

title: Compute sex-stratified standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds_strat
text: tibble one SDS column per retained variable: varname_SDS, varname original variable name (optionally prefixed prefix).

title: Compute sex-stratified standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds_strat
code: ref <- list(   M = list(BMI = c(mean=24.5, sd=3.8), waist = c(mean=88, sd=12)),   F = list(BMI = c(mean=22.1, sd=4.2), waist = c(mean=76, sd=11)) ) df <- data.frame(BMI=c(25.2,21.8,27.1), waist=c(85,72,95), sex=c("M","F","M")) adiposity_sds_strat(   df,   col_map = list(sex = "sex", vars = list(BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist")),   ref = ref ) #> adiposity_sds_strat(): preparing inputs (2 vars, 3 rows) #> adiposity_sds_strat(): column map: BMI -> 'BMI', waist -> 'waist' #> adiposity_sds_strat(): results: BMI_SDS 3/3, waist_SDS 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  0.184     -0.25  #> 2 -0.0714    -0.364 #> 3  0.684      0.583

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: Computes composite Allostatic Load (AL) score flagging biomarkers exceed user-specified high-risk thresholds (strict > multiple biomarkers; inclusive >= one biomarker). Aligned HM-CS v3: structured validation, diagnostic control, verbose reporting, optional summary output.

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
code: allostatic_load(   data,   thresholds,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("cap", "NA", "error"),   sds_limit = 6,   return_summary = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: data data.frame tibble numeric biomarker columns. thresholds named list scalar numeric cutoffs (names must match columns). col_map optional named list mapping keys thresholds column names data. na_action one c("keep","omit","error") ("keep" treats NA zero contribution). check_extreme logical; scan columns name containing "sds" |value| > sds_limit. extreme_action one c("cap","NA","error") SDS-like extremes. sds_limit positive numeric cutoff SDS-like scan (default 6). return_summary logical; TRUE returns list(data, summary, warnings). verbose logical; print progress messages via hm_inform() (also gated options(healthmarkers.verbose)).

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: tibble AllostaticLoad list return_summary = TRUE.

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: API pattern note: Unlike HealthMarkers functions follow standard (data, col_map, ...) signature, allostatic_load() uses (data, thresholds, col_map = NULL, ...) thresholds domain-specific required argument inferred column names alone. result, function directly dispatchable standard all_health_markers() registry; custom wrapper supplies thresholds explicitly required.

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: McEwen BS, Stellar E (1993). “Stress Individual: Mechanisms Leading Disease.” Archives Internal Medicine, 153(18), 2093–2101. doi:10.1001/archinte.153.18.2093 . ; Seeman TE, Singer BH, Rowe JW, Horwitz RI, McEwen BS (1997). “Price Adaptation—Allostatic Load Health Consequences.” Archives Internal Medicine, 157(19), 2259–2268. doi:10.1001/archinte.1997.00440400111013 . ; Juster R, McEwen BS, Lupien SJ (2010). “Allostatic Load Biomarkers Chronic Stress Impact Health Cognition.” Neuroscience Biobehavioral Reviews, 35(1), 2–16. doi:10.1016/j.neubiorev.2009.10.002 . ; Wiley JF, Gruenewald TL, Karlamangla , Seeman TE (2016). “Modeling Multisystem Physiological Dysregulation.” Psychosomatic Medicine, 78(3), 290–301. doi:10.1097/PSY.0000000000000288 .

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: Calculates: AIP: Atherogenic Index Plasma = log10(TG / HDL_c) CRI_I: Castelli Risk Index = TC / HDL_c CRI_II: Castelli Risk Index II = LDL_c / HDL_c

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
code: atherogenic_indices(   data,   col_map,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   normalize = c("none", "log10"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: data data.frame/tibble lipid columns. col_map named list mapping keys columns, e.g. list(TG="TG", HDL_c="HDL_c", TC="TC", LDL_c="LDL_c"). na_action one c("keep","omit","error"). check_extreme logical; TRUE, screen inputs extremes using extreme_rules. extreme_action one c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA"). extreme_rules optional named list bounds per key column, c(min, max). normalize one c("none","log10"). Reserved; AIP always uses log10(TG/HDL_c). verbose logical; prints step messages via hm_inform TRUE.

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: tibble columns AIP, CRI_I, CRI_II

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: Behavior: Required keys: TG, HDL_c. Optional: TC, LDL_c. NA policy via na_action: "keep" (default), "omit" (drop rows NA used lipids), "error". Extreme screening via check_extreme extreme_action ("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA"). Default bounds (mg/dL) used screening: TG (0, 10000), HDL_c (0, 1000), LDL_c (0, 10000), TC (0, 10000). Note: indices unitless ratios; units cancel computations. Emits progress via hm_inform() verbose = TRUE package option enables logs.

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: Dobiášová M (2004). “Atherogenic Index Plasma (AIP) log(Triglycerides/HDL-Cholesterol): Theoretical Practical Implications.” Clinical Chemistry, 50(7), 1113–1115. doi:10.1373/clinchem.2004.033175 . ; Castelli WP, Doyle JT, Gordon T, et al. (1977). “High-Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol Lipids Coronary Heart Disease: Framingham Study.” American Journal Medicine, 62(5), 707–714. doi:10.1016/0002-9343(77)90874-9 .

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   TG = c(150, 200),   HDL_c = c(50, 40),   TC = c(200, 220),   LDL_c = c(120, 150) ) cm <- list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c", TC = "TC", LDL_c = "LDL_c") atherogenic_indices(df, col_map = cm, verbose = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>     AIP CRI_I CRI_II #>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 0.477   4     2.4  #> 2 0.699   5.5   3.75

title: BODE Index (BMI, Obstruction, Dyspnea, Exercise capacity) — bode_index
text: data data.frame/tibble required columns. col_map named list keys: fev1_pct (fev1 fev1_pred) fev1_pp; plus sixmwd, mmrc, bmi. Example minimal: list(fev1_pct="FEV1pct", sixmwd="Walk_m", mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI") Example derive: list(fev1="FEV1", fev1_pred="FEV1_pred", sixmwd="Walk_m", mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI") Example spirometry_markers: list(fev1_pp="fev1_pp", sixmwd="Walk_m", mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI") na_action one c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). check_extreme logical; TRUE scan extreme values. extreme_action one c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA"). extreme_rules named list bounds (c(lo,hi)) fev1_pct,sixmwd,mmrc,bmi. Defaults: fev1_pct c(10,140), sixmwd c(50,800), mmrc c(0,4), bmi c(10,60). verbose logical; TRUE emits messages.

what: Compute Bone Health & Body-Composition Markers (HM-CS v3) — bone_markers
title: Compute Bone Health & Body-Composition Markers (HM-CS v3) — bone_markers
text: Given DXA, anthropometry, optional bone-turnover markers, computes: OSTA: (weight - age) x 0.2 ALMI: Appendicular Lean Mass Index = ALM / height^2 FMI: Fat Mass Index = FM / height^2 BMD_Tscore: (BMD - ref_mean) / ref_sd (col_map + data) passes : TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin.

title: Compute Bone Health & Body-Composition Markers (HM-CS v3) — bone_markers
code: bone_markers(   data,   col_map,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   check_extreme = FALSE,   sds_limit = 6,   extreme_action = c("cap", "NA", "error"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute Bone Health & Body-Composition Markers (HM-CS v3) — bone_markers
text: data data.frame tibble subject-level DXA/anthropometry data. col_map Named list mapping keys column names. Required keys: age, weight, height, ALM, FM, BMD, BMD_ref_mean, BMD_ref_sd Optional (passed-present found data): TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin. na_action One "keep", "omit", "error" controlling missing/non-finite input values treated. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan mapped columns whose key names contain "sds" (case-insensitive) absolute values > sds_limit. sds_limit Positive numeric; SDS magnitude limit used check_extreme TRUE. extreme_action One "cap", "NA", "error" handling extreme SDS-like values. verbose Logical; TRUE, emits progress messages via hm_inform().

title: Compute Bone Health & Body-Composition Markers (HM-CS v3) — bone_markers
text: tibble columns: OSTA, ALMI, FMI, BMD_Tscore, optionally TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin (order).

title: Compute Bone Health & Body-Composition Markers (HM-CS v3) — bone_markers
text: Notes: Units: height meters; ALM, FM, weight kilograms; BMD g/cm^2; ALMI/FMI kg/m^2. Non-finite values treated NA; division zero prevented input checks.

title: Compute Bone Health & Body-Composition Markers (HM-CS v3) — bone_markers

Assessment Fracture Risk Application Screening Postmenopausal Osteoporosis, volume 843 Technical Report Series. World Health Organization. DOI report; see URL, https://iris..int/handle/10665/39142.
