code: library(tibble) df <- tibble(   age = c(60, 72), weight = c(65, 50), height = c(1.65, 1.58),   ALM = c(18.2, 14.7), FM = c(22.0, 20.5),   BMD = c(0.95, 0.80), BMD_ref_mean = c(1.00, 1.00), BMD_ref_sd = c(0.12, 0.12) ) col_map <- list(   age = "age", weight = "weight", height = "height",   ALM = "ALM", FM = "FM", BMD = "BMD",   BMD_ref_mean = "BMD_ref_mean", BMD_ref_sd = "BMD_ref_sd" ) bone_markers(df, col_map) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>    OSTA  ALMI   FMI BMD_Tscore   TBS   HSA  PINP   CTX  BSAP Osteocalcin #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1   1    6.69  8.08     -0.417    NA    NA    NA    NA    NA          NA #> 2  -4.4  5.89  8.21     -1.67     NA    NA    NA    NA    NA          NA

what: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
title: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
text: Computes per-variable SDS (x - mean) / sd using supplied reference statistics. Includes input validation, NA/error handling, data quality warnings, verbose progress via package logger (hm_inform), aligned HM-CS v3.

title: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
code: calc_sds(   data,   vars,   ref,   id_col = NULL,   sds_cap = 6,   na_strategy = c("omit", "error", "keep"),   extreme_strategy = c("cap", "warn", "error", "NA"),   warn_thresholds = list(na_prop = 0.05, extreme_prop = 0.01),   return = c("data", "list"),   verbose = FALSE,   na_action = NULL,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = NULL )

title: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
text: data data.frame/tibble containing variables. vars Character vector variable names data compute SDS . Must non-empty unique. ref data.frame columns: variable, mean, sd supplying reference statistics variable listed vars. Must contain exactly one row per requested variable, finite mean sd > 0. id_col Optional character scalar naming ID column data (used messages duplicate-ID notes). sds_cap Numeric scalar; absolute cap SDS extreme_strategy = "cap". Must positive. Default 6. na_strategy One c("omit","error","keep"): "omit": drop rows missing values across vars (default) "error": stop missing values among vars "keep": keep rows; SDS missing inputs NA extreme_strategy One c("cap","warn","error","NA"): "cap": cap |SDS| sds_cap warn (default) "warn": keep extreme SDS, warn "error": stop |SDS| > sds_cap "NA": set extreme SDS NA warn_thresholds Named list controlling warnings (proportions \([0,1]\)): na_prop: warn proportion rows NA among vars exceeds (default 0.05) extreme_prop: warn proportion extreme SDS (cells) exceeds (default 0.01) return One c("data","list"). "data" returns tibble added [var]_sds columns (default). "list" returns list components data, summary, warnings (backward compatible). verbose Logical; TRUE, emit progress via hm_inform(). Also controlled options(healthmarkers.verbose = "none"|"inform"|"debug"). na_action Optional HM-CS alias na_strategy (accepted values: keep/omit/error; used provided). check_extreme Logical; TRUE, enables SDS extreme handling (alias legacy behavior; defaults TRUE implementation). extreme_action Optional HM-CS alias extreme_strategy (accepted values: cap/NA/error; used provided).

title: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
text: return = "data" (default): tibble added [var]_sds columns. return = "list": list : data: tibble added SDS columns summary: list input/output row counts, omitted rows, total extremes, per-variable summary warnings: character vector warning messages emitted

title: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
text: default, returns tibble added [var]_sds columns (tidyverse-friendly). backward compatibility, can request previous list output.

title: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
text: Cole TJ, Green PJ (1992). “Smoothing Reference Centile Curves: LMS Method Penalized Likelihood.” Statistics Medicine, 11(10), 1305–1319. doi:10.1002/sim.4780111005 . ; de Onis M, World Health Organization (2006). Child Growth Standards: Methods Development. World Health Organization. ISBN 924154693X, DOI report; see ISBN/URL, https://www..int/publications//item/924154693X. ; Kuczmarski RJ, Ogden CL, Grummer-Strawn LM, et al. (2000). “CDC Growth Charts: United States.” Technical Report 314, National Center Health Statistics. DOI CDC technical report; see URL, https://www.cdc.gov/growthcharts/cdc_charts.htm.

title: Calculate Standard Deviation Scores (SDS; z-scores) for health markers — calc_sds
code: ref <- data.frame(   variable = c("bmi","sbp"),   mean     = c(25, 120),   sd       = c(4, 15) ) df <- data.frame(   id  = 1:6,   bmi = c(24, 30, NA, 29, 10, 26),   sbp = c(118, 200, 119, 121, 500, 120) ) out_tbl <- calc_sds(   data = df,   vars = c("bmi","sbp"),   ref = ref,   id_col = "id",   na_strategy = "omit",   extreme_strategy = "cap",   sds_cap = 6,   verbose = FALSE ) #> Warning: calc_sds(): high NA proportion among `vars`: 16.7% of rows #> Warning: Capped 1 SDS beyond +/-6 for `sbp` #> Warning: calc_sds(): high proportion of extreme SDS: 10.00% of computed cells

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index (CCI) — charlson_index
code: charlson_index(   data,   col_map = list(mi = "mi", chf = "chf", pvd = "pvd", stroke = "stroke", dementia =     "dementia", copd = "copd", rheum = "rheum", ulcer = "ulcer", mild_liver =     "mild_liver", diabetes = "diabetes", diab_comp = "diab_comp", hemiplegia =     "hemiplegia", renal = "renal", cancer = "cancer", leukemia = "leukemia", lymphoma =     "lymphoma", sev_liver = "sev_liver", metastatic_cancer = "metastatic_cancer", hiv =     "hiv"),   verbose = FALSE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL )

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index (CCI) — charlson_index
text: data data.frame tibble binary indicators (0/1) comorbidity. col_map Named list mapping keys columns data: mi, chf, pvd, stroke, dementia, copd, rheum, ulcer, mild_liver, diabetes, diab_comp, hemiplegia, renal, cancer, leukemia, lymphoma, sev_liver, metastatic_cancer, hiv. verbose Logical; TRUE, emits progress messages. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs plausible ranges (binary 0-1). extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA"). extreme_rules Optional overrides (applied per key); default bounds c(0,1) .

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index (CCI) — charlson_index
text: tibble one column: charlson_index (integer total score; NA required input NA na_action != "omit").

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index (CCI) — charlson_index
text: Charlson Index predicts 10-year mortality summing weighted comorbidities. implement canonical 19-condition, weight scheme: 1 point: myocardial infarction, congestive heart failure, peripheral vascular disease, cerebrovascular disease (stroke), dementia, chronic pulmonary disease (COPD), rheumatologic disease, peptic ulcer disease 2 points: hemiplegia/paraplegia, moderate/severe renal disease, malignancy (non-metastatic), leukemia, lymphoma, diabetes complications 3 points: moderate/severe liver disease 6 points: metastatic solid tumor, AIDS/HIV avoid double counting paired conditions, following use maximum applicable weight: Diabetes: max(1 * diabetes without complications, 2 * diabetes complications) Liver disease: max(1 * mild liver disease, 3 * moderate/severe liver disease) Cancer: max(2 * non-metastatic solid tumor, 6 * metastatic solid tumor) Age points included can added separately needed.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index (CCI) — charlson_index
code: patient <- tibble::tibble(   mi=0, chf=0, pvd=0, stroke=0, dementia=0, copd=0, rheum=0, ulcer=0,   mild_liver=0, diabetes=0, diab_comp=1, hemiplegia=0, renal=1,   cancer=0, leukemia=0, lymphoma=0, sev_liver=0, metastatic_cancer=0, hiv=0 ) charlson_index(   patient,   col_map = as.list(stats::setNames(names(patient), names(patient))) ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   charlson_index #>            <int> #> 1              4

title: Albumin-Corrected Calcium — corrected_calcium
text: data data.frame tibble containing serum calcium albumin. col_map Named list calcium albumin indicating column names. units One c("auto","conventional","si"). "auto" attempts unit detection. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs plausible ranges (applied working units). extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA"). extreme_rules Optional overrides defaults working units: list(ca_mgdl = c(4, 15), alb_gdl = c(2, 5)). verbose Logical; TRUE, emits progress via hm_inform().

title: Albumin-Corrected Calcium — corrected_calcium
text: tibble one column: corrected_calcium (numeric, mg/dL conventional input mmol/L SI / auto-SI input).

title: Albumin-Corrected Calcium — corrected_calcium
text: Payne formula (conventional units): Corrected Ca (mg/dL) = measured Ca (mg/dL) + 0.8 * (4.0 - albumin (g/dL)). inputs appear SI units (calcium mmol/L, albumin g/L), converted mg/dL g/dL (using 1 mmol/L ~= 4 mg/dL; 1 g/L = 0.1 g/dL) correction converted back mmol/L output.

title: Albumin-Corrected Calcium — corrected_calcium
text: Payne RB, Little AJ, Williams RB, Milner JR (1973). “Interpretation serum calcium patients abnormal serum proteins.” British Medical Journal, 4(5893), 643–646. doi:10.1136/bmj.4.5893.643 .

what: Calculate fasting-based insulin sensitivity indices — fasting_is
title: Calculate fasting-based insulin sensitivity indices — fasting_is
text: Compute 10 fasting indices glucose (mmol/L) insulin (pmol/L): Fasting_inv, Raynaud, HOMA_IR_inv, FIRI, QUICKI, Belfiore_basal, Ig_ratio_basal, Isi_basal, Bennett, HOMA_IR_rev_inv. Units converted internally: G0_mg = G0*18 (mg/dL), I0_u = I0/6 (muU/mL).

title: Calculate fasting-based insulin sensitivity indices — fasting_is
code: fasting_is(   data,   col_map,   normalize = c("none", "z", "inverse", "range", "robust"),   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "warn"),   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_limit = 1000,   extreme_action = c("cap", "NA", "error"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Calculate fasting-based insulin sensitivity indices — fasting_is
text: data Data frame required inputs. col_map Named list mapping required keys: G0: fasting glucose (mmol/L) I0: fasting insulin (pmol/L) normalize One : "none","z","inverse","range","robust". na_action One : "keep"  (retain rows; indices become NA inputs missing/non-finite) "omit"  (drop rows missing/non-finite required inputs) "error" (abort required input missing/non-finite) "warn"  (emit warning rows missing inputs, keep ) check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan computed indices large magnitudes. extreme_limit Positive numeric threshold used check_extreme = TRUE. extreme_action One : "cap","NA","error". verbose Logical; TRUE, emit progress/completion messages.

title: Calculate fasting-based insulin sensitivity indices — fasting_is
text: Tibble 10 columns (indices listed ).

title: Calculate fasting-based insulin sensitivity indices — fasting_is
text: Matthews DR, Hosker JP, Rudenski , Naylor BA, Treacher DF, Turner RC (1985). “Homeostasis Model Assessment: Insulin Resistance Beta-Cell Function Fasting Plasma Glucose Insulin Concentrations Man.” Diabetologia, 28(7), 412–419. doi:10.1007/BF00280883 . ; Katz , Nambi SS, Mather K, Baron AD, Follmann DA, Sullivan G, Quon MJ (2000). “Quantitative Insulin Sensitivity Check Index: Simple, Accurate Method Assessing Insulin Sensitivity Humans.” Journal Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 85(7), 2402–2410. doi:10.1210/jcem.85.7.6661 . ; Raynaud E, Pérez-Martin , Brun J, Benhaddad AA, Mercier J (1999). “Fasting Plasma Insulin Insulin Resistance Indices.” Diabetes & Metabolism, 25(6), 524–532. DOI identified Crossref/PubMed 2026-03-16; see URL, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Fasting+Plasma+Insulin++Insulin+Resistance+Indices. ; Avignon , Charles M, Rabasa-Lhoret R, et al. (1999). “Assessment Insulin Sensitivity Oral Glucose Tolerance Test Normal Subjects Insulin-Resistant Patients.” International Journal Obesity, 23(5), 512–517. doi:10.1038/sj.ijo.0800864 . ; Belfiore F, Iannello S, Volpicelli G (1998). “Insulin Sensitivity Indices Calculated Basal OGTT-Related Insulin Glucose Levels.” Molecular Genetics Metabolism, 63(2), 134–141. doi:10.1006/mgme.1997.2658 . ; Sluiter D, Erkelens DW, Reitsma WD, Doorenbos H (1976). “Glucose Tolerance Insulin Release: Mathematical Approach.” Diabetes, 25, 245–249. doi:10.2337/diabetes.25.4.245 . ; Hanson RL, Pratley RE, Bogardus C, Narayan KMV, Roumain J, Imperatore G, Fagot-Campagna , Pettitt DJ, Bennett PH, Knowler WC (2000). “Evaluation Simple Indices Insulin Sensitivity Insulin Secretion Use Epidemiologic Studies.” American Journal Epidemiology, 151(2), 190–198. doi:10.1093/oxfordjournals.aje.a010187 . ; Anderson RL, Hamman RF, Savage PJ, Saad MF, Laws , Kades WW, Sands RE, Cefalu WT (1995). “Exploration Simple Measures Insulin Resistance.” American Journal Epidemiology, 142(7), 724–732. doi:10.1093/aje/142.7.724 . ; Suleman S, Madsen AL, Ängquist LH, Schubert M, Linneberg , Loos RJF, Hansen T, Grarup N (2024). “Genetic Underpinnings Fasting Oral Glucose-stimulated Based Insulin Sensitivity Indices.” Journal Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 109(11), 2754–2763. doi:10.1210/clinem/dgae275 .

title: Calculate fasting-based insulin sensitivity indices — fasting_is
code: # Minimal example (units: G0 in mmol/L, I0 in pmol/L) df <- data.frame(G0 = c(5.2, 6.1, 4.8), I0 = c(60, 120, 80)) res <- fasting_is(df, col_map = list(G0 = "G0", I0 = "I0")) head(res) #> # A tibble: 3 × 10 #>   Fasting_inv Raynaud HOMA_IR_inv  FIRI QUICKI Belfiore_basal Ig_ratio_basal #>         <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1       -10         4       -41.6  37.4  0.146       0.00213          -0.107 #> 2       -20         2       -97.6  87.8  0.130       0.000910         -0.182 #> 3       -13.3       3       -51.2  46.1  0.142       0.00173          -0.154 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Isi_basal <dbl>, Bennett <dbl>, HOMA_IR_rev_inv <dbl>  # With NA handling and extreme checking df2 <- data.frame(G0 = c(5.0, NA), I0 = c(90, 150)) fasting_is(df2, col_map = list(G0 = "G0", I0 = "I0"), na_action = "keep",            check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_limit = 1e3, extreme_action = "cap") #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   Fasting_inv Raynaud HOMA_IR_inv  FIRI QUICKI Belfiore_basal Ig_ratio_basal #>         <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1         -15    2.67         -60    54  0.139        0.00148         -0.167 #> 2         -25    1.6           NA    NA NA           NA               NA     #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Isi_basal <dbl>, Bennett <dbl>, HOMA_IR_rev_inv <dbl>

what: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
title: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
text: Thin wrapper around di package's di() : Validates inputs arguments. Coerces tibbles base data.frames (di()'s class checks). Auto-selects numeric deficit columns cols = NULL (excluding age supplied). Optionally scans missing/--range values warnings errors. Provides step--step verbose output completion summary. Optionally returns tidy tibble instead original list.

title: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
code: frailty_index(   data,   cols = NULL,   invert = NULL,   rescale = TRUE,   age = NULL,   rescale.custom = NULL,   rescale.avoid = NULL,   bins = 7,   visible = FALSE,   na_action = c("ignore", "warn", "error", "keep", "omit"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = NULL,   extreme_action = c("warn", "ignore", "error", "cap", "NA"),   return = c("list", "data"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
text: data data.frame tibble health deficits (ideally binary/logical scaled \([0,1]\)). Non-binary numeric columns can rescaled di::di rescale = TRUE. cols Character vector deficit column names use. NULL (default), numeric columns used except age (supplied). invert Character vector column names whose values inverted di::di (e.g., higher values indicate better health). rescale Logical; TRUE, non-binary columns rescaled \([0,1]\) di::di. Default TRUE. age Optional name column holding age (used di plotting optional age-binned outputs; excluded auto-selected cols). rescale.custom Advanced argument passed di::di. See di::di documentation syntax. rescale.avoid Advanced argument passed di::di; see di::di documentation syntax. bins Integer; number age bins FI--age plots. Default 7. visible Logical; TRUE age provided, di draw plot (via plot.di()). Default FALSE. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","warn","ignore"). Controls handling missing values selected deficit columns. "keep" (backward-compatible alias "ignore") passes NAs di::di. "warn" emits warning keeps NAs (alias "keep" missingness warning). "omit" drops rows NA selected deficits computing. "error" stops NA detected. Default "ignore" (retained backward compatibility; equivalent "keep"). na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) high-missingness warning emitted (per column) na_action = "warn". Default 0.2. check_extreme NULL/TRUE/FALSE gate --range scan: NULL (default): legacy behavior (scan rescale = FALSE) TRUE: always scan selected deficits values < 0 > 1 di::di FALSE: never scan extremes extreme_action One "warn","ignore","error","cap","NA" --range handling scanning enabled. "cap": truncate \([0,1]\) "NA": set --range NA Default "warn". return One c("list","data"). "list" (default) returns original di::di result (backward compatible). "data" returns tibble one row per individual, columns: di (frailty index) plus selected deficit columns (post-capping applied). Age included present. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress completion summary. Default FALSE.

title: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
text: return = "list" (default): object returned di::di (typically list di columns). return = "data": tibble di selected columns.

title: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
text: Background Frailty Index (FI) computed proportion health deficits present individual across set candidate deficits. approach introduced formalized Rockwood Mitnitski subsequently standardized construction reporting.

title: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
text: Mitnitski AB, Mogilner AJ, Rockwood K (2001). “Accumulation Deficits Proxy Measure Aging.” Scientific World Journal, 1, 323–336. doi:10.1100/tsw.2001.58 .  Rockwood K, Mitnitski (2007). “Frailty Relation Accumulation Deficits.” Journals Gerontology Series , 62(7), 722–727. doi:10.1093/gerona/62.7.722 .  Searle SD, Mitnitski , Gahbauer EA, Gill TM, Rockwood K (2008). “Standard Procedure Creating Frailty Index.” BMC Geriatrics, 8, 24. doi:10.1186/1471-2318-8-24 . title: Compute Frailty (Deficit) Index using di::di with QA and verbose summaries — frailty_index
code: # Minimal example (runs only if the 'di' package is installed) if (requireNamespace("di", quietly = TRUE)) {   df <- data.frame(     age = c(70, 75, 80),     d1 = c(0, 1, 1),     d2 = c(0.2, 0.8, 1.0),     d3 = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE)   )   # Auto-select numeric deficits; returns list (di, columns)   res <- frailty_index(df, cols = NULL, age = "age", verbose = TRUE)   # Tidy tibble return   tb  <- frailty_index(df, cols = c("d1","d2","d3"), age = "age",                        return = "data", verbose = TRUE) } #> frailty_index(): preparing inputs (3 rows, 2 deficits, age provided) #> frailty_index(): column map: d1, d2 #> frailty_index(): results: di range [0.000, 1.000] (3 rows, 2 deficits) #> frailty_index(): preparing inputs (3 rows, 3 deficits, age provided) #> frailty_index(): column map: d1, d2, d3 #> frailty_index(): results: di 3/3

what: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
title: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
text: Given fasting labs anthropometry, computes: SPISE (Single-Point Insulin Sensitivity Estimator) METS_IR (Metabolic Score Insulin Resistance) prediabetes flag (HbA1c >= 42 mmol/mol) diabetes flag (HbA1c >= 48 mmol/mol) HOMA_CP (C-peptide-based HOMA-IR variant; operational formula, see notes) LAR (Leptin/Adiponectin Ratio) ASI (Adiponectin Sensitivity Index; adiponectin/insulin) TyG_index (Triglyceride-Glucose Index)

title: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
code: glycemic_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("ignore", "warn", "error", "keep", "omit"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
text: data data.frame tibble containing least: HDL_c (mmol/L), TG (mmol/L), BMI (kg/m^2) Optional present: glucose (mmol/L), HbA1c (mmol/mol), C_peptide (pmol/L), G0 (mmol/L), I0 (pmol/L), leptin (ng/mL), adiponectin (ng/mL). col_map Optional named list mapping keys column names data. Required keys: HDL_c, TG, BMI. Optional keys (present used): glucose, HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin. NULL (default), function uses columns named exactly keys. na_action One c("ignore","warn","error","keep","omit"). HM-CS aliases: keep == ignore; omit drops rows NA/non-finite used inputs computing markers. Default "ignore". na_warn_prop Proportion (0-1) threshold high-missingness warnings among used columns na_action = "warn". Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan selected input variables values outside plausible ranges defined extreme_rules. Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") controlling handle extremes check_extreme = TRUE. "cap", values truncated allowed range; "NA", --range values become NA. Default "warn". extreme_rules Named list numeric length-2 ranges inputs scan check_extreme = TRUE. Defaults broad medical plausibility ranges: HDL_c: c(0.1, 5), TG: c(0.1, 20), BMI: c(10, 80), glucose: c(2, 30), HbA1c: c(20, 200), C_peptide: c(0, 5000), G0: c(2, 30), I0: c(0, 3000), leptin: c(0, 200), adiponectin: c(0, 300). variables present data checked. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress completion summary. Default FALSE.

title: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
text: tibble columns: SPISE, METS_IR, prediabetes, diabetes, HOMA_CP, LAR, ASI, TyG_index

title: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
text: Assumed units (automatic conversion inputs except noted): HDL_c, TG: mmol/L (TyG internally converts TG mg/dL via 88.57) BMI: kg/m^2 glucose, G0: mmol/L (TyG internally converts glucose mg/dL via 18) HbA1c: mmol/mol C_peptide, I0: pmol/L (HOMA_CP uses -like conversion factor 6 insulin muU/mL; see notes) leptin, adiponectin: ng/mL Quality controls options: Input validation ensures required variables exist numeric-coercible. Non-numeric inputs coerced numeric warning (NAs introduced reported). Missing non-finite inputs handled via na_action. Logs divisions computed safely (non-positive arguments yield NA). Optional detection/handling extreme input values via check_extreme extreme_action. Verbose mode prints step--step progress completion summary. Optional marker detection: col_map = NULL (default), identity map 10 keys built automatically. seven optional keys (glucose, HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin) computed corresponding column exists data. verbose = TRUE, optional markers whose columns absent listed informational message caller knows derived metrics (e.g., prediabetes, HOMA_CP, LAR) NA. Notes HOMA_CP: function retains package's existing operational formula: HOMA_CP = (G0 (mmol/L) * (C_peptide (pmol/L) / 6)) / 22.5 mirrors HOMA-IR's structure using 6 pmol/muU scaling used insulin. Users verify unit conventions datasets; alternative C-peptide HOMA implementations exist (e.g., HOMA2-CP).

title: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
text: Paulmichl K, Hatunic M, Höbaus C, et al. (2016). “Modification Validation Triglyceride-–HDL Cholesterol Ratio Surrogate Insulin Sensitivity White Juveniles Adults without Diabetes Mellitus: Single Point Insulin Sensitivity Estimator (SPISE).” Clinical Chemistry, 62(9), 1211–1219. doi:10.1373/clinchem.2016.257436 . ; Bello-Chavolla OY, Almeda-Valdes P, García-Sánchez , et al. (2018). “METS-IR, novel score evaluate insulin sensitivity, predictive visceral adiposity incident type 2 diabetes.” European Journal Endocrinology, 178(5), 533–544. doi:10.1530/EJE-17-0883 . ; Frühbeck G, Catalan V, Rodríguez , Ramón Sánchez-Recalde Á, Becerril S, Sánchez-González Á, Baena N, Valentí-Azcárate F, Burrell MA, Salvador J (2019). “Adiponectin-leptin Ratio Functional Biomarker Adipose Tissue Inflammation.” Nutrients, 11(2), 454. doi:10.3390/nu11020454 . ; Matthews DR, Hosker JP, Rudenski , Naylor BA, Treacher DF, Turner RC (1985). “Homeostasis Model Assessment: Insulin Resistance Beta-Cell Function Fasting Plasma Glucose Insulin Concentrations Man.” Diabetologia, 28(7), 412–419. doi:10.1007/BF00280883 . ; Simental-Mendía LE, Rodríguez-Morán M, Guerrero-Romero F (2008). “Product Fasting Glucose Triglycerides Surrogate Identifying Insulin Resistance.” Metabolic Syndrome Related Disorders, 6(4), 299–304. doi:10.1089/met.2008.0034 . ; Furler SM, Gan SK, Poynten , Chisholm DJ, Campbell LV, Kriketos AD (2006). “Relationship Adiponectin Insulin Sensitivity Humans, Independent Lipid Availability.” Obesity, 14(2), 228–234. doi:10.1038/oby.2006.29 .

title: Calculate glycemic-, C-peptide-, and additional metabolic markers — glycemic_markers
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   HDL_c       = c(1.0, 1.3),   TG          = c(1.3, 2.0),   BMI         = c(24, 30),   glucose     = c(5.6, 7.1),   HbA1c       = c(44, 38),   C_peptide   = c(300, 500),   G0          = c(5.5, 6.2),   I0          = c(60, 120),   leptin      = c(10, 20),   adiponectin = c(8, 5) ) # Quiet defaults glycemic_markers(df) #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   SPISE METS_IR prediabetes diabetes HOMA_CP   LAR    ASI TyG_index #>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>    <int>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  8.10     NA            1        0    12.2  1.25 0.133       8.67 #> 2  5.79    318.           0        0    23.0  4    0.0417      9.33 # Warn on missingness and scan for extremes with capping glycemic_markers(df,   na_action = "warn", na_warn_prop = 0.1,   check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE ) #> glycemic_markers(): preparing inputs #> glycemic_markers(): column map: HDL_c -> 'HDL_c', TG -> 'TG', BMI -> 'BMI' #> glycemic_markers(): results: SPISE 2/2, METS_IR 1/2, prediabetes 2/2, diabetes 2/2, HOMA_CP 2/2, LAR 2/2, ASI 2/2, TyG_index 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   SPISE METS_IR prediabetes diabetes HOMA_CP   LAR    ASI TyG_index #>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>    <int>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  8.10     NA            1        0    12.2  1.25 0.133       8.67 #> 2  5.79    318.           0        0    23.0  4    0.0417      9.33

what: Compute a suite of hormone ratio markers with QA and verbose summaries — hormone_markers
title: Compute a suite of hormone ratio markers with QA and verbose summaries — hormone_markers
text: Ratios computed: FAI = (total_testosterone / SHBG) * 100 LH_FSH = LH / FSH E2_P = estradiol / progesterone T3_T4 = free_T3 / free_T4 ARR = aldosterone / renin Ins_Glu = insulin / glucagon GH_IGF1 = GH / IGF1 PRL_T = prolactin / total_testosterone CAR_slope = (cortisol_30 - cortisol_0) / 30

title: Compute a suite of hormone ratio markers with QA and verbose summaries — hormone_markers
code: hormone_markers(   data,   col_map,   na_action = c("ignore", "warn", "error", "keep", "omit"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute a suite of hormone ratio markers with QA and verbose summaries — hormone_markers
text: data Data frame tibble mapped hormone inputs. col_map Named list mapping required keys column names: total_testosterone, SHBG, LH, FSH, estradiol, progesterone, free_T3, free_T4, aldosterone, renin, insulin, glucagon, GH, IGF1, prolactin, cortisol_0, cortisol_30. na_action One "ignore","warn","error","keep","omit". HM-CS: keep == ignore; omit drops rows NA used inputs. na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) high-missingness warnings na_action="warn". Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs --range values (see extreme_rules). Default FALSE. extreme_action One "warn","cap","error","ignore","NA" check_extreme=TRUE. "cap" truncates range; "NA" sets --range NA. extreme_rules Optional list c(min,max) per key override defaults. verbose Logical; print progress completion summary.

title: Compute a suite of hormone ratio markers with QA and verbose summaries — hormone_markers
text: Tibble nine ratio markers.

title: Compute a suite of hormone ratio markers with QA and verbose summaries — hormone_markers
code: df <- data.frame(   TT = c(15, 12), SHBG = c(40, 35), LH = c(5, 6), FSH = c(4, 5),   E2 = c(100, 120), Prog = c(0.5, 0.6), fT3 = c(4.5, 4.2),   fT4 = c(15, 14), Aldo = c(200, 180), Renin = c(10, 12),   Ins = c(60, 70), Gluc = c(8, 9), GH = c(1.2, 1.0),   IGF1 = c(180, 160), Prl = c(10, 12), Cort0 = c(400, 380),   Cort30 = c(600, 580) ) col_map <- list(   total_testosterone = "TT", SHBG = "SHBG", LH = "LH", FSH = "FSH",   estradiol = "E2", progesterone = "Prog", free_T3 = "fT3",   free_T4 = "fT4", aldosterone = "Aldo", renin = "Renin",   insulin = "Ins", glucagon = "Gluc", GH = "GH", IGF1 = "IGF1",   prolactin = "Prl", cortisol_0 = "Cort0", cortisol_30 = "Cort30" ) hormone_markers(df, col_map = col_map) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>     FAI LH_FSH  E2_P  E2_T T3_T4   ARR Ins_Glu GH_IGF1 PRL_T CAR_slope #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  37.5   1.25   200  6.67   0.3    20    7.5  0.00667 0.667      6.67 #> 2  34.3   1.2    200 10      0.3    15    7.78 0.00625 1          6.67

what: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
title: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
text: Implements linear proxy immunosenescence based key inflammatory biomarkers, following approach introduced Sayed et al. inflammatory aging clock (iAge). simplified iAge computed weighted sum C-reactive protein (CRP), interleukin-6 (IL6), tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNFa).

title: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
code: iAge(   data,   col_map,   weights = c(CRP = 0.33, IL6 = 0.33, TNFa = 0.34),   verbose = FALSE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL )

title: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
text: data data.frame tibble containing biomarker columns mapped col_map. col_map Named list mapping: CRP  -> column name C-reactive protein (mg/L) IL6  -> column name interleukin-6 (pg/mL) TNFa -> column name tumor necrosis factor-alpha (pg/mL) weights Named numeric vector weights marker (must sum 1). Defaults c(CRP = 0.33, IL6 = 0.33, TNFa = 0.34). verbose Logical; TRUE, prints stepwise progress completion summary. Default FALSE. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn") controlling missing inputs affect iAge: "keep": return NA rows required marker NA (default; consistent functions). "omit": ignore NAs weighted sum (treat missing markers 0). "error": abort required marker contains NA. "ignore": alias "omit". "warn": alias "omit" emits missingness warnings (per na_warn_prop). na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) high-missingness warning emitted na_action = "warn". Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs values outside plausible ranges. Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") controlling extremes detected. "warn": issue warning, modify values (default check_extreme = TRUE). "cap": truncate values allowed range warn. "error": abort detection. "ignore": nothing. "NA": set --range values NA. extreme_rules Optional named list numeric ranges c(min, max) CRP, IL6, TNFa. NULL, broad defaults used.

title: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
text: tibble one column: iAge (numeric): computed inflammatory age index.

title: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
text: default, missing inputs omitted sum (consistent prior behavior). Optional diagnostics can warn high missingness scan extreme values, ability cap, warn, error extremes. Verbose mode prints step--step progress final summary. Assumed units (automatic unit conversion): CRP: mg/L IL6: pg/mL TNFa: pg/mL Note: original iAge model Sayed et al. (Nature Aging, 2021) multi-marker machine learning model. function provides simple, linear proxy using three canonical inflammatory biomarkers. identical original published iAge inspired rationale. Default extreme ranges (extreme_rules = NULL): CRP: 0 300 mg/L IL6: 0 1,000 pg/mL TNFa: 0 1,000 pg/mL deliberately broad. Adjust extreme_rules fit cohort.

title: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
text: Sayed N, others (2021). “inflammatory aging clock (iAge) predicts multimorbidity, immunosenescence, frailty cardiovascular aging.” Nature Aging, 1, 598–610. doi:10.1038/s43587-021-00082-y .

title: Compute a simplified Inflammatory Age Index (iAge) with QA and verbose summaries — iAge
code: library(tibble) df <- tibble(   CRP  = c(1.2, 3.5, NA),  # mg/L   IL6  = c(2.0, 4.1, 1.5), # pg/mL   TNFa = c(1.0, 1.8, 0.9)  # pg/mL ) # Default behavior (omit NAs in row-wise sum) iAge(   df,   col_map = list(CRP = "CRP", IL6 = "IL6", TNFa = "TNFa") ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  1.40 #> 2  3.12 #> 3 NA     # Keep NA if any marker missing in a row iAge(   df,   col_map = list(CRP = "CRP", IL6 = "IL6", TNFa = "TNFa"),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  1.40 #> 2  3.12 #> 3 NA     # Scan and cap extreme values iAge(   df,   col_map = list(CRP = "CRP", IL6 = "IL6", TNFa = "TNFa"),   check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_action = "cap", verbose = TRUE ) #> iAge(): preparing inputs #> iAge(): column map: CRP -> 'CRP', IL6 -> 'IL6', TNFa -> 'TNFa' #> iAge(): results: iAge 2/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  1.40 #> 2  3.12 #> 3 NA

text: data data.frame tibble containing missing values. method Character; one c("mean","median","zero","constant"). cols Optional character vector column names impute. what: Infer column names from user data based on flexible patterns, with logging — infer_cols
title: Infer column names from user data based on flexible patterns, with logging — infer_cols
text: Given data.frame named mapping spec (e.g., list(G0 = NULL, I0 = NULL)), infer source column names key using set regex patterns. can supply patterns "preferred" names deterministically resolve ambiguous matches. structured log kept can written disk. Exact-name matching helper used all_health_markers() related wrappers. picks first matching candidate key, logs decisions via hm_inform() verbose = TRUE, errors required keys resolved.

title: Infer column names from user data based on flexible patterns, with logging — infer_cols
code: infer_cols(   data,   map,   verbose = TRUE,   log_file = NULL,   patterns = NULL,   prefer = NULL,   strategy = c("error", "prefer", "first", "stable"),   strict = TRUE,   ignore_case = TRUE,   fuzzy = FALSE,   max_distance = 0.1,   return = c("map", "list") )  hm_infer_cols(data, patterns, required_keys = names(patterns), verbose = FALSE)

title: Infer column names from user data based on flexible patterns, with logging — infer_cols
text: data Data frame whose column names scanned. map Named list names target keys (e.g., "G0","I0","TG") values NULL (infer) user-supplied column name (keep -). verbose Logical; TRUE, emits hm_inform() messages matches unresolved keys. log_file Optional file path; supplied, human-readable mapping log written . patterns Named list character vectors, giving candidate column names key (first match wins). prefer Optional named list character vectors preferred column names key, used resolve multiple matches deterministically. Matching case-insensitive exact provided names. strategy One c("error","prefer","first","stable") controlling resolution multiple candidates. Default "error" (backward compatible). "prefer": use prefer names first; else fall back "stable" tie-break. "first": take first match data's column order. "stable": choose shortest name, alphabetical. strict Logical; TRUE (default), missing matches error. FALSE, missing matches leave map[[key]] NULL issue warning. ignore_case Logical; pass grep(ignore.case = ...). Default TRUE. fuzzy Logical; TRUE regex matches found, attempt fuzzy match agrep using max_distance. Default FALSE. max_distance Numeric \([0,1]\) passed agrep fuzzy = TRUE. Default 0.1. return One c("map","list"). "map" (default) invisibly returns filled mapping list. "list" returns list(map = ..., log = tibble) auditing. required_keys Character vector keys must resolve; otherwise error raised.

title: Infer column names from user data based on flexible patterns, with logging — infer_cols
text: default, invisibly returns filled map. return = "list", returns list(map = named list, log = tibble). Named list mapping keys column names; unresolved non-required keys become NA_character_.

title: Infer column names from user data based on flexible patterns, with logging — infer_cols
text: helper produces col_map can pass HealthMarkers functions (e.g., fasting_is(), lipid-derived indices). Backward compatibility: default, strict = TRUE strategy = "error" keep prior behavior: Error match found. Error multiple candidates found. can opt smarter resolution via strategy = "prefer" "first".

title: Infer column names from user data based on flexible patterns, with logging — infer_cols
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   fasting_glucose = c(5.5, 6.1),   fasting_insulin = c(60, 88),   TG = c(120, 150),   `HDL-c` = c(50, 45),   age = c(55, 60) ) spec <- list(G0 = NULL, I0 = NULL, TG = NULL, HDL_c = NULL) # Backward-compatible: strict and "error" strategy res1 <- infer_cols(df, spec, verbose = FALSE) # Prefer/resolve ties deterministically res2 <- infer_cols(df, spec, strategy = "prefer", verbose = TRUE) #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - G0 -> fasting_glucose (unique match) #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - I0 -> fasting_insulin (unique match) #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - TG -> TG (unique match) #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - HDL_c -> HDL-c (unique match) # Get structured log res3 <- infer_cols(df, spec, return = "list") #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - G0 -> fasting_glucose (unique match) #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - I0 -> fasting_insulin (unique match) #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - TG -> TG (unique match) #> HealthMarkers::infer_cols - HDL_c -> HDL-c (unique match)

title: Compute inflammatory indices (classic, eosinophil, or both) — inflammatory_markers
text: data data.frame tibble col_map named list mapping keys column names data. Keys: neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, platelets, WBC, CRP, albumin, eosinophils, ESR. panel one c("auto","classic","eos",""). "auto" uses presence eosinophils key. na_action one c("keep","omit","error"). Default "keep" propagates NA outputs inputs missing. "omit" drops rows NA required inputs. "error" aborts required inputs contain NA. check_extreme logical; TRUE, handle extremes per extreme_action extreme_action one c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") verbose logical; TRUE, prints progress messages via hm_inform

title: Compute inflammatory indices (classic, eosinophil, or both) — inflammatory_markers
text: tibble selected inflammatory indices

title: Compute inflammatory indices (classic, eosinophil, or both) — inflammatory_markers
text: Derived markers: NLR  = neutrophils / lymphocytes PLR  = platelets / lymphocytes LMR  = lymphocytes / monocytes dNLR = neutrophils / (WBC - neutrophils) WBC available SII  = platelets * neutrophils / lymphocytes SIRI = neutrophils * monocytes / lymphocytes AISI = neutrophils * monocytes * platelets / lymphocytes CRP_category: "low" (<1 mg/L), "moderate" (1-3 mg/L), "high" (>3 mg/L) CRP available Eosinophil-panel extras: NER = neutrophils / eosinophils; PIV = plateletsneutrophilsmonocytes/lymphocytes; CLR = CRP/lymphocytes; CAR = CRP/albumin; PCR = platelets/CRP; mGPS (CRP, albumin); ESR passthrough.

title: Compute inflammatory indices (classic, eosinophil, or both) — inflammatory_markers
text: Zahorec R (2001). “Ratio neutrophil lymphocyte counts–rapid simple parameter systemic inflammation stress.” Bratislavske lekarske listy, 102(1), 5–14. DOI identified; PMID: 11723675, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11723675/. ; Templeton AJ, others (2014). “Prognostic role neutrophil--lymphocyte ratio solid tumors: systematic review meta-analysis.” Journal National Cancer Institute, 106(6), dju124. doi:10.1093/jnci/dju124 . ; Hu B, others (2014). “Systemic Immune-Inflammation Index Predicts Prognosis Patients Curative Resection Hepatocellular Carcinoma.” Clinical Cancer Research, 20(23), 6212–6222. doi:10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-14-0442 . ; Qi Q, others (2016). “novel systemic inflammation response index (SIRI) predicting survival patients pancreatic cancer chemotherapy.” Cancer, 122(14), 2158–2167. doi:10.1002/cncr.30057 . ; Mammadova , Naurzvai D, others (2025). “Association systemic immune-inflammation index aggregate index systemic inflammation clinical status stable exacerbated COPD: single-center retrospective study.” Medicine, 104(39), e44589. doi:10.1097/MD.0000000000044589 . ; Proctor MJ, others (2011). “inflammation-based prognostic score (mGPS) predicts cancer survival independent tumour site: Glasgow Inflammation Outcome Study.” British Journal Cancer, 104(4), 726–734. doi:10.1038/sj.bjc.6606087 . ; Pearson TA, others (2003). “Markers inflammation cardiovascular disease: statement healthcare professionals CDC AHA.” Circulation, 107(3), 499–511. doi:10.1161/01.CIR.0000052939.59093.45 .

title: Compute inflammatory indices (classic, eosinophil, or both) — inflammatory_markers
code: df <- data.frame(   neutrophils = c(4, 2),   lymphocytes = c(2, 0),   monocytes   = c(0.5, 0.3),   platelets   = c(200, 150),   WBC         = c(7, 4.5),   CRP         = c(2.5, 0.8),   albumin     = c(40, 42),   eosinophils = c(0.2, 0.1),   ESR         = c(12, 15) ) cm <- list(   neutrophils = "neutrophils", lymphocytes = "lymphocytes", monocytes = "monocytes",   platelets = "platelets", WBC = "WBC", CRP = "CRP", albumin = "albumin",   eosinophils = "eosinophils", ESR = "ESR" ) # Classic panel (no eosinophils key) classic_cm <- cm; classic_cm$eosinophils <- NULL; classic_cm$ESR <- NULL inflammatory_markers(df, classic_cm, panel = "classic", na_action = "keep") #> Warning: inflammatory_markers(): zero denominators detected. #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR  dNLR   SII  SIRI  AISI CRP_category #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <ord>        #> 1     2   100     4  1.33   400     1   200 moderate     #> 2   Inf   Inf     0  0.8    Inf   Inf   Inf low          # Eosinophil panel inflammatory_markers(df, cm, panel = "eos", na_action = "keep",                      check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_action = "cap", verbose = TRUE) #> inflammatory_markers(): preparing inputs #> inflammatory_markers(): column map: neutrophils -> 'neutrophils', lymphocytes -> 'lymphocytes', monocytes -> 'monocytes', platelets -> 'platelets', CRP -> 'CRP' #> Warning: inflammatory_markers(): capped out-of-range extreme input values. #> inflammatory_markers(): results: NLR 2/2, PLR 2/2, LMR 2/2, NER 2/2, SII 2/2, SIRI 2/2, PIV 2/2, CLR 2/2, CAR 2/2, PCR 2/2, mGPS 2/2, ESR 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 12 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR   NER   SII  SIRI   PIV   CLR    CAR   PCR  mGPS   ESR #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl> #> 1     2   100  4        4   400   1     200  1.25 0.0625   80      0    12 #> 2     2   150  3.33     2   300   0.6    90  0.8  0.0190  188.     0    15

what: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
title: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
text: Compute 2- 5-year risk end-stage kidney disease using original 4-variable KFRE (Tangri et al., 2011) optional data-quality diagnostics, extreme-value handling, verbose progress reporting.

title: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
code: kidney_failure_risk(   data,   col_map = list(age = "age", sex = "sex", eGFR = "eGFR", UACR = "UACR"),   na_action = c("keep", "error", "omit", "warn"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
text: data data.frame tibble containing least columns mapped col_map. col_map Named list mapping: age  -> age years sex  -> sex code (1 = male, 2 = female) eGFR -> estimated GFR (mL/min/1.73 m^2) UACR -> urine albumin--creatinine ratio (mg/g) na_action One c("keep","error","omit","warn"). Default "keep" preserve previous behavior: "keep": propagate NA/NaN logs outputs. "error": abort required input contains missing values. "omit": drop rows NA required inputs computation. "warn": like "keep" emits high-missingness warnings. na_warn_prop Numeric \([0,1]\); per-variable threshold high-missingness warnings. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan --range values (see extreme_rules). Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") used check_extreme = TRUE. "warn": warn --range values (default). "cap": truncate range warn. "error": abort --range detected. "ignore": nothing. "NA": set --range input values NA computation. extreme_rules Optional named list numeric c(min,max) ranges c(age, eGFR, UACR). NULL, broad defaults used: age (18, 120), eGFR (1, 200), UACR (0.1, 10000) (mg/g). verbose Logical; TRUE, prints stepwise messages completion summary. Default FALSE.

title: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
text: tibble : KFRE_2yr risk (0-1) 2 years KFRE_5yr risk (0-1) 5 years

title: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
text: function preserves prior behavior default: Inputs taken -; NA values propagate outputs (na_action = "keep"). capping --range checks applied unless requested. Units (automatic conversion): age: years; sex: 1 = male, 2 = female eGFR: mL/min/1.73 m^2 UACR: mg/g (albumin--creatinine ratio) Details Prognostic index: PI = 0.220 x log(age) + (-0.556) x log(eGFR) + 0.451 x log(UACR) + 0.391 x (male) male = 1 sex == 1, else 0. Baseline survival: S0(2y) = 0.934, S0(5y) = 0.881 (Tangri 2011). Risks: KFRE_t = 1 - (S0_t ^ exp(PI)). 2016 JAMA study provides large, multinational validation KFRE humans.

title: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
text: Tangri N, Stevens LA, Griffith J, others (2011). “predictive model progression chronic kidney disease kidney failure.” JAMA, 305(15), 1553–1559. doi:10.1001/jama.2011.451 . ; Tangri N, Grams , Levey , others (2016). “Multinational assessment accuracy equations predicting risk kidney failure: meta-analysis.” JAMA, 315(2), 164–174. doi:10.1001/jama.2015.18202 . ; Gansevoort RT, Matsushita K, van der Velde M, Astor BC, others (2011). “Lower estimated GFR higher albuminuria associated adverse kidney outcomes. collaborative meta-analysis general high-risk population cohorts.” Kidney International, 80(1), 93–104. doi:10.1038/ki.2010.531 . ; Hundemer GL, Tangri N, Sood MM, Ramsay T, Bugeja , Brown PA, Clark EG, Biyani M, White CA, Akbari (2020). “Performance Kidney Failure Risk Equation Disease Etiology Advanced CKD.” Clinical Journal American Society Nephrology, 15(10), 1424–1432. doi:10.2215/CJN.03940320 .

title: Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE, 2- and 5-year risk) — kidney_failure_risk
code: library(tibble) df <- tibble(   age  = c(65, 72),   sex  = c(1, 2),          # 1 = male, 2 = female   eGFR = c(45, 22),        # mL/min/1.73 m^2   UACR = c(300, 1200)      # mg/g ) # Default behavior (NA propagate, no extreme checks) kidney_failure_risk(   data = df,   col_map = list(age = "age", sex = "sex", eGFR = "eGFR", UACR = "UACR") ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr #>      <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1    0.329    0.523 #> 2    0.536    0.759  # With diagnostics and capping # \donttest{ kidney_failure_risk(   data = df,   col_map = list(age = "age", sex = "sex", eGFR = "eGFR", UACR = "UACR"),   check_extreme = TRUE, extreme_action = "cap", verbose = TRUE ) #> kidney_failure_risk(): preparing inputs #> kidney_failure_risk(): column map: age -> 'age', sex -> 'sex', eGFR -> 'eGFR', UACR -> 'UACR' #> kidney_failure_risk(): results: KFRE_2yr 2/2, KFRE_5yr 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr #>      <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1    0.329    0.523 #> 2    0.536    0.759 # }

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan Ratio (KTR) — kyn_trp_ratio

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan Ratio (KTR) — kyn_trp_ratio
text: KTR calculated Kyn (nmol/L) divided Trp (mumol/L). Elevated KTR indicates increased tryptophan catabolism via kynurenine pathway, often reflecting inflammation cell-mediated immune activation. Inputs already Kyn (nmol/L) Trp (mumol/L).

title: Calculate lipid-panel markers, Visceral Adiposity Index (VAI), Lipid Accumulation Product (LAP), and TyG-BMI index — lipid_markers
text: data data.frame tibble containing lipid (optional anthropometry/glucose) data. col_map Named list mapping: TC    -> total cholesterol HDL_c -> HDL-C TG    -> triglycerides LDL_c -> (optional) LDL-C; missing, estimated via Friedewald ApoB, ApoA1 -> (optional) apolipoproteins waist -> (optional) waist circumference (cm) BMI   -> (optional) body mass index (kg/m^2) na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). keep/ignore: compute propagate NA outputs omit: drop rows NA required inputs (TC, HDL_c, TG) error: abort required input contains NA warn: like keep, emit missingness warnings check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs --range values. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") controlling extremes handled check_extreme=TRUE. "cap" truncates range; "NA" sets flagged values NA. extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) per key override defaults. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints messages computing markers.

what: Liver fat surrogates: HSI and NAFLD Liver Fat Score — liver_fat_markers
title: Liver fat surrogates: HSI and NAFLD Liver Fat Score — liver_fat_markers
text: Computes: HSI = 8 * (ALT/AST) + BMI + 2 (female) + 2 (diabetes) NAFLD-LFS = -2.89 + 1.18MetS + 0.45Type2DM + 0.15Insulin_u + 0.04AST - 0.94*(AST/ALT)

title: Liver fat surrogates: HSI and NAFLD Liver Fat Score — liver_fat_markers
text: data Data frame needed columns (see col_map). col_map Named list mapping: Required HSI: ALT, AST, BMI Optional direct inputs: sex, diabetes, MetS, insulin Optional derive MetS insulin: I0, waist, TG, HDL_c, sbp, bp_sys, bp_treated, glucose, G0 na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) high-missingness warnings na_action = "warn". Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan selected inputs plausible ranges. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") controlling extremes handled. extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) override defaults. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress.

what: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
title: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
text: Given routine labs anthropometry, computes: FLI      - Fatty Liver Index (Bedogni et al. 2006) NFS      - NAFLD Fibrosis Score (Angulo et al. 2007) APRI     - AST--Platelet Ratio Index FIB4     - Fibrosis-4 Index BARD     - BMI-AST/ALT-Diabetes score ALBI     - Albumin-Bilirubin score MELD_XI  - MELD excluding INR

title: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
code: liver_markers(   data,   col_map = list(BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist", TG = "TG", GGT = "GGT", age = "age", AST =     "AST", ALT = "ALT", platelets = "platelets", albumin = "albumin", diabetes =     "diabetes", bilirubin = "bilirubin", creatinine = "creatinine"),   verbose = FALSE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL )

title: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
text: data data.frame tibble containing liver anthropometry data. col_map Named list mapping keys -> column names data: BMI (kg/m^2), waist (cm), TG (mg/dL), GGT (U/L), age (years), AST (U/L), ALT (U/L), platelets (10^9/L), albumin (g/L), diabetes (0/1 logical), bilirubin (mg/dL), creatinine (mg/dL). verbose Logical; TRUE, prints stepwise messages final summary. Default FALSE. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") controlling missing-data policy. Default "keep" (preserves prior behavior). "keep": leave NAs; propagate outputs. "omit": drop rows NA required input. "error": abort required input contains NA. na_warn_prop Numeric \([0,1]\); per-variable threshold high-missingness warnings. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs --range values (see extreme_rules). Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore") extremes detected (used check_extreme = TRUE). "warn": warn (default), "cap": truncate allowed range, "error": abort, "ignore": nothing. extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) ranges keys col_map. NULL, broad defaults used.

title: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
text: tibble one column per marker: FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB4, BARD, ALBI, MELD_XI.

title: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
text: Enhancements: Robust input validation (columns present, types) informative errors. Configurable NA policy optional extreme-value scanning/capping. Data-quality warnings (high missingness, non-positive logs, zero denominators). Verbose stepwise progress completion summary. Units (automatic conversion): BMI: kg/m^2; Waist: cm; TG: mg/dL; GGT/AST/ALT: U/L; Platelets: 10^9/L; Albumin: g/L; Bilirubin: mg/dL; Creatinine: mg/dL. ALBI uses bilirubin mumol/L internally (converted bilirubin (mg/dL) * 17.1). Formulas FLI      = logistic(0.953ln(TG) + 0.139BMI + 0.718ln(GGT) + 0.053waist - 15.745) * 100 NFS      = -1.675 + 0.037age + 0.094BMI + 1.13diabetes + 0.99(AST/ALT) - 0.013platelets - 0.66albumin APRI     = (AST / 40) / platelets * 100; assumes AST upper limit normal = 40 U/L FIB-4    = (age * AST) / (platelets * sqrt(ALT)) BARD     = 1 BMI>=28, +1 AST/ALT>=0.8, +1 diabetes present; sum 0,1,2,3 ALBI     = 0.66log10(bilirubin (mumol/L)) - 0.0852albumin (g/L) MELD-XI  = 5.11ln(bilirubin (mg/dL)) + 11.76ln(creatinine (mg/dL)) + 9.44

title: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
text: Bedogni G, Bellentani S, Miglioli L, others (2006). “Fatty Liver Index: simple accurate predictor hepatic steatosis general population.” BMC Gastroenterology, 6, 33. doi:10.1186/1471-230X-6-33 .  Angulo P, Hui JM, Marchesini G, others (2007). “NAFLD fibrosis score: noninvasive system identifies liver fibrosis patients NAFLD.” Hepatology, 45(4), 846–854. doi:10.1002/hep.21496 .  Wai CT, Greenson JK, Fontana RJ, others (2003). “simple noninvasive index can predict significant fibrosis cirrhosis patients chronic hepatitis C.” Hepatology, 38(2), 518–526. doi:10.1053/jhep.2003.50346 .  Sterling RK, Lissen E, Clumeck N, others (2006). “Development simple noninvasive index predict significant fibrosis patients HIV/HCV coinfection (FIB-4).” Hepatology, 43(6), 1317–1325. doi:10.1002/hep.21178 .  Harrison SA, Oliver D, Arnold HL, others (2008). “Development validation simple NAFLD clinical scoring system identifying patients without advanced disease.” Gut, 57(10), 1441–1447. doi:10.1136/gut.2007.146019 .  Johnson PJ, Berhane S, Kagebayashi C, others (2015). “Assessment liver function patients hepatocellular carcinoma: ALBI grade.” Journal Clinical Oncology, 33(6), 550–558. doi:10.1200/JCO.2014.57.9151 .  Heuman DM, Abou-Assi SG, Habib , others (2006). “MELD-XI: rational approach "sickest first" liver transplantation cirrhotic patients requiring anticoagulant therapy.” Liver Transplantation, 13(1), 30–37. doi:10.1002/lt.20906 .

title: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) with validation and diagnostics — liver_markers
code: library(tibble) df <- tibble(   BMI           = 24,   waist         = 80,   TG = 150, # mg/dL   GGT           = 30,   age           = 45,   AST           = 25,   ALT           = 20,   platelets     = 250, # 10^9/L   albumin       = 42,  # g/L   diabetes      = FALSE,   bilirubin     = 1.0, # mg/dL   creatinine    = 0.9  # mg/dL ) liver_markers(df, verbose = TRUE) #> liver_markers(): preparing inputs #> liver_markers(): column map: BMI -> 'BMI', waist -> 'waist', TG -> 'TG', GGT -> 'GGT', age -> 'age', AST -> 'AST', ALT -> 'ALT', platelets -> 'platelets', albumin -> 'albumin', diabetes -> 'diabetes', bilirubin -> 'bilirubin', creatinine -> 'creatinine' #> liver_markers(): results: FLI 1/1, NFS 1/1, APRI 1/1, FIB4 1/1, BARD 1/1, ALBI 1/1, MELD_XI 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>     FLI   NFS  APRI  FIB4  BARD  ALBI MELD_XI #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  27.9 -27.5  0.25  1.01     1 -2.76    8.20  # \donttest{ # With extreme-value capping and diagnostics liver_markers(   df,   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_action = "cap",   verbose = TRUE ) #> liver_markers(): preparing inputs #> liver_markers(): column map: BMI -> 'BMI', waist -> 'waist', TG -> 'TG', GGT -> 'GGT', age -> 'age', AST -> 'AST', ALT -> 'ALT', platelets -> 'platelets', albumin -> 'albumin', diabetes -> 'diabetes', bilirubin -> 'bilirubin', creatinine -> 'creatinine' #> liver_markers(): results: FLI 1/1, NFS 1/1, APRI 1/1, FIB4 1/1, BARD 1/1, ALBI 1/1, MELD_XI 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>     FLI   NFS  APRI  FIB4  BARD  ALBI MELD_XI #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  27.9 -27.5  0.25  1.01     1 -2.76    8.20 # }

text: data Data frame containing questionnaire item columns. col_map Named list mapping canonical item IDs column names; defaults assume items already named. na_action handle rows missing items: keep, omit, error. missing_prop_max Maximum allowed proportion missing items per row score set NA. prefix Prefix output column names. symptom_cutoff Minimum symptom count positive screen. require_clustering Require clustering item == 1 positive. require_impairment Require impairment item == 1 positive. verbose Logical flag verbose messaging (reserved).

text: Compute four binary risk flags routine clinical measures: dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia (prediabetes-range glycemia) hypertension (BP >=95th percentile via z > 1.64)

title: Calculate metabolic risk feature flags (pediatric-friendly thresholds) — metabolic_risk_features
code: metabolic_risk_features(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Calculate metabolic risk feature flags (pediatric-friendly thresholds) — metabolic_risk_features
text: data data.frame tibble containing least numeric columns: chol_total, chol_ldl, chol_hdl, triglycerides (mmol/L) age_year (years) z_HOMA (standardized HOMA-IR) glucose (mmol/L) HbA1c (mmol/mol; IFCC units) bp_sys_z, bp_dia_z (BP z-scores) col_map Optional named list map required keys column names data. Keys: c("chol_total","chol_ldl","chol_hdl","triglycerides","age_year", "z_HOMA","glucose","HbA1c","bp_sys_z","bp_dia_z"). Default NULL (use names). na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn") controlling missing-data policy. "keep": keep NA; outputs become NA inputs NA. "omit": drop rows NA required input. "error": abort required input contains NA. "ignore"/"warn": aliases "keep"; "warn" also emits missingness diagnostics. na_warn_prop Numeric \([0,1]\); per-variable threshold high-missingness warnings. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs --range values (see extreme_rules). Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") used extremes detected (check_extreme = TRUE). "warn": warn (default), "cap": truncate range warn, "error": abort, "ignore": nothing, "NA": set flagged inputs NA. extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) ranges required keys. NULL, broad defaults used. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints stepwise messages final summary. Default FALSE.

title: Calculate metabolic risk feature flags (pediatric-friendly thresholds) — metabolic_risk_features
text: tibble four factor columns (levels c("0","1")): dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension

title: Calculate metabolic risk feature flags (pediatric-friendly thresholds) — metabolic_risk_features
text: default, behavior matches prior implementation: required columns validated, NA values kept (propagate outputs), extreme-value checks capping applied, tibble 0/1 factor flags returned. Units criteria (automatic unit conversion): Lipids (mmol/L): total cholesterol > 5.2 LDL-C > 3.4 HDL-C < 1.0 triglycerides > 1.1 (age 0-9) > 1.5 (age 10-19) => dyslipidemia = 1. Insulin resistance: z_HOMA > 1.28 (~=90th percentile) => insulin_resistance = 1. z_HOMA within-sample external z-score HOMA-IR. Hyperglycemia: fasting glucose (5.6, 6.9) mmol/L HbA1c (39, 47) mmol/mol => hyperglycemia = 1. Hypertension: either BP z-score > 1.64 (~=95th percentile) systolic diastolic => hypertension = 1.

title: Calculate metabolic risk feature flags (pediatric-friendly thresholds) — metabolic_risk_features
code: df <- data.frame(   chol_total = c(5.2, 6.4), chol_ldl = c(3.2, 4.1), chol_hdl = c(1.3, 1.0),   triglycerides = c(1.8, 2.5), age_year = c(45, 60), z_HOMA = c(0.5, 1.2),   glucose = c(5.5, 6.8), HbA1c = c(38, 46), bp_sys_z = c(0.2, 1.1),   bp_dia_z = c(0.1, 0.9) ) metabolic_risk_features(df) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 0            0                  0             0            #> 2 1            0                  1             0

title: Metabolic Syndrome Severity Score (MetSSS) — metss
text: Computes continuous metabolic syndrome severity z-score using sex- race-specific standardized components coefficients (factor-loading style). Behavior note: Parameters selected per-row based row's (race, sex) key. unique keys present data must matching entry params. Required columns (unit conversion performed): waist (cm), bp_sys (mmHg), bp_dia (mmHg) TG, HDL_c, glucose (mmol/L) sex (1=male, 2=female) race (one "NHW","NHB","HW","HA" accepted synonyms)

title: Metabolic Syndrome Severity Score (MetSSS) — metss
code: metss(   data,   params = list(NHW_M = list(intercept = -2.344, waist = c(mean = 94, sd = 12.4, coef =     0.846), TG = c(mean = 1.5, sd = 0.6, coef = 0.701), HDL = c(mean = 1.1, sd = 0.3,     coef = -0.663), glucose = c(mean = 5.3, sd = 0.6, coef = 0.658), MAP = c(mean = 97,     sd = 11, coef = 0.466)), NHW_F = list(intercept = -2.381, waist = c(mean = 89.7, sd =     14.8, coef = 0.817), TG = c(mean = 1.28, sd = 0.91, coef = 0.679), HDL = c(mean =     1.5, sd = 0.4, coef = -0.727), glucose = c(mean = 5.08, sd = 0.52, coef = 0.622), MAP     = c(mean = 91,       sd = 11, coef = 0.557)), NHB_M = list(intercept = -2.399, waist     = c(mean = 92.8, sd = 13.1, coef = 0.83), TG = c(mean = 1.18, sd = 0.75, coef =     0.551), HDL = c(mean = 1.27, sd = 0.37, coef = -0.598), glucose = c(mean = 5.55, sd =     0.85, coef = 0.702), MAP = c(mean = 98, sd = 13, coef = 0.564)), NHB_F =     list(intercept = -2.395, waist = c(mean = 96.4, sd = 16.4, coef = 0.858), TG = c(mean     = 1.14, sd = 0.7, coef = 0.57), HDL = c(mean = 1.36, sd = 0.39, coef = -0.634),     glucose = c(mean = 5.42, sd = 0.84,       coef = 0.687), MAP = c(mean = 95, sd = 13,     coef = 0.577)), HW_M = list(intercept = -2.377, waist = c(mean = 98.5, sd = 11.5,     coef = 0.864), TG = c(mean = 1.95, sd = 1.19, coef = 0.724), HDL = c(mean = 1.13, sd     = 0.3, coef = -0.62), glucose = c(mean = 5.67, sd = 0.9, coef = 0.624), MAP = c(mean     = 97, sd = 11, coef = 0.448)), HW_F = list(intercept = -2.388, waist = c(mean = 97.9,     sd = 14.2, coef = 0.858), TG = c(mean = 1.66, sd = 1.06, coef = 0.715), HDL = c(mean     = 1.29, sd = 0.35, coef = -0.657),       glucose = c(mean = 5.53, sd = 0.87, coef =     0.644), MAP = c(mean = 91, sd = 11, coef = 0.512))),   verbose = FALSE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   diagnostics = TRUE )

title: Metabolic Syndrome Severity Score (MetSSS) — metss
text: data data.frame / tibble. params Named list keyed "RACE_SEX" (e.g. "NHW_M"). element: list(intercept, waist, TG, HDL, glucose, MAP) component (except intercept) named numeric vector c(mean=, sd=, coef=). verbose Logical; print progress. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn") required-input NAs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion (0-1) high-missingness warning fires na_action='warn'. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; scan extreme values TRUE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") extremes detected. Default "warn". extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) inputs (waist, bp_sys, bp_dia, TG, HDL_c, glucose). diagnostics Logical; TRUE (default) emit value/range diagnostic warnings (negative, --range checks). Set FALSE suppress (e.g., tests also using check_extreme).

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: Utility used across HealthMarkers normalize numeric vectors several common schemes handling edge cases (constant vectors, -NA, non-finite values) robustly. NA positions preserved.

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
code: normalize_vec(   x,   method = c("none", "z", "inverse", "range", "robust"),   na_rm = TRUE,   feature_range = c(0, 1),   invnorm_denominator = c("n", "n+1", "blom"),   ties = c("average", "first", "last", "random", "max", "min"),   warn_constant = TRUE )

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: x numeric (numeric-coercible) vector. method One c("none","z","inverse","range","robust"). Default "none". na_rm Logical; remove NAs estimating statistics (mean, sd, etc.). Default TRUE. feature_range Numeric length-2 vector giving target range method = "range". Default c(0, 1). invnorm_denominator One c("n","n+1","blom") controlling denominator inverse-normal transform: "n": p = (r - 0.5) / n  (Rankit; default) "n+1": p = (r - 0.5) / (n + 1) "blom": p = (r - 3/8) / (n + 1/4)  (Blom, 1958) ties Ties method passed base::rank method = "inverse". One c("average","first","last","random","max","min"). Default "average". warn_constant Logical; TRUE, warn input constant zero vector (lower bound range) returned. Default TRUE.

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: numeric vector length x.

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: Methods: "none": return input -(coercion; fully backward compatible). "z": z-score (mean 0, sd 1). Constant vectors return zeros (non-NA entries). "range": min-max target interval (default \([0,1]\)). Constant vectors return lower bound (mapped zeros). "robust": median/MAD scaling. Constant vectors (MAD=0) return zeros. "inverse": rank-based inverse normal transform (normal scores).

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: Beasley TM, Erickson S, Allison DB (2009). Rank-based inverse normal transformations increasingly used, merited? Behav Genet, 39(2):214-227. Leys C, Ley C, Klein O, Bernard P, Licata L (2013). Detecting outliers: use standard deviation around mean, use median absolute deviation around median. Front Psychol, 4:241. doi:10.3389/fpsyg.2013.00241  Bland JM, Altman DG (1996). Standard deviations standard errors. BMJ, 313(7047):41-42.

what: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: Given data frame tibble routine biochemical labs, nutrient_markers() returns set widely used ratios, products, simple percentages summarize iron metabolism, protein status, omega-3 balance, renal excretion, mineral homeostasis, aromatic amino-acid patterns.

code: nutrient_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: data data frame tibble containing subject-level data. col_map Optional named list mapping variable keys (see Details) column names data. need supply keys ; markers missing inputs return NA. NULL, defaults identity mapping known keys. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") controlling missing-data policy across columns referenced col_map. "keep" (default): keep NA; outputs become NA inputs NA. "omit": drop rows NA used input column. "error": abort used input contains NA. na_warn_prop Numeric \([0,1]\); per-variable threshold high-missingness diagnostics used input columns. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan used input columns --range values (see extreme_rules). Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") used extremes detected (check_extreme = TRUE). Default "warn". "warn": warn, "cap": truncate range warn, "error": abort, "ignore": -op, "NA": set flagged inputs NA. extreme_rules Optional named list input keys c(min,max) ranges. NULL, broad defaults used (see Details). verbose Logical; TRUE, prints stepwise messages final summary via hm_inform. Default FALSE.

title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: tibble one row per input row columns: FerritinTS, AGR, Omega3Index, Mg_Cr_Ratio, GlycatedAlbuminPct, UA_Cr_Ratio, BUN_Cr_Ratio, Ca_x_Phosphate, AnionGap, Tyr_Phe_Ratio.

title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: Recognized markers (returned columns): FerritinTS: Ferritin / Transferrin saturation AGR: Albumin / Globulin, Globulin = Total protein - Albumin Omega3Index: EPA + DHA (percentage points) Mg_Cr_Ratio: Magnesium / Creatinine GlycatedAlbuminPct: (Glycated albumin / Albumin) x 100 UA_Cr_Ratio: Uric acid / Creatinine BUN_Cr_Ratio: BUN / Creatinine Ca_x_Phosphate: Calcium x Phosphate AnionGap: (Na + K) - (Cl + HCO3) Tyr_Phe_Ratio: Tyrosine / Phenylalanine Recognized col_map keys expected units (automatic conversion): ferritin: Serum ferritin (ng/mL) transferrin_sat: Transferrin saturation (%) albumin: Serum albumin (g/L) total_protein: Total serum protein (g/L) EPA: Red-cell EPA % total fatty acids DHA: Red-cell DHA % total fatty acids Mg: Serum magnesium (mmol/L) creatinine: Serum creatinine (umol/L) glycated_albumin: Glycated albumin (g/L) uric_acid: Serum uric acid (umol/L) BUN: Blood urea nitrogen (mg/dL) phosphate: Serum phosphate (mmol/L) calcium: Serum calcium (mmol/L) Na: Serum sodium (mmol/L) K: Serum potassium (mmol/L) Cl: Serum chloride (mmol/L) HCO3: Serum bicarbonate (mmol/L) Tyr: Serum tyrosine (umol/L) Phe: Serum phenylalanine (umol/L) Default extreme_rules (inputs) broad intended unit/entry checks: ferritin (0, 2000), transferrin_sat (0, 100), albumin (10, 60), total_protein (40, 100), EPA (0, 20), DHA (0, 20), Mg (0.2, 3), creatinine (20, 2000), glycated_albumin (0, 60), uric_acid (50, 1000), BUN (1, 150), phosphate (0.1, 5), calcium (0.5, 4), Na (100, 200), K (2, 8), Cl (70, 130), HCO3 (5, 45), Tyr (10, 300), Phe (20, 300).
