code: library(tibble) df <- tibble(   wt     = c(70, 80),  # kg   ht     = c(175, 165),# cm   waist  = c(80, 90),  # cm   hip    = c(100, 95), # cm   sex    = c(0, 1) ) obesity_indices(   df,   weight       = wt,   height       = ht,   waist        = waist,   hip          = hip,   sex          = sex,   weight_unit  = "kg",   height_unit  = "cm",   adjust_WHR   = TRUE,   include_RFM  = TRUE,   verbose      = TRUE ) #> obesity_indices(): preparing inputs #> obesity_indices(): column map: weight -> 'wt', height -> 'ht', waist -> 'waist', hip -> 'hip', sex -> 'sex' #> obesity_indices(): results: weight_kg 2/2, height_m 2/2, BMI 2/2, BMI_cat 2/2, WHR 2/2, WHRadjBMI 2/2, waist_to_height_ratio 2/2, waist_to_BMI_ratio 2/2, weight_to_height_ratio 2/2, AVI 2/2, BAI 2/2, ABSI 2/2, BRI 2/2, CI 2/2, RFM 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 15 #>   weight_kg height_m   BMI BMI_cat         WHR WHRadjBMI waist_to_height_ratio #>       <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <chr>         <dbl>     <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1        70     1.75  22.9 Normal weight 0.8           0                  45.7 #> 2        80     1.65  29.4 Overweight    0.947         0                  54.5 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: waist_to_BMI_ratio <dbl>, weight_to_height_ratio <dbl>, #> #   AVI <dbl>, BAI <dbl>, ABSI <dbl>, BRI <dbl>, CI <dbl>, RFM <dbl>

what: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: Given glucose & insulin 0, 30, 120 min (plus weight, BMI, age, sex), computes: Isi_120 Cederholm_index Gutt_index Avignon_Si0 Avignon_Si120 Avignon_Sim Modified_stumvoll Stumvoll_Demographics Matsuda_AUC Matsuda_ISI BigttSi Ifc_inv HIRI_inv Belfiore_isi_gly

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
code: ogtt_is(   data,   col_map,   normalize = "none",   verbose = FALSE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_limit = 1000,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA") )

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: data data.frame tibble containing least columns mapped col_map. col_map Named list mapping: G0, G30, G120 -> glucose 0, 30, 120 min (mmol/L) I0, I30, I120 -> insulin 0, 30, 120 min (pmol/L) weight -> body weight (kg) bmi -> body-mass index (kg/m^2) age -> age (years) sex -> sex (1 = male, 2 = female) normalize One c("none","z","inverse","range","robust") used normalize_vec(). verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages via hm_inform(). na_action One c("keep","omit","error") missing/non-finite required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion (0-1) high-missingness diagnostics (debug). Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan outputs |value| > extreme_limit. Default FALSE. extreme_limit Positive numeric magnitude threshold extremes. Default 1e3. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA") extremes detected. Default "warn".

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: tibble OGTT-based index columns listed .

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: Units assumed: OGTT glucose mmol/L (internally converted mg/dL via *18 select indices) OGTT insulin pmol/L (internally converted muU/mL via /6 select indices) weight kg; BMI kg/m^2; age years; sex coded 1 = male, 2 = female Notes Conversions mirror existing implementation preserve outputs. formulas intentionally use unconverted inputs (prior code). Logs safe: log(x) becomes NA x <= 0 non-finite.

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: Matsuda M, DeFronzo RA (1999). “Insulin Sensitivity Indices Obtained Oral Glucose Tolerance Testing: Comparison Minimal Model Assessment.” Diabetes Care, 22(9), 1462–1470. doi:10.2337/diacare.22.9.1462 . ; Gutt M, Davis CL, Spitzer SB, et al. (2000). “Validation Insulin Sensitivity Index (ISI\(_{0,120}\)) Derived Oral Glucose Tolerance Testing.” Diabetes Research Clinical Practice, 47(3), 177–184. doi:10.1016/S0168-8227(99)00116-3 . ; Stumvoll M, Mitrakou , Pimenta W, et al. (2000). “Use Oral Glucose Tolerance Test Assess Insulin Release Sensitivity.” Diabetes Care, 23(3), 295–301. doi:10.2337/diacare.23.3.295 . ; Hansen T, Drivsholm T, Urhammer SA, Palacios RR, et al. (2007). “BIGTT Test.” Diabetes Care, 30(2), 257–262. doi:10.2337/dc06-1240 . ; Avignon , Charles M, Rabasa-Lhoret R, et al. (1999). “Assessment Insulin Sensitivity Oral Glucose Tolerance Test Normal Subjects Insulin-Resistant Patients.” International Journal Obesity, 23(5), 512–517. doi:10.1038/sj.ijo.0800864 . ; Belfiore F, Iannello S, Volpicelli G (1998). “Insulin Sensitivity Indices Calculated Basal OGTT-Related Insulin Glucose Levels.” Molecular Genetics Metabolism, 63(2), 134–141. doi:10.1006/mgme.1997.2658 . ; Matthews DR, Hosker JP, Rudenski , Naylor BA, Treacher DF, Turner RC (1985). “Homeostasis Model Assessment: Insulin Resistance Beta-Cell Function Fasting Plasma Glucose Insulin Concentrations Man.” Diabetologia, 28(7), 412–419. doi:10.1007/BF00280883 . ; Suleman S, Madsen AL, Ängquist LH, Schubert M, Linneberg , Loos RJF, Hansen T, Grarup N (2024). “Genetic Underpinnings Fasting Oral Glucose-stimulated Based Insulin Sensitivity Indices.” Journal Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 109(11), 2754–2763. doi:10.1210/clinem/dgae275 .

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   G0 = 5.5, I0 = 60,   G30 = 7.8, I30 = 90,   G120 = 6.2, I120 = 50,   weight = 70, bmi = 24, age = 30, sex = 1 ) ogtt_is(   df,   col_map = list(     G0 = "G0", I0 = "I0",     G30 = "G30", I30 = "I30",     G120 = "G120", I120 = "I120",     weight = "weight", bmi = "bmi",     age = "age", sex = "sex"   ),   normalize = "none",   verbose = TRUE ) #> ogtt_is(): preparing inputs #> ogtt_is(): column map: G0 -> 'G0', I0 -> 'I0', G30 -> 'G30', I30 -> 'I30', G120 -> 'G120', I120 -> 'I120', weight -> 'weight', bmi -> 'bmi', age -> 'age', sex -> 'sex' #> ogtt_is(): results: Isi_120 1/1, Cederholm_index 1/1, Gutt_index 1/1, Avignon_Si0 1/1, Avignon_Si120 1/1, Avignon_Sim 1/1, Modified_stumvoll 1/1, Stumvoll_Demographics 1/1, Matsuda_AUC 1/1, Matsuda_ISI 1/1, BigttSi 1/1, Ifc_inv 1/1, HIRI_inv 1/1, Belfiore_isi_gly 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   Isi_120 Cederholm_index Gutt_index Avignon_Si0 Avignon_Si120 Avignon_Sim #>     <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1    10.8            1.10       2.67        9.62          10.2        9.93 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: Modified_stumvoll <dbl>, Stumvoll_Demographics <dbl>, #> #   Matsuda_AUC <dbl>, Matsuda_ISI <dbl>, BigttSi <dbl>, Ifc_inv <dbl>, #> #   HIRI_inv <dbl>, Belfiore_isi_gly <dbl>

what: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
text: Calls frailty_index() visible = TRUE; see frailty_index() arguments, validation, references.

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
code: plot_frailty_age(   data,   cols = NULL,   invert = NULL,   rescale = TRUE,   age = NULL,   rescale.custom = NULL,   rescale.avoid = NULL,   bins = 7,   na_action = c("ignore", "warn", "error", "keep", "omit"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = NULL,   extreme_action = c("warn", "ignore", "error", "cap", "NA"),   return = c("list", "data"),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
text: data data.frame tibble health deficits (ideally binary/logical scaled \([0,1]\)). Non-binary numeric columns can rescaled di::di rescale = TRUE. cols Character vector deficit column names use. NULL (default), numeric columns used except age (supplied). invert Character vector column names whose values inverted di::di (e.g., higher values indicate better health). rescale Logical; TRUE, non-binary columns rescaled \([0,1]\) di::di. Default TRUE. age Optional name column holding age (used di plotting optional age-binned outputs; excluded auto-selected cols). rescale.custom Advanced argument passed di::di. See di::di documentation syntax. rescale.avoid Advanced argument passed di::di; see di::di documentation syntax. bins Integer; number age bins FI--age plots. Default 7. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","warn","ignore"). Controls handling missing values selected deficit columns. "keep" (backward-compatible alias "ignore") passes NAs di::di. "warn" emits warning keeps NAs (alias "keep" missingness warning). "omit" drops rows NA selected deficits computing. "error" stops NA detected. Default "ignore" (retained backward compatibility; equivalent "keep"). na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) high-missingness warning emitted (per column) na_action = "warn". Default 0.2. check_extreme NULL/TRUE/FALSE gate --range scan: NULL (default): legacy behavior (scan rescale = FALSE) TRUE: always scan selected deficits values < 0 > 1 di::di FALSE: never scan extremes extreme_action One "warn","ignore","error","cap","NA" --range handling scanning enabled. "cap": truncate \([0,1]\) "NA": set --range NA Default "warn". return One c("list","data"). "list" (default) returns original di::di result (backward compatible). "data" returns tibble one row per individual, columns: di (frailty index) plus selected deficit columns (post-capping applied). Age included present. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress completion summary. Default FALSE.

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
text: object returned frailty_index() (di::di object return="list").

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
code: if (requireNamespace("di", quietly = TRUE)) {   df <- data.frame(age = c(70, 75, 80), d1 = c(0, 1, 1),     d2 = c(0.2, 0.8, 1.0), d3 = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE))   plot_frailty_age(df, cols = c("d1", "d2", "d3"), age = "age") }  #> $di #> [1] 0.3333333 0.5833333 1.0000000 #>  #> $columns #>   d1   d2 d3 #> 1  0 0.00  1 #> 2  1 0.75  0 #> 3  1 1.00  1 #>

what: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: Uses rspiro reference equations compute predicted normals, z-scores, percent predicted lower limits normal (LLN) FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC ratio.

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
code: pulmo_markers(   data,   equation = c("GLI", "GLIgl", "NHANES3"),   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: data data.frame tibble columns: age       (numeric): years sex       (character numeric): "male"/"female" (case-insensitive) codes 1/2 0/1 height    (numeric): cm m (auto-detected) ethnicity (character): e.g. "Caucasian", "African-American", "NE Asian", "SE Asian", "/Mixed" fev1      (numeric): observed FEV1 L fvc       (numeric): observed FVC L equation One c("GLI","GLIgl","NHANES3") (see rspiro details). GLIgl ignores ethnicity. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") handling missing values required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness warnings required inputs. Default 0.2. verbose Logical; TRUE prints progress messages completion summary.

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: tibble columns: fev1_pred, fev1_z,   fev1_pctpred, fev1_LLN fvc_pred,  fvc_z,    fvc_pctpred,  fvc_LLN fev1_fvc_ratio, fev1_fvc_pred, fev1_fvc_z, fev1_fvc_pctpred, fev1_fvc_LLN

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: Inputs validated, missingness handled via na_action, heights auto-detected cm height > 3; otherwise interpreted metres (automatic unit conversion beyond heuristic, preserving prior behavior).

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: Quanjer PH, Stanojevic S, Cole TJ, Baur X, Hall GL, Culver BH, et al. (2012). “Multi-ethnic reference values spirometry 3–95-yr age range: global lung function 2012 equations.” European Respiratory Journal, 40, 1324–1343. doi:10.1183/09031936.00080312 .  Hankinson JL, Odencrantz JR, Fedan KB (1999). “Spirometric reference values sample general U.S. population.” American Journal Respiratory Critical Care Medicine, 159, 179–187. doi:10.1164/ajrccm.159.1.9712108 .  Bowerman SD, Quanjer PH, others (2023). “GLI-Global update.” European Respiratory Journal, 61, 2201632. DOI resolvable Crossref 2026-03-16.

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
code: if (requireNamespace("rspiro", quietly = TRUE)) {   df <- data.frame(     age = c(40, 55), sex = c("male", "female"),     height = c(175, 162), ethnicity = c("Caucasian", "Caucasian"),     fev1 = c(3.5, 2.4), fvc = c(4.4, 3.1)   )   pulmo_markers(df) } #> # A tibble: 2 × 13 #>   fev1_pred fev1_z fev1_pctpred fev1_LLN fvc_pred  fvc_z fvc_pctpred fvc_LLN #>       <dbl>  <dbl>        <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>       <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      4.08 -1.13          85.8     3.23     5.05 -1.04         87.0    4.02 #> 2      2.62 -0.595         91.7     2.01     3.29 -0.410        94.2    2.54 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: fev1_fvc_ratio <dbl>, fev1_fvc_pred <dbl>, #> #   fev1_fvc_z <dbl>, fev1_fvc_pctpred <dbl>, fev1_fvc_LLN <dbl>

what: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: Given routine blood urine assays, renal_markers() computes: eGFR_cr: CKD-EPI creatinine equation (2009 variant; race factor retained preserve prior behavior) eGFR_cys: CKD-EPI cystatin C equation (cystatin_C provided) eGFR_combined: CKD-EPI combined creatinine+cystatin C (provided) BUN_Cr_ratio: Blood urea nitrogen / serum creatinine FE_Urea: Fractional excretion urea (%) NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP: pass-urinary injury markers (mapped)

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
code: renal_markers(   data,   col_map,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: data data.frame tibble renal lab data. col_map Named list mapping: creatinine -> serum creatinine (mg/dL) age -> age (years) sex -> sex indicator (1 = male, 0 = female). Also accepts "male"/"female". race -> race ("white", "black", ""). Also accepts common aliases. BUN -> blood urea nitrogen (mg/dL) optional cystatin_C -> serum cystatin C (mg/L) optional urea_serum -> serum urea (mg/dL) optional creatinine_urine -> urine creatinine (mg/dL) optional urea_urine -> urine urea (mg/dL) optional NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro, IL18, L_FABP -> urine injury markers na_action One c("keep","omit","error") handling missing values required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness warnings required inputs. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scans selected inputs --range values using simple heuristics. Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore") extremes detected. Default "warn". extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) bounds keyed input names (e.g., "creatinine","BUN","cystatin_C","urea_serum","creatinine_urine","urea_urine"). NULL, built-defaults used. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages completion summary.

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: tibble columns: eGFR_cr, eGFR_cys, eGFR_combined, BUN_Cr_ratio, FE_Urea, NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: Robust validation applied, including NA handling (na_action), high-missingness diagnostics, safe divisions consolidated zero-denominator warning, optional input extremes scan/cap. New arguments appended backward compatibility. Expected units (automatic conversion performed): creatinine (serum): mg/dL cystatin C (serum): mg/L BUN (serum): mg/dL urea_serum, urea_urine: mg/dL creatinine_urine: mg/dL

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: Levey , Stevens LA, Schmid CH, others (2009). “new equation estimate glomerular filtration rate.” Annals Internal Medicine, 150(9), 604–612. doi:10.7326/0003-4819-150-9-200905050-00006 . what: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
text: Computes: log_cortisol_wake (log-transformed waking cortisol) CAR_AUC           (Cortisol Awakening Response, trapezoidal AUC 0-60 min default) log_amylase       (log-transformed salivary alpha-amylase) saliva_glucose    (raw salivary glucose)

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
code: saliva_markers(   data,   col_map = list(cort1 = "saliva_cort1", cort2 = "saliva_cort2", cort3 = "saliva_cort3",     amylase = "saliva_amylase", glucose = "saliva_glucose"),   verbose = FALSE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   times = c(0, 30, 60) )

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
text: data data.frame tibble salivary markers. col_map Named list mapping required inputs. Defaults assume names: cort1   -> "saliva_cort1" (nmol/L wake) cort2   -> "saliva_cort2" (nmol/L ~30 min) cort3   -> "saliva_cort3" (nmol/L ~60 min) amylase -> "saliva_amylase" (U/mL) glucose -> "saliva_glucose" (mg/dL) verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages via hm_inform(). na_action One c("keep","omit","error") required-input NA handling. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness diagnostics (debug). Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs extreme values. Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore") extremes detected. Default "warn". extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) bounds. NULL, broad defaults used (keyed mapped column names). times Numeric vector sampling times (minutes) CAR AUC. Must align cort1/2/3. Default c(0,30,60).

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score

what: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: Uses stable isotope tracer infusion rates DXA-measured fat mass compute peripheral adipose insulin sensitivity related metrics.

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
code: tracer_dxa_is(   data,   col_map,   normalize = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: data data.frame tibble containing raw measurements. col_map Named list entries (depending mode): Adipose-required: - I0: fasting insulin (pmol/L) - rate_glycerol, rate_palmitate: tracer rates (mumol/min) - fat_mass, weight, bmi: body composition - HDL_c: HDL cholesterol (mmol/L) Full mode additionally requires: - G0, G30, G120: glucose (mmol/L) - I30, I120: insulin (pmol/L) - TG: triglycerides (mmol/L) - FFA: free fatty acids (mmol/L) normalize Ignored (kept backward compatibility). na_action One c("keep","omit","error") NA handling required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness warnings required inputs. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs extreme values. Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore") extremes detected. Default "warn". extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) bounds inputs (keys col_map). NULL, broad defaults used. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages completion summary.

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: Adipose-tibble columns: LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv Full-mode tibble columns: I_AUC, FFA_AUC, tracer_palmitate_SI, tracer_glycerol_SI, LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: Modes: Adipose-indices adipose-related keys mapped (OGTT glucose/insulin time series) Full indices otherwise Expected units: Glucose: mmol/L (internally converted mg/dL needed) Insulin: pmol/L (internally converted muU/mL via /6) TG: mmol/L (mg/dL via *88.57); HDL-c: mmol/L (mg/dL via *38.67) Tracer rates: mumol/min Fat mass, weight: kg; BMI: kg/m^2

Liver fatty acid-binding protein biomarker acute kidney injury cardiac surgery. Kidney Int. 2008;73(4):465-472. doi:10.1038/sj.ki.5002688  (L-FABP biomarker validation)

title: Calculate urine-only renal and tubular markers (research-ready) — urine_markers
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   urine_albumin    = 30,   urine_creatinine = 1.2,   serum_creatinine = 0.9,   plasma_Na        = 140,   urine_Na         = 100,   age              = 55,   sex              = 2,   urine_protein    = 150 ) urine_markers(df) #> # A tibble: 1 × 12 #>    UACR albuminuria_stage microalbuminuria  UPCR U_Na_K_ratio NGAL_per_gCr #>   <dbl> <fct>             <fct>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 25000 A3                normal           12500           NA           NA #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl>

what: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
text: Ensures required keys exist col_map non-empty mappings. Missing keys reported stable order aligned tests.

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
code: validate_inputs(data, col_map, fun_name, required_keys = NULL)

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
text: data data.frame tibble col_map named list mapping keys column names fun_name character scalar naming calling function (e.g., "lipid_markers"). Used look built-required keys required_keys supplied. required_keys optional character vector required col_map keys. supplied, takes precedence fun_name built-lookup, making function useful caller regardless fun_name.

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
text: invisibly TRUE success; otherwise aborts

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
code: df <- data.frame(TG = c(1.5, 2.0), HDL_c = c(1.2, 1.0),   LDL_c = c(2.0, 2.5), TC = c(4.5, 5.0)) # Using built-in lookup validate_inputs(df,   list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c", LDL_c = "LDL_c", TC = "TC"),   fun_name = "lipid_markers") # Using explicit required_keys (works for any function) validate_inputs(df,   list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c"),   fun_name = "my_function",   required_keys = c("TG", "HDL_c"))

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: Categorizes vitamin D status based serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D (25(OH)D) levels.

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
code: vitamin_d_status(   data,   col_map = list(vitd = "VitD"),   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore", "NA"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: data data.frame tibble 25-hydroxyvitamin D concentration column. col_map named list vitamin_d giving column name data 25(OH)D. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). keep/ignore: compute propagate NA omit: drop rows NA required input error: abort required input contains NA warn: like keep, emit missingness warnings check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs plausible ranges. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA"). extreme_rules Optional named list overriding defaults; default: list(vitamin_d = c(0, 250)). verbose Logical; TRUE, emits progress via rlang::inform.

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: tibble one column: vitamin_d_status (ordered factor levels "Deficient","Insufficient","Sufficient").

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: Serum 25(OH)D standard biomarker vitamin D status. function classifies levels (assumed ng/mL) categories: Deficient (< 20 ng/mL) Insufficient (20-29 ng/mL) Sufficient (>= 30 ng/mL) Note: Ensure input units ng/mL. values appear extremely high (e.g., median > 150), might nmol/L (divide 2.5 convert ng/mL).

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: Vitamin D IoM(CtRDRI, Calcium (2011). Dietary Reference Intakes Calcium Vitamin D. National Academies Press. doi:10.17226/13050 . ; Holick MF, Binkley NC, Bischoff-Ferrari HA, et al. (2011). “Evaluation, Treatment, Prevention Vitamin D Deficiency: Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline.” Journal Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 96(7), 1911–1930. doi:10.1210/jc.2011-0385 .

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status

what: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: Given serum/plasma vitamins related analytes, vitamin_markers() computes: VitD_Z: z-score 25(OH)D using provided reference mean/sd B12_Fol_Ratio: vitamin B12 / folate Ferr_TSat_R: ferritin / transferrin saturation (TSat) Cort_DHEA_R: cortisol / DHEA-S T_E2_Ratio: testosterone / estradiol TSH_fT4_R: TSH / free T4 Retinol_Z: z-score retinol using provided reference mean/sd Toco_Lip_R: alpha-tocopherol / total lipids Mg_Zn_R: magnesium / zinc Cu_Zn_R: copper / zinc Plus pass-: PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
code: vitamin_markers(   data,   col_map,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   check_extreme = FALSE,   extreme_action = c("warn", "cap", "error", "ignore"),   extreme_rules = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: data data.frame tibble vitamin/analyte columns. col_map Named list mapping required keys column names: VitD, VitD_ref_mean, VitD_ref_sd, B12, Folate, Ferritin, TSat, Cortisol, DHEAS, Testosterone, Estradiol, TSH, free_T4, Retinol, Retinol_ref_mean, Retinol_ref_sd, Tocopherol, Total_lipids, PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA, Magnesium, Zinc, Copper. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness debug notices. Default 0.2. check_extreme Logical; TRUE, scan inputs extreme values. Default FALSE. extreme_action One c("warn","cap","error","ignore") extremes detected. Default "warn". extreme_rules Optional named list c(min,max) bounds keyed input keys column names. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages via hm_inform().

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: tibble columns: VitD_Z, B12_Fol_Ratio, Ferr_TSat_R, Cort_DHEA_R, T_E2_Ratio, TSH_fT4_R, Retinol_Z, Toco_Lip_R, PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA, Mg_Zn_R, Cu_Zn_R

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: HM-CS v2: Validation via hm_validate_inputs(data, col_map, required_keys, fn) User errors via rlang::abort(..., class=...) Verbosity via hm_inform(level) controlled options(healthmarkers.verbose) High-missingness diagnostics debug level

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: Holick MF. Vitamin D deficiency. N Engl J Med. 2007;357:266-281. doi:10.1056/NEJMra070553  O'Leary F, Samman S. Vitamin B12 health disease. Nutrients. 2010;2(3):299-316. doi:10.3390/nu2030299  Ganz T, Nemeth E. Iron homeostasis host defence inflammation. Nat Rev Immunol. 2015;15:500-510. doi:10.1038/nri3863  Huxtable RJ. Physiological actions taurine. Physiol Rev. 1992;72(1):101-163. (endocrine ratios context)

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   VitD = 50, VitD_ref_mean = 40, VitD_ref_sd = 5,   B12 = 300, Folate = 15,   Ferritin = 100, TSat = 0.25,   Cortisol = 200, DHEAS = 100,   Testosterone = 12, Estradiol = 120,   TSH = 2, free_T4 = 14,   Retinol = 0.8, Retinol_ref_mean = 0.9, Retinol_ref_sd = 0.2,   Tocopherol = 30, Total_lipids = 3,   PIVKA_II = 5, VitC = 60, Homocysteine = 10, MMA = 0.3,   Magnesium = 0.8, Zinc = 15, Copper = 15 ) cm <- as.list(names(df)); names(cm) <- names(df) vitamin_markers(df, cm) #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   VitD_Z B12_Fol_Ratio Ferr_TSat_R Cort_DHEA_R T_E2_Ratio TSH_fT4_R Retinol_Z #>    <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1      2            20         400           2        0.1     0.143      -0.5 #> # ℹ 7 more variables: Toco_Lip_R <dbl>, PIVKA_II <dbl>, VitC <dbl>, #> #   Homocysteine <dbl>, MMA <dbl>, Mg_Zn_R <dbl>, Cu_Zn_R <dbl>

title: WHO-5 scoring — who5_score
code: who5_score(   data,   col_map = list(),   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   missing_prop_max = 0.2,   impute = c("none", "mean"),   prefix = "WHO5",   low_cutoff_percent = 50,   verbose = FALSE )

title: WHO-5 scoring — who5_score
text: data Data frame containing questionnaire item columns. col_map Named list mapping canonical item IDs column names; defaults assume items already named. na_action handle rows missing items: keep, omit, error. missing_prop_max Maximum allowed proportion missing items per row score set NA. impute Imputation strategy missing items threshold: none mean (row-wise mean). prefix Prefix output column names. low_cutoff_percent Percentage threshold low well-flag. verbose Logical flag verbose messaging (reserved).

title: WHO-5 scoring — who5_score
text: tibble score columns : WHO5_raw, WHO5_percent, WHO5_low_wellbeing. Input columns included.

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.1
text: Added liver_fat_markers() hepatic steatosis fibrosis index calculation. Added nfl_marker() plasma neurofilament light chain (NfL) z-score computation. Added impute_missing() within-row imputation missing biomarker values.

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.1
text: pulmo_markers(): fixed column-inference logic spirometry z-score inputs. marker functions: standardised verbose progress messages emit "debug" level verbose = FALSE, getOption("healthmarkers.verbose") controls visibility consistently across every function. Computing-phase messages now unconditionally emitted "debug" level (independent per-call verbose argument) bone_markers(), ckd_stage(), corrected_calcium(). all_health_markers(): column inference now keyed requested groups, falls back regex-based infer_cols() exact matches fail, reports per-group status (including missing optional packages) verbose summaries. Cardiovascular risk wrappers: cvd_risk_qrisk3() now prefers correctly named ethnicity column (typo tolerated backward compatibility), optional dependency errors now report missing package name consistently. Shared validation: lipid marker helpers now route hm_validate_inputs() duplicate/empty mappings caught uniformly.

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.1
